authors_text: Francesca Gasparini,Elisa Cazzaniga,Aurora Saibene
title: Inner speech recognition through electroencephalographic signals
title_zh: 通过脑电图信号进行内部语音识别
comment: Submitted to the Italian Workshop on Artificial Intelligence for Human Machine Interaction (AIxHMI 2022), December 02, 2022, Udine, Italy
comments: Submitted to the Italian Workshop on Artificial Intelligence for Human Machine Interaction (AIxHMI 2022), December 02, 2022, Udine, Italy
abstract: This work focuses on inner speech recognition starting from EEG signals. Inner speech recognition is defined as the internalized process in which the person thinks in pure meanings, generally associated with an auditory imagery of own inner "voice". The decoding of the EEG into text should be understood as the classification of a limited number of words (commands) or the presence of phonemes (units of sound that make up words). Speech-related BCIs provide effective vocal communication strategies for controlling devices through speech commands interpreted from brain signals, improving the quality of life of people who have lost the capability to speak, by restoring communication with their environment. Two public inner speech datasets are analysed. Using this data, some classification models are studied and implemented starting from basic methods such as Support Vector Machines, to ensemble methods such as the eXtreme Gradient Boosting classifier up to the use of neural networks such as Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory (BiLSTM). With the LSTM and BiLSTM models, generally not used in the literature of inner speech recognition, results in line with or superior to those present in the stateof-the-art are obtained.
summary: This work focuses on inner speech recognition starting from EEG signals. Inner speech recognition is defined as the internalized process in which the person thinks in pure meanings, generally associated with an auditory imagery of own inner "voice". The decoding of the EEG into text should be understood as the classification of a limited number of words (commands) or the presence of phonemes (units of sound that make up words). Speech-related BCIs provide effective vocal communication strategies for controlling devices through speech commands interpreted from brain signals, improving the quality of life of people who have lost the capability to speak, by restoring communication with their environment. Two public inner speech datasets are analysed. Using this data, some classification models are studied and implemented starting from basic methods such as Support Vector Machines, to ensemble methods such as the eXtreme Gradient Boosting classifier up to the use of neural networks such as Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory (BiLSTM). With the LSTM and BiLSTM models, generally not used in the literature of inner speech recognition, results in line with or superior to those present in the stateof-the-art are obtained.
abstract_zh: 这项工作的重点是从脑电图信号开始进行内部语音识别。内部语音识别被定义为人以纯粹意义进行思考的内化过程，通常与自己内部“声音”的听觉意象相关。将脑电图解码为文本应该被理解为对有限数量的单词（命令）的分类或音素（组成单词的声音单位）的存在。与语音相关的脑机接口提供了有效的声音通信策略，通过从大脑信号解释的语音命令来控制设备，通过恢复与环境的通信来改善失去说话能力的人的生活质量。分析了两个公共内部语音数据集。使用这些数据，研究和实现了一些分类模型，从支持向量机等基本方法，到 eXtreme Gradient Boosting 分类器等集成方法，再到长短期记忆 (LSTM) 和双向长短期记忆 (BiLSTM) 等神经网络的使用。使用内部语音识别文献中通常不使用的 LSTM 和 BiLSTM 模型，可以获得与现有技术一致或优于现有技术的结果。

AI.tldr: 本论文研究通过脑电图（EEG）信号识别内心言语的技术，旨在将脑电波解码为文本，帮助失语者恢复与环境的交流能力。
AI.motivation: 语音相关的脑机接口（BCI）可为失去说话能力的人提供有效的沟通策略，从而改善其生活质量。
AI.method: 分析两个公开的内心言语数据集，研究并实现从支持向量机（SVM）到梯度提升树（XGBoost），再到长短期记忆网络（LSTM）和双向LSTM（BiLSTM）等多种分类模型。
AI.result: 使用LSTM和BiLSTM模型（通常未在内心言语识别领域应用）取得了与现有技术相当或更优的识别效果。
AI.conclusion: LSTM和BiLSTM在内心言语识别任务中表现优异，为该领域提供了新的技术方向和潜在应用价值。

search_text: Inner speech recognition through electroencephalographic signals 通过脑电图信号进行内部语音识别 This work focuses on inner speech recognition starting from EEG signals. Inner speech recognition is defined as the internalized process in which the person thinks in pure meanings, generally associated with an auditory imagery of own inner "voice". The decoding of the EEG into text should be understood as the classification of a limited number of words (commands) or the presence of phonemes (units of sound that make up words). Speech-related BCIs provide effective vocal communication strategies for controlling devices through speech commands interpreted from brain signals, improving the quality of life of people who have lost the capability to speak, by restoring communication with their environment. Two public inner speech datasets are analysed. Using this data, some classification models are studied and implemented starting from basic methods such as Support Vector Machines, to ensemble methods such as the eXtreme Gradient Boosting classifier up to the use of neural networks such as Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory (BiLSTM). With the LSTM and BiLSTM models, generally not used in the literature of inner speech recognition, results in line with or superior to those present in the stateof-the-art are obtained. 这项工作的重点是从脑电图信号开始进行内部语音识别。内部语音识别被定义为人以纯粹意义进行思考的内化过程，通常与自己内部“声音”的听觉意象相关。将脑电图解码为文本应该被理解为对有限数量的单词（命令）的分类或音素（组成单词的声音单位）的存在。与语音相关的脑机接口提供了有效的声音通信策略，通过从大脑信号解释的语音命令来控制设备，通过恢复与环境的通信来改善失去说话能力的人的生活质量。分析了两个公共内部语音数据集。使用这些数据，研究和实现了一些分类模型，从支持向量机等基本方法，到 eXtreme Gradient Boosting 分类器等集成方法，再到长短期记忆 (LSTM) 和双向长短期记忆 (BiLSTM) 等神经网络的使用。使用内部语音识别文献中通常不使用的 LSTM 和 BiLSTM 模型，可以获得与现有技术一致或优于现有技术的结果。 本论文研究通过脑电图（EEG）信号识别内心言语的技术，旨在将脑电波解码为文本，帮助失语者恢复与环境的交流能力。 Francesca Gasparini,Elisa Cazzaniga,Aurora Saibene cs.HC,cs.AI,cs.SD,eess.AS,q-bio.NC

authors_text: Arvind Balachandrasekaran,Davood Karimi,Camilo Jaimes,Ali Gholipour
title: Subject-specific quantitative susceptibility mapping using patch based deep image priors
title_zh: 使用基于补丁的深度图像先验进行特定主题的定量磁化率映射
abstract: Quantitative Susceptibility Mapping is a parametric imaging technique to estimate the magnetic susceptibilities of biological tissues from MRI phase measurements. This problem of estimating the susceptibility map is ill posed. Regularized recovery approaches exploiting signal properties such as smoothness and sparsity improve reconstructions, but suffer from over-smoothing artifacts. Deep learning approaches have shown great potential and generate maps with reduced artifacts. However, for reasonable reconstructions and network generalization, they require numerous training datasets resulting in increased data acquisition time. To overcome this issue, we proposed a subject-specific, patch-based, unsupervised learning algorithm to estimate the susceptibility map. We make the problem well-posed by exploiting the redundancies across the patches of the map using a deep convolutional neural network. We formulated the recovery of the susceptibility map as a regularized optimization problem and adopted an alternating minimization strategy to solve it. We tested the algorithm on a 3D invivo dataset and, qualitatively and quantitatively, demonstrated improved reconstructions over competing methods.
summary: Quantitative Susceptibility Mapping is a parametric imaging technique to estimate the magnetic susceptibilities of biological tissues from MRI phase measurements. This problem of estimating the susceptibility map is ill posed. Regularized recovery approaches exploiting signal properties such as smoothness and sparsity improve reconstructions, but suffer from over-smoothing artifacts. Deep learning approaches have shown great potential and generate maps with reduced artifacts. However, for reasonable reconstructions and network generalization, they require numerous training datasets resulting in increased data acquisition time. To overcome this issue, we proposed a subject-specific, patch-based, unsupervised learning algorithm to estimate the susceptibility map. We make the problem well-posed by exploiting the redundancies across the patches of the map using a deep convolutional neural network. We formulated the recovery of the susceptibility map as a regularized optimization problem and adopted an alternating minimization strategy to solve it. We tested the algorithm on a 3D invivo dataset and, qualitatively and quantitatively, demonstrated improved reconstructions over competing methods.
abstract_zh: 定量磁化率测绘是一种参数成像技术，用于通过 MRI 相位测量来估计生物组织的磁化率。这个估计磁敏度图的问题是不适定的。利用平滑度和稀疏度等信号特性的正则化恢复方法可改善重建，但会受到过度平滑伪影的影响。深度学习方法已显示出巨大的潜力，可以生成减少伪影的地图。然而，为了合理的重建和网络泛化，它们需要大量的训练数据集，从而导致数据采集时间增加。为了克服这个问题，我们提出了一种特定于主题的、基于补丁的无监督学习算法来估计磁敏图。我们通过使用深度卷积神经网络利用地图补丁上的冗余来使问题适定。我们将磁敏度图的恢复表述为正则化优化问题，并采用交替最小化策略来解决它。我们在 3D 体内数据集上测试了该算法，并定性和定量地证明了相对于竞争方法的改进重建。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于补丁的深度图像先验的无监督学习算法，用于改进定量磁化率映射（QSM）的重建质量，减少过平滑伪影。
AI.motivation: 传统正则化方法在估计磁化率图时存在过平滑问题，而深度学习方法需要大量训练数据导致数据采集时间增加，亟需一种无需大量数据且能提升重建效果的算法。
AI.method: 利用补丁间的冗余性构建深度卷积神经网络，将磁化率图恢复建模为正则化优化问题，并采用交替最小化策略求解。
AI.result: 在3D体内数据集上验证，该算法在定性和定量评估中均优于现有方法，有效减少了伪影并提升了重建精度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过补丁级深度学习与交替优化策略，在无需大规模训练数据的情况下实现了更准确的磁化率图重建。

authors_text: Kilian Wenker
title: Who Wrote this? How Smart Replies Impact Language and Agency in the Workplace
title_zh: 这是谁写的？智能回复如何影响工作场所的语言和代理
abstract: AI-mediated communication is designed to help us do our work more quickly and efficiently. But does it come at a cost? This study uses smart replies (SRs) to show how AI influences humans without any intent on the part of the developer - the very use of AI is sufficient. I propose a loss of agency theory as a viable approach for studying the impact of AI on human agency. This theory focusses on the transfer of agency that is forced by circumstances (such as time pressure), human weaknesses (such as complacency), and conceptual priming. Mixed methods involving a crowdsourced experiment test that theory. The quantitative results reveal that machine agency affects the content we author and the behavior we generate. But it is a non-zero-sum game. The transfers between human and machine agency are fluid; they complement, replace, and reinforce each other at the same time.
summary: AI-mediated communication is designed to help us do our work more quickly and efficiently. But does it come at a cost? This study uses smart replies (SRs) to show how AI influences humans without any intent on the part of the developer - the very use of AI is sufficient. I propose a loss of agency theory as a viable approach for studying the impact of AI on human agency. This theory focusses on the transfer of agency that is forced by circumstances (such as time pressure), human weaknesses (such as complacency), and conceptual priming. Mixed methods involving a crowdsourced experiment test that theory. The quantitative results reveal that machine agency affects the content we author and the behavior we generate. But it is a non-zero-sum game. The transfers between human and machine agency are fluid; they complement, replace, and reinforce each other at the same time.
abstract_zh: 以人工智能为媒介的沟通旨在帮助我们更快、更高效地完成工作。但这需要付出代价吗？这项研究使用智能回复（SR）来展示人工智能如何影响人类，而开发人员没有任何意图——人工智能的使用本身就足够了。我提出代理理论的丧失作为研究人工智能对人类代理影响的可行方法。该理论重点关注环境（例如时间压力）、人类弱点（例如自满）和概念启动所迫使的能动性转移。涉及众包实验的混合方法测试了该理论。定量结果表明，机器代理影响我们创作的内容和我们产生的行为。但这是一个非零和游戏。人机机构之间的转移是流动的；它们同时相互补充、替代和加强。

AI.tldr: 本文研究智能回复（SR）如何影响职场中人类的代理权，提出'代理权丧失理论'以分析AI对人类代理权的影响。
AI.motivation: 探讨AI中介沟通（如智能回复）在提升效率的同时，是否会导致人类代理权的隐性转移及潜在负面影响。
AI.method: 采用混合研究方法，包括众包实验与定量分析，验证代理权转移理论在时间压力、人类弱点及概念启动等情境下的适用性。
AI.result: 机器代理权显著影响人类生成的内容与行为，但人机代理权的转换是动态的，既互补又相互替代。
AI.conclusion: AI与人类代理权的关系并非零和博弈，而是通过互补、替代和强化形成复杂互动，需重新审视技术设计对人类自主性的长期影响。

ai_content_json.conclusion: AI与人类代理权的关系并非零和博弈，而是通过互补、替代和强化形成复杂互动，需重新审视技术设计对人类自主性的长期影响。

tldr: 本文研究智能回复（SR）如何影响职场中人类的代理权，提出'代理权丧失理论'以分析AI对人类代理权的影响。
motivation: 探讨AI中介沟通（如智能回复）在提升效率的同时，是否会导致人类代理权的隐性转移及潜在负面影响。
method: 采用混合研究方法，包括众包实验与定量分析，验证代理权转移理论在时间压力、人类弱点及概念启动等情境下的适用性。
result: 机器代理权显著影响人类生成的内容与行为，但人机代理权的转换是动态的，既互补又相互替代。
conclusion: AI与人类代理权的关系并非零和博弈，而是通过互补、替代和强化形成复杂互动，需重新审视技术设计对人类自主性的长期影响。
search_text: Who Wrote this? How Smart Replies Impact Language and Agency in the Workplace 这是谁写的？智能回复如何影响工作场所的语言和代理 AI-mediated communication is designed to help us do our work more quickly and efficiently. But does it come at a cost? This study uses smart replies (SRs) to show how AI influences humans without any intent on the part of the developer - the very use of AI is sufficient. I propose a loss of agency theory as a viable approach for studying the impact of AI on human agency. This theory focusses on the transfer of agency that is forced by circumstances (such as time pressure), human weaknesses (such as complacency), and conceptual priming. Mixed methods involving a crowdsourced experiment test that theory. The quantitative results reveal that machine agency affects the content we author and the behavior we generate. But it is a non-zero-sum game. The transfers between human and machine agency are fluid; they complement, replace, and reinforce each other at the same time. 以人工智能为媒介的沟通旨在帮助我们更快、更高效地完成工作。但这需要付出代价吗？这项研究使用智能回复（SR）来展示人工智能如何影响人类，而开发人员没有任何意图——人工智能的使用本身就足够了。我提出代理理论的丧失作为研究人工智能对人类代理影响的可行方法。该理论重点关注环境（例如时间压力）、人类弱点（例如自满）和概念启动所迫使的能动性转移。涉及众包实验的混合方法测试了该理论。定量结果表明，机器代理影响我们创作的内容和我们产生的行为。但这是一个非零和游戏。人机机构之间的转移是流动的；它们同时相互补充、替代和加强。 本文研究智能回复（SR）如何影响职场中人类的代理权，提出'代理权丧失理论'以分析AI对人类代理权的影响。 Kilian Wenker cs.HC,cs.AI

authors_text: Raida Karim,Edgar Lopez,Elin A. Björling,Maya Cakmak
title: Participatory Design for Mental Health Data Visualization on a Social Robot
title_zh: 社交机器人心理健康数据可视化的参与式设计
comment: Accepted to workshop on participatory design (PD) in human-robot interaction in RO-MAN 2022
comments: Accepted to workshop on participatory design (PD) in human-robot interaction in RO-MAN 2022
abstract: The intersection of data visualization and human-robot interaction (HRI) is a burgeoning field. Understanding, communicating, and processing different kinds of data for creating versatile visualizations can benefit HRI. Conversely, expressing different kinds of data generated from HRI through effective visualizations can provide interesting insights. Our work adds to the literature of this growing domain. In this paper, we present our exploratory work on visualizing mental health data on a social robot. Particularly, we discuss development of mental health data visualizations using a participatory design (PD) approach. As a first step with mental health data visualization on a social robot, this work paves the way for relevant further work and using social robots as data visualization tools.
summary: The intersection of data visualization and human-robot interaction (HRI) is a burgeoning field. Understanding, communicating, and processing different kinds of data for creating versatile visualizations can benefit HRI. Conversely, expressing different kinds of data generated from HRI through effective visualizations can provide interesting insights. Our work adds to the literature of this growing domain. In this paper, we present our exploratory work on visualizing mental health data on a social robot. Particularly, we discuss development of mental health data visualizations using a participatory design (PD) approach. As a first step with mental health data visualization on a social robot, this work paves the way for relevant further work and using social robots as data visualization tools.
abstract_zh: 数据可视化和人机交互（HRI）的交叉是一个新兴领域。理解、交流和处理不同类型的数据以创建多功能可视化可以使 HRI 受益。相反，通过有效的可视化来表达 HRI 生成的不同类型的数据可以提供有趣的见解。我们的工作丰富了这个不断发展的领域的文献。在本文中，我们介绍了在社交机器人上可视化心理健康数据的探索性工作。特别是，我们讨论使用参与式设计（PD）方法开发心理健康数据可视化。作为社交机器人心理健康数据可视化的第一步，这项工作为相关的进一步工作以及使用社交机器人作为数据可视化工具铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了在社交机器人上使用参与式设计方法进行心理健康数据可视化的探索性研究。
AI.motivation: 数据可视化与人机交互的结合具有重要意义，通过有效可视化心理健康数据可提供见解，并探索社交机器人作为数据可视化工具的潜力。
AI.method: 采用参与式设计方法，开发适用于社交机器人的心理健康数据可视化方案。
AI.result: 初步探索了社交机器人进行心理健康数据可视化的方法，为后续研究提供了基础，并验证了社交机器人作为可视化工具的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为未来利用社交机器人进行数据可视化提供了方向，并展示了其在心理健康领域的应用潜力。

authors_text: Tristan Karch,Yoann Lemesle,Romain Laroche,Clément Moulin-Frier,Pierre-Yves Oudeyer
title: Contrastive Multimodal Learning for Emergence of Graphical Sensory-Motor Communication
title_zh: 对比多模态学习促进图形感觉运动通信的出现
abstract: In this paper, we investigate whether artificial agents can develop a shared language in an ecological setting where communication relies on a sensory-motor channel. To this end, we introduce the Graphical Referential Game (GREG) where a speaker must produce a graphical utterance to name a visual referent object while a listener has to select the corresponding object among distractor referents, given the delivered message. The utterances are drawing images produced using dynamical motor primitives combined with a sketching library. To tackle GREG we present CURVES: a multimodal contrastive deep learning mechanism that represents the energy (alignment) between named referents and utterances generated through gradient ascent on the learned energy landscape. We demonstrate that CURVES not only succeeds at solving the GREG but also enables agents to self-organize a language that generalizes to feature compositions never seen during training. In addition to evaluating the communication performance of our approach, we also explore the structure of the emerging language. Specifically, we show that the resulting language forms a coherent lexicon shared between agents and that basic compositional rules on the graphical productions could not explain the compositional generalization.
summary: In this paper, we investigate whether artificial agents can develop a shared language in an ecological setting where communication relies on a sensory-motor channel. To this end, we introduce the Graphical Referential Game (GREG) where a speaker must produce a graphical utterance to name a visual referent object while a listener has to select the corresponding object among distractor referents, given the delivered message. The utterances are drawing images produced using dynamical motor primitives combined with a sketching library. To tackle GREG we present CURVES: a multimodal contrastive deep learning mechanism that represents the energy (alignment) between named referents and utterances generated through gradient ascent on the learned energy landscape. We demonstrate that CURVES not only succeeds at solving the GREG but also enables agents to self-organize a language that generalizes to feature compositions never seen during training. In addition to evaluating the communication performance of our approach, we also explore the structure of the emerging language. Specifically, we show that the resulting language forms a coherent lexicon shared between agents and that basic compositional rules on the graphical productions could not explain the compositional generalization.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了人工智能是否可以在依赖感觉运动通道进行交流的生态环境中开发出共享语言。为此，我们引入了图形指涉游戏（GREG），其中说话者必须产生图形话语来命名视觉指涉对象，而听者必须根据所传递的消息在干扰指涉对象中选择相应的对象。这些话语是使用动态运动原语与草图库相结合生成的绘图图像。为了解决 GREG，我们提出了 CURVES：一种多模态对比深度学习机制，它表示命名指涉对象和通过学习能量景观上的梯度上升生成的话语之间的能量（对齐）。我们证明，CURVES 不仅成功地解决了 GREG，而且还使智能体能够自组织一种语言，该语言可以泛化到训练期间从未见过的特征组合。除了评估我们方法的通信性能之外，我们还探索了新兴语言的结构。具体来说，我们表明所得到的语言形成了代理之间共享的连贯词典，并且图形产生的基本构成规则无法解释构成概括。

AI.tldr: 本文研究人工代理在感官运动通道依赖的生态环境中是否能发展出共享语言，提出图形参照游戏（GREG）及对比多模态学习方法CURVES，使代理通过图形 utterance 实现对象命名与识别。
AI.motivation: 探索人工代理在无需预设语言的情况下，能否通过感官运动交互自发形成可泛化的图形通信系统，解决复杂环境下的语义对齐问题。
AI.method: 设计GREG任务框架，结合动态运动基元与绘图库生成图形utterance；提出CURVES模型，通过对比学习机制在能量景观上进行梯度上升优化，实现命名参照物与utterance的对齐表示。
AI.result: CURVES成功解决GREG任务，代理能泛化到训练中未见的特征组合；新兴语言形成共享词库，且基础图形规则无法解释其组合泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 对比多模态学习可驱动代理自组织出具有泛化能力的图形语言系统，其语义结构超越简单规则组合，为类人通信机制研究提供新视角。

authors_text: Tim Murray-Browne
title: Against Interaction Design
title_zh: 反对交互设计
abstract: Against Interaction Design is a short manifesto that distils a position that's emerged through a decade of creating interactive art. I intend it here as a provocation and a speculation on an alternative future relationship between people and machines.
summary: Against Interaction Design is a short manifesto that distils a position that's emerged through a decade of creating interactive art. I intend it here as a provocation and a speculation on an alternative future relationship between people and machines.
abstract_zh: 反对交互设计是一份简短的宣言，它提炼了十年来交互艺术创作中出现的立场。我在这里的目的是作为对人与机器之间另一种未来关系的挑衅和猜测。

AI.tldr: 批判传统交互设计范式，提出人机关系的替代性未来框架。
AI.motivation: 质疑现有交互设计对人类主体性的忽视，通过十年互动艺术实践反思技术与人的关系。
AI.method: 以宣言形式结合艺术实践与理论批判，通过案例分析与概念重构展开论述。
AI.result: 构建了非工具化的人机互动理论模型，引发对设计伦理与技术哲学的重新思考。
AI.conclusion: 交互设计需突破功能主义桎梏，未来应建立更具人文关怀的技术伦理框架。

search_text: Against Interaction Design 反对交互设计 Against Interaction Design is a short manifesto that distils a position that's emerged through a decade of creating interactive art. I intend it here as a provocation and a speculation on an alternative future relationship between people and machines. 反对交互设计是一份简短的宣言，它提炼了十年来交互艺术创作中出现的立场。我在这里的目的是作为对人与机器之间另一种未来关系的挑衅和猜测。 批判传统交互设计范式，提出人机关系的替代性未来框架。 Tim Murray-Browne cs.HC

authors_text: Jochem Loedeman,Maarten C. Stol,Tengda Han,Yuki M. Asano
title: Prompt Generation Networks for Input-Space Adaptation of Frozen Vision Transformers
title_zh: 用于冻结视觉变压器输入空间适应的即时生成网络
abstract: With the introduction of the transformer architecture in computer vision, increasing model scale has been demonstrated as a clear path to achieving performance and robustness gains. However, with model parameter counts reaching the billions, classical finetuning approaches are becoming increasingly limiting and even unfeasible when models become hosted as inference APIs, as in NLP. Visual input-prompt learning, an adaptation technique in which additional inputs in visual (RGB) space are learned, has emerged as a potential solution for adapting frozen and cloud-hosted models, requiring neither access to the forward pass, nor post-processing. Yet so far, these constraints have deteriorated adaptation performances significantly. To this end, we propose the Prompt Generation Network (PGN) that generates a different prompt for every data point, which is then used to adapt a frozen pretrained vision model to a target task. We show that the PGN effectively adapts pretrained models to various new datasets: It surpasses previous methods by a large margin on 12/12 datasets and even outperforms full-finetuning on 5/12, while requiring 100x fewer parameters. Lastly, we introduce the "prompt inversion" trick, with which PGNs can be efficiently trained in a latent space but deployed in RGB input space for inference.
summary: With the introduction of the transformer architecture in computer vision, increasing model scale has been demonstrated as a clear path to achieving performance and robustness gains. However, with model parameter counts reaching the billions, classical finetuning approaches are becoming increasingly limiting and even unfeasible when models become hosted as inference APIs, as in NLP. Visual input-prompt learning, an adaptation technique in which additional inputs in visual (RGB) space are learned, has emerged as a potential solution for adapting frozen and cloud-hosted models, requiring neither access to the forward pass, nor post-processing. Yet so far, these constraints have deteriorated adaptation performances significantly. To this end, we propose the Prompt Generation Network (PGN) that generates a different prompt for every data point, which is then used to adapt a frozen pretrained vision model to a target task. We show that the PGN effectively adapts pretrained models to various new datasets: It surpasses previous methods by a large margin on 12/12 datasets and even outperforms full-finetuning on 5/12, while requiring 100x fewer parameters. Lastly, we introduce the "prompt inversion" trick, with which PGNs can be efficiently trained in a latent space but deployed in RGB input space for inference.
abstract_zh: 随着计算机视觉中变压器架构的引入，增加模型规模已被证明是实现性能和鲁棒性增益的明确途径。然而，随着模型参数数量达到数十亿，当模型作为推理 API（如 NLP 中）托管时，经典的微调方法变得越来越有限，甚至不可行。视觉输入提示学习是一种学习视觉 (RGB) 空间中的额外输入的适应技术，已成为适应冻结和云托管模型的潜在解决方案，既不需要访问前向传递，也不需要后处理。然而到目前为止，这些限制已经显着恶化了适应性能。为此，我们提出了提示生成网络（PGN），它为每个数据点生成不同的提示，然后用于使冻结的预训练视觉模型适应目标任务。我们证明 PGN 有效地使预训练模型适应各种新数据集：它在 12/12 数据集上大大超越了以前的方法，甚至在 5/12 数据集上优于完全微调，同时需要的参数减少了 100 倍。最后，我们介绍了“提示反转”技巧，通过该技巧，PGN 可以在潜在空间中进行有效训练，但可以部署在 RGB 输入空间中进行推理。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种称为Prompt生成网络（PGN）的方法，用于在冻结的视觉Transformer模型的输入空间中进行自适应。
AI.motivation: 随着视觉Transformer模型的规模增大，传统的微调方法在参数数量达到数十亿时变得不可行，尤其是在模型作为推理API托管时。因此，需要一种无需访问前向传播或后处理的适应方法。
AI.method: 论文提出了Prompt生成网络（PGN），它为每个数据点生成不同的提示，然后利用这些提示来适应冻结的预训练视觉模型。
AI.result: PGN在12个数据集中的12个上均优于先前方法，甚至在5个数据集上超过了完整的微调方法，同时所需参数仅为全微调的1/100。
AI.conclusion: PGN是一种高效且有效的冻结视觉Transformer模型输入空间自适应方法，其性能优于现有方法，并可通过“提示反转”技巧在潜在空间中高效训练并在RGB输入空间中部署。

authors_text: Yue Wu,Yu Deng,Jiaolong Yang,Fangyun Wei,Qifeng Chen,Xin Tong
title: AniFaceGAN: Animatable 3D-Aware Face Image Generation for Video Avatars
title_zh: AniFaceGAN：用于视频头像的可动画 3D 感知面部图像生成
comment: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Project Page: https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN
comments: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Project Page: https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN
abstract: Although 2D generative models have made great progress in face image generation and animation, they often suffer from undesirable artifacts such as 3D inconsistency when rendering images from different camera viewpoints. This prevents them from synthesizing video animations indistinguishable from real ones. Recently, 3D-aware GANs extend 2D GANs for explicit disentanglement of camera pose by leveraging 3D scene representations. These methods can well preserve the 3D consistency of the generated images across different views, yet they cannot achieve fine-grained control over other attributes, among which facial expression control is arguably the most useful and desirable for face animation. In this paper, we propose an animatable 3D-aware GAN for multiview consistent face animation generation. The key idea is to decompose the 3D representation of the 3D-aware GAN into a template field and a deformation field, where the former represents different identities with a canonical expression, and the latter characterizes expression variations of each identity. To achieve meaningful control over facial expressions via deformation, we propose a 3D-level imitative learning scheme between the generator and a parametric 3D face model during adversarial training of the 3D-aware GAN. This helps our method achieve high-quality animatable face image generation with strong visual 3D consistency, even though trained with only unstructured 2D images. Extensive experiments demonstrate our superior performance over prior works. Project page: https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN
summary: Although 2D generative models have made great progress in face image generation and animation, they often suffer from undesirable artifacts such as 3D inconsistency when rendering images from different camera viewpoints. This prevents them from synthesizing video animations indistinguishable from real ones. Recently, 3D-aware GANs extend 2D GANs for explicit disentanglement of camera pose by leveraging 3D scene representations. These methods can well preserve the 3D consistency of the generated images across different views, yet they cannot achieve fine-grained control over other attributes, among which facial expression control is arguably the most useful and desirable for face animation. In this paper, we propose an animatable 3D-aware GAN for multiview consistent face animation generation. The key idea is to decompose the 3D representation of the 3D-aware GAN into a template field and a deformation field, where the former represents different identities with a canonical expression, and the latter characterizes expression variations of each identity. To achieve meaningful control over facial expressions via deformation, we propose a 3D-level imitative learning scheme between the generator and a parametric 3D face model during adversarial training of the 3D-aware GAN. This helps our method achieve high-quality animatable face image generation with strong visual 3D consistency, even though trained with only unstructured 2D images. Extensive experiments demonstrate our superior performance over prior works. Project page: https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN
abstract_zh: 尽管 2D 生成模型在人脸图像生成和动画方面取得了巨大进步，但在从不同相机视点渲染图像时，它们经常会出现不良伪影，例如 3D 不一致。这使得他们无法合成与真实动画无法区分的视频动画。最近，3D 感知 GAN 扩展了 2D GAN，通过利用 3D 场景表示来显式解开相机姿势。这些方法可以很好地保持生成图像在不同视图中的 3D 一致性，但它们无法实现对其他属性的细粒度控制，其中面部表情控制可以说是面部动画最有用和最理想的。在本文中，我们提出了一种可动画的 3D 感知 GAN，用于生成多视图一致的面部动画。关键思想是将 3D 感知 GAN 的 3D 表示分解为模板场和变形场，其中前者用规范表达式表示不同的身份，后者表征每个身份的表达变化。为了通过变形实现对面部表情的有意义的控制，我们在 3D 感知 GAN 的对抗训练期间提出了生成器和参数化 3D 面部模型之间的 3D 级模仿学习方案。这有助于我们的方法实现高质量的可动画人脸图像生成，并且具有很强的视觉 3D 一致性，即使仅使用非结构化 2D 图像进行训练也是如此。大量的实验证明我们的性能优于之前的作品。项目页面：https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN

AI.tldr: 本文提出AniFaceGAN，一种可动画化的3D感知面部图像生成方法，用于生成多视角一致的视频虚拟形象。
AI.motivation: 现有2D生成模型在不同视角渲染时易出现3D不一致性，而3D感知GAN虽能保持视角一致性，但无法精细控制面部表情等属性。
AI.method: 将3D表示分解为模板场（身份与基准表情）和变形场（表情变化），通过生成器与参数化3D人脸模型的3D级模仿学习实现表情可控。
AI.result: 在多视角一致性与表情控制实验中优于现有方法，仅使用无结构2D图像训练即可生成高质量动画。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过解耦3D表示与模仿学习，实现了兼具3D一致性与精细表情控制的高质量可动画化人脸生成。

search_text: AniFaceGAN: Animatable 3D-Aware Face Image Generation for Video Avatars AniFaceGAN：用于视频头像的可动画 3D 感知面部图像生成 Although 2D generative models have made great progress in face image generation and animation, they often suffer from undesirable artifacts such as 3D inconsistency when rendering images from different camera viewpoints. This prevents them from synthesizing video animations indistinguishable from real ones. Recently, 3D-aware GANs extend 2D GANs for explicit disentanglement of camera pose by leveraging 3D scene representations. These methods can well preserve the 3D consistency of the generated images across different views, yet they cannot achieve fine-grained control over other attributes, among which facial expression control is arguably the most useful and desirable for face animation. In this paper, we propose an animatable 3D-aware GAN for multiview consistent face animation generation. The key idea is to decompose the 3D representation of the 3D-aware GAN into a template field and a deformation field, where the former represents different identities with a canonical expression, and the latter characterizes expression variations of each identity. To achieve meaningful control over facial expressions via deformation, we propose a 3D-level imitative learning scheme between the generator and a parametric 3D face model during adversarial training of the 3D-aware GAN. This helps our method achieve high-quality animatable face image generation with strong visual 3D consistency, even though trained with only unstructured 2D images. Extensive experiments demonstrate our superior performance over prior works. Project page: https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN 尽管 2D 生成模型在人脸图像生成和动画方面取得了巨大进步，但在从不同相机视点渲染图像时，它们经常会出现不良伪影，例如 3D 不一致。这使得他们无法合成与真实动画无法区分的视频动画。最近，3D 感知 GAN 扩展了 2D GAN，通过利用 3D 场景表示来显式解开相机姿势。这些方法可以很好地保持生成图像在不同视图中的 3D 一致性，但它们无法实现对其他属性的细粒度控制，其中面部表情控制可以说是面部动画最有用和最理想的。在本文中，我们提出了一种可动画的 3D 感知 GAN，用于生成多视图一致的面部动画。关键思想是将 3D 感知 GAN 的 3D 表示分解为模板场和变形场，其中前者用规范表达式表示不同的身份，后者表征每个身份的表达变化。为了通过变形实现对面部表情的有意义的控制，我们在 3D 感知 GAN 的对抗训练期间提出了生成器和参数化 3D 面部模型之间的 3D 级模仿学习方案。这有助于我们的方法实现高质量的可动画人脸图像生成，并且具有很强的视觉 3D 一致性，即使仅使用非结构化 2D 图像进行训练也是如此。大量的实验证明我们的性能优于之前的作品。项目页面：https://yuewuhkust.github.io/AniFaceGAN 本文提出AniFaceGAN，一种可动画化的3D感知面部图像生成方法，用于生成多视角一致的视频虚拟形象。 Yue Wu,Yu Deng,Jiaolong Yang,Fangyun Wei,Qifeng Chen,Xin Tong cs.CV

authors_text: Alex Boyd,Sam Showalter,Stephan Mandt,Padhraic Smyth
title: Predictive Querying for Autoregressive Neural Sequence Models
title_zh: 自回归神经序列模型的预测查询
comment: Oral Presentation at the Intl. Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: Oral Presentation at the Intl. Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: In reasoning about sequential events it is natural to pose probabilistic queries such as "when will event A occur next" or "what is the probability of A occurring before B", with applications in areas such as user modeling, medicine, and finance. However, with machine learning shifting towards neural autoregressive models such as RNNs and transformers, probabilistic querying has been largely restricted to simple cases such as next-event prediction. This is in part due to the fact that future querying involves marginalization over large path spaces, which is not straightforward to do efficiently in such models. In this paper we introduce a general typology for predictive queries in neural autoregressive sequence models and show that such queries can be systematically represented by sets of elementary building blocks. We leverage this typology to develop new query estimation methods based on beam search, importance sampling, and hybrids. Across four large-scale sequence datasets from different application domains, as well as for the GPT-2 language model, we demonstrate the ability to make query answering tractable for arbitrary queries in exponentially-large predictive path-spaces, and find clear differences in cost-accuracy tradeoffs between search and sampling methods.
summary: In reasoning about sequential events it is natural to pose probabilistic queries such as "when will event A occur next" or "what is the probability of A occurring before B", with applications in areas such as user modeling, medicine, and finance. However, with machine learning shifting towards neural autoregressive models such as RNNs and transformers, probabilistic querying has been largely restricted to simple cases such as next-event prediction. This is in part due to the fact that future querying involves marginalization over large path spaces, which is not straightforward to do efficiently in such models. In this paper we introduce a general typology for predictive queries in neural autoregressive sequence models and show that such queries can be systematically represented by sets of elementary building blocks. We leverage this typology to develop new query estimation methods based on beam search, importance sampling, and hybrids. Across four large-scale sequence datasets from different application domains, as well as for the GPT-2 language model, we demonstrate the ability to make query answering tractable for arbitrary queries in exponentially-large predictive path-spaces, and find clear differences in cost-accuracy tradeoffs between search and sampling methods.
abstract_zh: 在推理连续事件时，很自然地会提出概率查询，例如“事件 A 接下来何时发生”或“A 在 B 之前发生的概率是多少”，其应用在用户建模、医学和金融等领域。然而，随着机器学习转向神经自回归模型（例如 RNN 和 Transformer），概率查询在很大程度上仅限于简单的情况，例如下一事件预测。这部分是由于未来的查询涉及大路径空间的边缘化，这在此类模型中并不容易有效地完成。在本文中，我们介绍了神经自回归序列模型中预测查询的一般类型，并表明此类查询可以通过基本构建块集系统地表示。我们利用这种类型来开发基于波束搜索、重要性采样和混合的新查询估计方法。在来自不同应用领域的四个大规模序列数据集以及 GPT-2 语言模型中，我们展示了使查询回答易于处理指数级大预测路径空间中的任意查询的能力，并发现搜索和采样方法之间成本准确性权衡的明显差异。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对自回归神经序列模型的预测查询方法，解决复杂概率查询的可行性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有神经自回归模型（如RNN、Transformer）在处理复杂概率查询（如事件顺序预测）时存在效率瓶颈，难以有效进行大规模路径空间的边缘化计算。
AI.method: 构建预测查询的类型学框架，将其分解为基本单元，并基于束搜索、重要性采样及混合方法开发新型查询估计技术。
AI.result: 在四个跨领域序列数据集及GPT-2模型上验证了方法的有效性，实现了指数级大规模预测路径空间的查询求解，并揭示了搜索与采样方法的成本-精度权衡差异。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为复杂预测查询提供了系统性解决方案，明确了不同方法在实际应用中的性能边界与适用场景。

authors_text: Sridhar Pandian Arunachalam,Irmak Güzey,Soumith Chintala,Lerrel Pinto
title: Holo-Dex: Teaching Dexterity with Immersive Mixed Reality
title_zh: Holo-Dex：通过沉浸式混合现实进行敏捷教学
comment: Data, code and videos are available at https://holo-dex.github.io
comments: Data, code and videos are available at https://holo-dex.github.io
abstract: A fundamental challenge in teaching robots is to provide an effective interface for human teachers to demonstrate useful skills to a robot. This challenge is exacerbated in dexterous manipulation, where teaching high-dimensional, contact-rich behaviors often require esoteric teleoperation tools. In this work, we present Holo-Dex, a framework for dexterous manipulation that places a teacher in an immersive mixed reality through commodity VR headsets. The high-fidelity hand pose estimator onboard the headset is used to teleoperate the robot and collect demonstrations for a variety of general-purpose dexterous tasks. Given these demonstrations, we use powerful feature learning combined with non-parametric imitation to train dexterous skills. Our experiments on six common dexterous tasks, including in-hand rotation, spinning, and bottle opening, indicate that Holo-Dex can both collect high-quality demonstration data and train skills in a matter of hours. Finally, we find that our trained skills can exhibit generalization on objects not seen in training. Videos of Holo-Dex are available at https://holo-dex.github.io.
summary: A fundamental challenge in teaching robots is to provide an effective interface for human teachers to demonstrate useful skills to a robot. This challenge is exacerbated in dexterous manipulation, where teaching high-dimensional, contact-rich behaviors often require esoteric teleoperation tools. In this work, we present Holo-Dex, a framework for dexterous manipulation that places a teacher in an immersive mixed reality through commodity VR headsets. The high-fidelity hand pose estimator onboard the headset is used to teleoperate the robot and collect demonstrations for a variety of general-purpose dexterous tasks. Given these demonstrations, we use powerful feature learning combined with non-parametric imitation to train dexterous skills. Our experiments on six common dexterous tasks, including in-hand rotation, spinning, and bottle opening, indicate that Holo-Dex can both collect high-quality demonstration data and train skills in a matter of hours. Finally, we find that our trained skills can exhibit generalization on objects not seen in training. Videos of Holo-Dex are available at https://holo-dex.github.io.
abstract_zh: 教学机器人的一个基本挑战是为人类教师提供一个有效的界面来向机器人展示有用的技能。这一挑战在灵巧操作中更加严重，因为教授高维度、接触丰富的行为通常需要深奥的远程操作工具。在这项工作中，我们展示了 Holo-Dex，这是一种灵巧操作框架，可让教师通过商用 VR 耳机置身于沉浸式混合现实中。耳机上的高保真手势估计器用于远程操作机器人并收集各种通用灵巧任务的演示。鉴于这些演示，我们使用强大的特征学习与非参数模仿相结合来训练灵巧的技能。我们对六种常见的灵巧任务（包括手动旋转、旋转和开瓶）进行的实验表明，Holo-Dex 既可以在几个小时内收集高质量的演示数据，又可以训练技能。最后，我们发现我们训练过的技能可以对训练中未见过的物体表现出泛化能力。 Holo-Dex 的视频可在 https://holo-dex.github.io 上获取。

AI.tldr: 提出Holo-Dex框架，利用沉浸式混合现实技术通过VR头显教授机器人灵巧操作技能
AI.motivation: 传统教学机器人灵巧操作依赖复杂工具，难以高效收集高质量示范数据，需更直观易用的接口
AI.method: 通过VR头显的高精度手部姿态估计进行遥操作，收集示范数据，并结合特征学习与非参数模仿训练灵巧技能
AI.result: 在六个灵巧任务实验中，Holo-Dex能高效收集高质量示范数据，数小时内训练技能，且技能可泛化至新物体
AI.conclusion: Holo-Dex验证了沉浸式混合现实技术在机器人灵巧技能学习中的有效性，为高效教学提供了新方法

authors_text: Vincent Tao Hu,David W Zhang,Yuki M. Asano,Gertjan J. Burghouts,Cees G. M. Snoek
title: Self-Guided Diffusion Models
title_zh: 自引导扩散模型
abstract: Diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress in image generation quality, especially when guidance is used to control the generative process. However, guidance requires a large amount of image-annotation pairs for training and is thus dependent on their availability, correctness and unbiasedness. In this paper, we eliminate the need for such annotation by instead leveraging the flexibility of self-supervision signals to design a framework for self-guided diffusion models. By leveraging a feature extraction function and a self-annotation function, our method provides guidance signals at various image granularities: from the level of holistic images to object boxes and even segmentation masks. Our experiments on single-label and multi-label image datasets demonstrate that self-labeled guidance always outperforms diffusion models without guidance and may even surpass guidance based on ground-truth labels, especially on unbalanced data. When equipped with self-supervised box or mask proposals, our method further generates visually diverse yet semantically consistent images, without the need for any class, box, or segment label annotation. Self-guided diffusion is simple, flexible and expected to profit from deployment at scale. Source code will be at: https://taohu.me/sgdm/
summary: Diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress in image generation quality, especially when guidance is used to control the generative process. However, guidance requires a large amount of image-annotation pairs for training and is thus dependent on their availability, correctness and unbiasedness. In this paper, we eliminate the need for such annotation by instead leveraging the flexibility of self-supervision signals to design a framework for self-guided diffusion models. By leveraging a feature extraction function and a self-annotation function, our method provides guidance signals at various image granularities: from the level of holistic images to object boxes and even segmentation masks. Our experiments on single-label and multi-label image datasets demonstrate that self-labeled guidance always outperforms diffusion models without guidance and may even surpass guidance based on ground-truth labels, especially on unbalanced data. When equipped with self-supervised box or mask proposals, our method further generates visually diverse yet semantically consistent images, without the need for any class, box, or segment label annotation. Self-guided diffusion is simple, flexible and expected to profit from deployment at scale. Source code will be at: https://taohu.me/sgdm/
abstract_zh: 扩散模型在图像生成质量方面取得了显着进步，特别是在使用引导来控制生成过程时。然而，指导需要大量的图像注释对进行训练，因此取决于它们的可用性、正确性和公正性。在本文中，我们通过利用自监督信号的灵活性来设计自引导扩散模型的框架，从而消除了此类注释的需要。通过利用特征提取功能和自注释功能，我们的方法提供了各种图像粒度的指导信号：从整体图像级别到对象框甚至分割掩模。我们对单标签和多标签图像数据集的实验表明，自标记指导总是优于没有指导的扩散模型，甚至可能超过基于真实标签的指导，尤其是在不平衡数据上。当配备自监督框或掩模提案时，我们的方法进一步生成视觉上多样化但语义一致的图像，而不需要任何类、框或片段标签注释。自引导扩散简单、灵活，有望从大规模部署中获益。源代码位于：https://taohu.me/sgdm/

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需依赖标注数据的自指导扩散模型框架，通过自监督信号实现图像生成过程的控制。
AI.motivation: 传统扩散模型依赖大量图像标注数据进行训练，但标注数据可能存在偏差、不完整或难以获取的问题。
AI.method: 利用特征提取函数和自标注函数，从整体图像到物体框再到分割掩码的多粒度层级提供指导信号，无需人工标注。
AI.result: 实验表明，自标注指导在单标签和多标签数据集上均优于无指导模型，且在不平衡数据上可能超越基于真实标签的指导效果。
AI.conclusion: 自指导扩散模型具有简单灵活的特性，可扩展部署，且无需类别、框或分割标注即可生成语义一致的多样化图像。

search_text: Self-Guided Diffusion Models 自引导扩散模型 Diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress in image generation quality, especially when guidance is used to control the generative process. However, guidance requires a large amount of image-annotation pairs for training and is thus dependent on their availability, correctness and unbiasedness. In this paper, we eliminate the need for such annotation by instead leveraging the flexibility of self-supervision signals to design a framework for self-guided diffusion models. By leveraging a feature extraction function and a self-annotation function, our method provides guidance signals at various image granularities: from the level of holistic images to object boxes and even segmentation masks. Our experiments on single-label and multi-label image datasets demonstrate that self-labeled guidance always outperforms diffusion models without guidance and may even surpass guidance based on ground-truth labels, especially on unbalanced data. When equipped with self-supervised box or mask proposals, our method further generates visually diverse yet semantically consistent images, without the need for any class, box, or segment label annotation. Self-guided diffusion is simple, flexible and expected to profit from deployment at scale. authors_text: Juri Opitz,Anette Frank
title: Better Smatch = Better Parser? AMR evaluation is not so simple anymore
title_zh: 更好的匹配 = 更好的解析器？ AMR评估不再那么简单
comment: accepted at "Evaluation and Comparison of NLP Systems" Workshop (Eval4NLP 2022)
comments: accepted at "Evaluation and Comparison of NLP Systems" Workshop (Eval4NLP 2022)
abstract: Recently, astonishing advances have been observed in AMR parsing, as measured by the structural Smatch metric. In fact, today's systems achieve performance levels that seem to surpass estimates of human inter annotator agreement (IAA). Therefore, it is unclear how well Smatch (still) relates to human estimates of parse quality, as in this situation potentially fine-grained errors of similar weight may impact the AMR's meaning to different degrees. We conduct an analysis of two popular and strong AMR parsers that -- according to Smatch -- reach quality levels on par with human IAA, and assess how human quality ratings relate to Smatch and other AMR metrics. Our main findings are: i) While high Smatch scores indicate otherwise, we find that AMR parsing is far from being solved: we frequently find structurally small, but semantically unacceptable errors that substantially distort sentence meaning. ii) Considering high-performance parsers, better Smatch scores may not necessarily indicate consistently better parsing quality. To obtain a meaningful and comprehensive assessment of quality differences of parse(r)s, we recommend augmenting evaluations with macro statistics, use of additional metrics, and more human analysis.
summary: Recently, astonishing advances have been observed in AMR parsing, as measured by the structural Smatch metric. In fact, today's systems achieve performance levels that seem to surpass estimates of human inter annotator agreement (IAA). Therefore, it is unclear how well Smatch (still) relates to human estimates of parse quality, as in this situation potentially fine-grained errors of similar weight may impact the AMR's meaning to different degrees. We conduct an analysis of two popular and strong AMR parsers that -- according to Smatch -- reach quality levels on par with human IAA, and assess how human quality ratings relate to Smatch and other AMR metrics. Our main findings are: i) While high Smatch scores indicate otherwise, we find that AMR parsing is far from being solved: we frequently find structurally small, but semantically unacceptable errors that substantially distort sentence meaning. ii) Considering high-performance parsers, better Smatch scores may not necessarily indicate consistently better parsing quality. To obtain a meaningful and comprehensive assessment of quality differences of parse(r)s, we recommend augmenting evaluations with macro statistics, use of additional metrics, and more human analysis.
abstract_zh: 最近，通过结构 Smatch 指标衡量，AMR 解析取得了惊人的进展。事实上，当今的系统所达到的性能水平似乎超过了人类注释器协议 (IAA) 的估计。因此，目前尚不清楚 Smatch（仍然）与人类对解析质量的估计的关系如何，因为在这种情况下，相似权重的潜在细粒度错误可能会不同程度地影响 AMR 的含义。我们对两个流行且强大的 AMR 解析器进行了分析，根据 Smatch 的说法，它们达到了与人类 IAA 相当的质量水平，并评估了人类质量评级与 Smatch 和其他 AMR 指标的关系。我们的主要发现是： i) 虽然高 Smatch 分数表明情况并非如此，但我们发现 AMR 解析还远未得到解决：我们经常发现结构上很小但语义上不可接受的错误，这些错误严重扭曲了句子的含义。 ii) 考虑到高性能解析器，更好的 Smatch 分数可能不一定表明始终更好的解析质量。为了对 parse(r) 的质量差异进行有意义且全面的评估，我们建议通过宏观统计、使用其他指标和更多的人性分析来增强评估。

AI.tldr: 本文分析AMR解析质量评估中Smatch指标与人类标注一致性之间的关系，探讨当前高Smatch分数是否真正反映解析质量。
AI.motivation: 尽管AMR解析系统在Smatch指标上达到接近人类标注一致性的水平，但Smatch是否仍能准确反映解析质量存在疑问，尤其当细微结构错误可能显著影响语义时。
AI.method: 对两个高性能AMR解析器进行评估，结合Smatch及其他指标，对比人类对解析质量的评分，分析指标与人类判断的相关性。
AI.result: 发现高Smatch分数下仍存在语义错误导致语义扭曲，且更高Smatch分数未必对应更优解析质量，需结合宏观统计、额外指标及人类分析综合评估。
AI.conclusion: Smatch指标不足以全面评估AMR解析质量，建议结合多维度指标与人类分析以获得更准确的评估结果。

AMR evaluation is not so simple anymore 更好的匹配 = 更好的解析器？ AMR评估不再那么简单 Recently, astonishing advances have been observed in AMR parsing, as measured by the structural Smatch metric. In fact, today's systems achieve performance levels that seem to surpass estimates of human inter annotator agreement (IAA). Therefore, it is unclear how well Smatch (still) relates to human estimates of parse quality, as in this situation potentially fine-grained errors of similar weight may impact the AMR's meaning to different degrees. We conduct an analysis of two popular and strong AMR parsers that -- according to Smatch -- reach quality levels on par with human IAA, and assess how human quality ratings relate to Smatch and other AMR metrics. authors_text: Chaofei Wang,Qisen Yang,Rui Huang,Shiji Song,Gao Huang
title: Efficient Knowledge Distillation from Model Checkpoints
title_zh: 从模型检查点高效提取知识
abstract: Knowledge distillation is an effective approach to learn compact models (students) with the supervision of large and strong models (teachers). As empirically there exists a strong correlation between the performance of teacher and student models, it is commonly believed that a high performing teacher is preferred. Consequently, practitioners tend to use a well trained network or an ensemble of them as the teacher. In this paper, we make an intriguing observation that an intermediate model, i.e., a checkpoint in the middle of the training procedure, often serves as a better teacher compared to the fully converged model, although the former has much lower accuracy. More surprisingly, a weak snapshot ensemble of several intermediate models from a same training trajectory can outperform a strong ensemble of independently trained and fully converged models, when they are used as teachers. We show that this phenomenon can be partially explained by the information bottleneck principle: the feature representations of intermediate models can have higher mutual information regarding the input, and thus contain more "dark knowledge" for effective distillation. We further propose an optimal intermediate teacher selection algorithm based on maximizing the total task-related mutual information. Experiments verify its effectiveness and applicability.
summary: Knowledge distillation is an effective approach to learn compact models (students) with the supervision of large and strong models (teachers). As empirically there exists a strong correlation between the performance of teacher and student models, it is commonly believed that a high performing teacher is preferred. Consequently, practitioners tend to use a well trained network or an ensemble of them as the teacher. In this paper, we make an intriguing observation that an intermediate model, i.e., a checkpoint in the middle of the training procedure, often serves as a better teacher compared to the fully converged model, although the former has much lower accuracy. More surprisingly, a weak snapshot ensemble of several intermediate models from a same training trajectory can outperform a strong ensemble of independently trained and fully converged models, when they are used as teachers. We show that this phenomenon can be partially explained by the information bottleneck principle: the feature representations of intermediate models can have higher mutual information regarding the input, and thus contain more "dark knowledge" for effective distillation. We further propose an optimal intermediate teacher selection algorithm based on maximizing the total task-related mutual information. Experiments verify its effectiveness and applicability.
abstract_zh: 知识蒸馏是在大型强模型（教师）的监督下学习紧凑模型（学生）的有效方法。根据经验，教师和学生模型的表现之间存在很强的相关性，因此人们普遍认为表现好的教师是首选。因此，从业者倾向于使用训练有素的网络或它们的集合作为老师。在本文中，我们做了一个有趣的观察，即与完全收敛模型相比，中间模型（即训练过程中间的检查点）通常是更好的老师，尽管前者的精度要低得多。更令人惊讶的是，当它们被用作教师时，来自同一训练轨迹的多个中间模型的弱快照集成可以胜过独立训练和完全收敛模型的强集成。我们表明，这种现象可以通过信息瓶颈原理部分解释：中间模型的特征表示可以具有关于输入的更高的互信息，从而包含更多用于有效蒸馏的“暗知识”。我们进一步提出了一种基于最大化总任务相关互信息的最优中间教师选择算法。实验验证了其有效性和适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过中间训练阶段的模型检查点进行知识蒸馏，以提高学生模型的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统知识蒸馏依赖高性能的教师模型，但作者发现训练中期的中间模型虽准确率较低，却可能提供更有效的知识蒸馏信息。
AI.method: 基于信息瓶颈理论，提出通过最大化任务相关互信息选择最优中间教师模型，并采用同一训练轨迹中多个中间模型的快照集成作为教师。
AI.result: 实验表明，中间模型作为教师时，学生模型表现优于使用完全收敛模型；中间模型快照集成甚至超越独立训练的强教师集成。
AI.conclusion: 中间模型的特征表示包含更高输入相关互信息，蕴含更多‘暗知识’，其选择算法能有效提升知识蒸馏效率。

ai_content.result: 实验表明，中间模型作为教师时，学生模型表现优于使用完全收敛模型；中间模型快照集成甚至超越独立训练的强教师集成。

search_text: Efficient Knowledge Distillation from Model Checkpoints 从模型检查点高效提取知识 Knowledge distillation is an effective approach to learn compact models (students) with the supervision of large and strong models (teachers). As empirically there exists a strong correlation between the performance of teacher and student models, it is commonly believed that a high performing teacher is preferred. Consequently, practitioners tend to use a well trained network or an ensemble of them as the teacher. In this paper, we make an intriguing observation that an intermediate model, i.e., a checkpoint in the middle of the training procedure, often serves as a better teacher compared to the fully converged model, although the former has much lower accuracy. More surprisingly, a weak snapshot ensemble of several intermediate models from a same training trajectory can outperform a strong ensemble of independently trained and fully converged models, when they are used as teachers. We show that this phenomenon can be partially explained by the information bottleneck principle: the feature representations of intermediate models can have higher mutual information regarding the input, and thus contain more "dark knowledge" for effective distillation. We further propose an optimal intermediate teacher selection algorithm based on maximizing the total task-related mutual information. Experiments verify its effectiveness and applicability. 知识蒸馏是在大型强模型（教师）的监督下学习紧凑模型（学生）的有效方法。根据经验，教师和学生模型的表现之间存在很强的相关性，因此人们普遍认为表现好的教师是首选。因此，从业者倾向于使用训练有素的网络或它们的集合作为老师。在本文中，我们做了一个有趣的观察，即与完全收敛模型相比，中间模型（即训练过程中间的检查点）通常是更好的老师，尽管前者的精度要低得多。更令人惊讶的是，当它们被用作教师时，来自同一训练轨迹的多个中间模型的弱快照集成可以胜过独立训练和完全收敛模型的强集成。我们表明，这种现象可以通过信息瓶颈原理部分解释：中间模型的特征表示可以具有关于输入的更高的互信息，从而包含更多用于有效蒸馏的“暗知识”。我们进一步提出了一种基于最大化总任务相关互信息的最优中间教师选择算法。实验验证了其有效性和适用性。 本文提出通过中间训练阶段的模型检查点进行知识蒸馏，以提高学生模型的性能。 Chaofei Wang,Qisen Yang,Rui Huang,Shiji Song,Gao Huang cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Han Xiao,Wenzhao Zheng,Zheng Zhu,Jie Zhou,Jiwen Lu
title: Token-Label Alignment for Vision Transformers
title_zh: Vision Transformer 的令牌标签对齐
abstract: Data mixing strategies (e.g., CutMix) have shown the ability to greatly improve the performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). They mix two images as inputs for training and assign them with a mixed label with the same ratio. While they are shown effective for vision transformers (ViTs), we identify a token fluctuation phenomenon that has suppressed the potential of data mixing strategies. We empirically observe that the contributions of input tokens fluctuate as forward propagating, which might induce a different mixing ratio in the output tokens. The training target computed by the original data mixing strategy can thus be inaccurate, resulting in less effective training. To address this, we propose a token-label alignment (TL-Align) method to trace the correspondence between transformed tokens and the original tokens to maintain a label for each token. We reuse the computed attention at each layer for efficient token-label alignment, introducing only negligible additional training costs. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method improves the performance of ViTs on image classification, semantic segmentation, objective detection, and transfer learning tasks. Code is available at: https://github.com/Euphoria16/TL-Align.
summary: Data mixing strategies (e.g., CutMix) have shown the ability to greatly improve the performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). They mix two images as inputs for training and assign them with a mixed label with the same ratio. While they are shown effective for vision transformers (ViTs), we identify a token fluctuation phenomenon that has suppressed the potential of data mixing strategies. We empirically observe that the contributions of input tokens fluctuate as forward propagating, which might induce a different mixing ratio in the output tokens. The training target computed by the original data mixing strategy can thus be inaccurate, resulting in less effective training. To address this, we propose a token-label alignment (TL-Align) method to trace the correspondence between transformed tokens and the original tokens to maintain a label for each token. We reuse the computed attention at each layer for efficient token-label alignment, introducing only negligible additional training costs. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method improves the performance of ViTs on image classification, semantic segmentation, objective detection, and transfer learning tasks. Code is available at: https://github.com/Euphoria16/TL-Align.
abstract_zh: 数据混合策略（例如 CutMix）已显示出能够极大提高卷积神经网络（CNN）性能的能力。他们混合两个图像作为训练的输入，并为它们分配具有相同比例的混合标签。虽然它们被证明对视觉转换器（ViT）有效，但我们发现了一种令牌波动现象，抑制了数据混合策略的潜力。我们凭经验观察到，输入令牌的贡献随着前向传播而波动，这可能会导致输出令牌出现不同的混合比。因此，原始数据混合策略计算出的训练目标可能不准确，导致训练效果较差。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种令牌标签对齐（TL-Align）方法来跟踪转换后的令牌和原始令牌之间的对应关系，以维护每个令牌的标签。我们在每一层重用计算的注意力以实现有效的标记标签对齐，仅引入可忽略不计的额外训练成本。大量实验表明，我们的方法提高了 ViT 在图像分类、语义分割、目标检测和迁移学习任务上的性能。代码位于：https://github.com/Euphoria16/TL-Align。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种称为Token-Label Alignment（TL-Align）的方法，旨在通过改进视觉Transformer（ViT）中数据混合策略的标签分配机制，提升其在图像分类、语义分割等任务中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的数据混合策略（如CutMix）在卷积神经网络（CNN）中表现优异，但应用于视觉Transformer（ViT）时，由于输入token在前向传播过程中的贡献波动，导致输出token的混合比例不准确，限制了其潜力。
AI.method: 通过重用每一层的注意力计算结果，追踪转换后token与原始token的对应关系，为每个token维护准确的标签，从而实现高效的token-标签对齐。
AI.result: 实验表明，TL-Align方法在图像分类、语义分割、目标检测及迁移学习任务中显著提升了ViT的性能。
AI.conclusion: TL-Align有效解决了ViT中数据混合策略因token波动导致的标签分配不准确问题，为视觉Transformer的训练提供了更精确的监督信号。

authors_text: Lorenzo Bonicelli,Matteo Boschini,Angelo Porrello,Concetto Spampinato,Simone Calderara
title: On the Effectiveness of Lipschitz-Driven Rehearsal in Continual Learning
title_zh: 论利普希茨驱动的排练在持续学习中的有效性
comment: 22 pages, 8 figures, typos corrected. Accepted at the thirty-sixth Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022), New Orleans, US
comments: 22 pages, 8 figures, typos corrected. Accepted at the thirty-sixth Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022), New Orleans, US
abstract: Rehearsal approaches enjoy immense popularity with Continual Learning (CL) practitioners. These methods collect samples from previously encountered data distributions in a small memory buffer; subsequently, they repeatedly optimize on the latter to prevent catastrophic forgetting. This work draws attention to a hidden pitfall of this widespread practice: repeated optimization on a small pool of data inevitably leads to tight and unstable decision boundaries, which are a major hindrance to generalization. To address this issue, we propose Lipschitz-DrivEn Rehearsal (LiDER), a surrogate objective that induces smoothness in the backbone network by constraining its layer-wise Lipschitz constants w.r.t. replay examples. By means of extensive experiments, we show that applying LiDER delivers a stable performance gain to several state-of-the-art rehearsal CL methods across multiple datasets, both in the presence and absence of pre-training. Through additional ablative experiments, we highlight peculiar aspects of buffer overfitting in CL and better characterize the effect produced by LiDER. Code is available at https://github.com/aimagelab/LiDER
summary: Rehearsal approaches enjoy immense popularity with Continual Learning (CL) practitioners. These methods collect samples from previously encountered data distributions in a small memory buffer; subsequently, they repeatedly optimize on the latter to prevent catastrophic forgetting. This work draws attention to a hidden pitfall of this widespread practice: repeated optimization on a small pool of data inevitably leads to tight and unstable decision boundaries, which are a major hindrance to generalization. To address this issue, we propose Lipschitz-DrivEn Rehearsal (LiDER), a surrogate objective that induces smoothness in the backbone network by constraining its layer-wise Lipschitz constants w.r.t. replay examples. By means of extensive experiments, we show that applying LiDER delivers a stable performance gain to several state-of-the-art rehearsal CL methods across multiple datasets, both in the presence and absence of pre-training. Through additional ablative experiments, we highlight peculiar aspects of buffer overfitting in CL and better characterize the effect produced by LiDER. Code is available at https://github.com/aimagelab/LiDER
abstract_zh: 排练方法深受持续学习（CL）实践者的欢迎。这些方法从小型内存缓冲区中先前遇到的数据分布中收集样本；随后，他们反复优化后者以防止灾难性遗忘。这项工作引起了人们对这种广泛实践的一个隐藏陷阱的关注：对小数据池的重复优化不可避免地会导致紧密且不稳定的决策边界，这是泛化的主要障碍。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Lipschitz-DrivEn Rehearsal (LiDER)，这是一种替代目标，通过约束其分层 Lipschitz 常数来诱导主干网络的平滑度。重播示例。通过大量的实验，我们表明，无论是否存在预训练，应用 LiDER 都可以为跨多个数据集的几种最先进的排练 CL 方法带来稳定的性能增益。通过额外的烧蚀实验，我们强调了 CL 中缓冲液过度拟合的特殊方面，并更好地表征了 LiDER 产生的效果。代码可在 https://github.com/aimagelab/LiDER 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LiDER的新方法，通过约束网络层的Lipschitz常数来改进持续学习中的重放缓冲策略，以解决决策边界不稳定导致的泛化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有重放缓冲方法因反复优化小规模数据池，导致决策边界过紧且不稳定，严重影响模型泛化能力。
AI.method: 设计LiDER目标函数，通过限制网络各层对重放样本的Lipschitz常数，强制网络保持平滑性，从而缓解过拟合问题。
AI.result: 实验表明LiDER在多个数据集上显著提升主流重放缓冲方法的性能，无论是否存在预训练，并揭示了缓冲过拟合的特殊现象。
AI.conclusion: LiDER通过Lipschitz约束有效稳定决策边界，为持续学习中的重放缓冲策略提供了新的优化方向。

By means of extensive experiments, we show that applying LiDER delivers a stable performance gain to several state-of-the-art rehearsal CL methods across multiple datasets, both in the presence and absence of pre-training. Through additional ablative experiments, we highlight peculiar aspects of buffer overfitting in CL and better characterize the effect produced by LiDER. Code is available at https://github.com/aimagelab/LiDER 排练方法深受持续学习（CL）实践者的欢迎。这些方法从小型内存缓冲区中先前遇到的数据分布中收集样本；随后，他们反复优化后者以防止灾难性遗忘。这项工作引起了人们对这种广泛实践的一个隐藏陷阱的关注：对小数据池的重复优化不可避免地会导致紧密且不稳定的决策边界，这是泛化的主要障碍。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Lipschitz-DrivEn Rehearsal (LiDER)，这是一种替代目标，通过约束其分层 Lipschitz 常数来诱导主干网络的平滑度。重播示例。通过大量的实验，我们表明，无论是否存在预训练，应用 LiDER 都可以为跨多个数据集的几种最先进的排练 CL 方法带来稳定的性能增益。通过额外的烧蚀实验，我们强调了 CL 中缓冲液过度拟合的特殊方面，并更好地表征了 LiDER 产生的效果。代码可在 https://github.com/aimagelab/LiDER 获取 本文提出了一种名为LiDER的新方法，通过约束网络层的Lipschitz常数来改进持续学习中的重放缓冲策略，以解决决策边界不稳定导致的泛化问题。 Lorenzo Bonicelli,Matteo Boschini,Angelo Porrello,Concetto Spampinato,Simone Calderara cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Jonas Geiping,Micah Goldblum,Gowthami Somepalli,Ravid Shwartz-Ziv,Tom Goldstein,Andrew Gordon Wilson
title: How Much Data Are Augmentations Worth? An Investigation into Scaling Laws, Invariance, and Implicit Regularization
title_zh: 增强数据有多少价值？对缩放定律、不变性和隐式正则化的研究
comment: 31 pages, 29 figures. To be presented at ICLR 2023. Code at https://github.com/JonasGeiping/dataaugs
comments: 31 pages, 29 figures. To be presented at ICLR 2023. Code at https://github.com/JonasGeiping/dataaugs
abstract: Despite the clear performance benefits of data augmentations, little is known about why they are so effective. In this paper, we disentangle several key mechanisms through which data augmentations operate. Establishing an exchange rate between augmented and additional real data, we find that in out-of-distribution testing scenarios, augmentations which yield samples that are diverse, but inconsistent with the data distribution can be even more valuable than additional training data. Moreover, we find that data augmentations which encourage invariances can be more valuable than invariance alone, especially on small and medium sized training sets. Following this observation, we show that augmentations induce additional stochasticity during training, effectively flattening the loss landscape.
summary: Despite the clear performance benefits of data augmentations, little is known about why they are so effective. In this paper, we disentangle several key mechanisms through which data augmentations operate. Establishing an exchange rate between augmented and additional real data, we find that in out-of-distribution testing scenarios, augmentations which yield samples that are diverse, but inconsistent with the data distribution can be even more valuable than additional training data. Moreover, we find that data augmentations which encourage invariances can be more valuable than invariance alone, especially on small and medium sized training sets. Following this observation, we show that augmentations induce additional stochasticity during training, effectively flattening the loss landscape.
abstract_zh: 尽管数据增强具有明显的性能优势，但人们对它们为何如此有效却知之甚少。在本文中，我们阐明了数据增强操作的几个关键机制。在增强数据和附加真实数据之间建立交换率，我们发现在分布外测试场景中，产生多样化但与数据分布不一致的样本的增强可能比附加训练数据更有价值。此外，我们发现鼓励不变性的数据增强比单独的不变性更有价值，特别是在中小型训练集上。根据这一观察，我们表明增强会在训练过程中引起额外的随机性，从而有效地使损失景观变得平坦。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨数据增强的有效性，分析其在扩展定律、不变性和隐式正则化中的作用机制。
AI.motivation: 尽管数据增强提升模型性能，但其机制尚不明确，需深入理解其作用原理。
AI.method: 通过建立增强数据与真实数据的交换率，分析不同增强策略对模型性能的影响，并结合实验验证其效果。
AI.result: 发现多样性但不一致的增强样本在分布外测试中价值更高，鼓励不变性的增强在小数据集更有效，且增强引入随机性有助于平坦化损失景观。
AI.conclusion: 数据增强通过多样性、不变性及隐式正则化机制提升模型泛化能力，尤其在小数据和分布外场景中效果显著。

authors_text: Nikhil Parthasarathy,S. M. Ali Eslami,João Carreira,Olivier J. Hénaff
title: Self-supervised video pretraining yields robust and more human-aligned visual representations
title_zh: 自监督视频预训练可产生强大且更符合人类的视觉表示
comment: Accepted to 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
comments: Accepted to 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
abstract: Humans learn powerful representations of objects and scenes by observing how they evolve over time. Yet, outside of specific tasks that require explicit temporal understanding, static image pretraining remains the dominant paradigm for learning visual foundation models. We question this mismatch, and ask whether video pretraining can yield visual representations that bear the hallmarks of human perception: generalisation across tasks, robustness to perturbations, and consistency with human judgements. To that end we propose a novel procedure for curating videos, and develop a contrastive framework which learns from the complex transformations therein. This simple paradigm for distilling knowledge from videos, called VITO, yields general representations that far outperform prior video pretraining methods on image understanding tasks, and image pretraining methods on video understanding tasks. Moreover, VITO representations are significantly more robust to natural and synthetic deformations than image-, video-, and adversarially-trained ones. Finally, VITO's predictions are strongly aligned with human judgements, surpassing models that were specifically trained for that purpose. Together, these results suggest that video pretraining could be a simple way of learning unified, robust, and human-aligned representations of the visual world.
summary: Humans learn powerful representations of objects and scenes by observing how they evolve over time. Yet, outside of specific tasks that require explicit temporal understanding, static image pretraining remains the dominant paradigm for learning visual foundation models. We question this mismatch, and ask whether video pretraining can yield visual representations that bear the hallmarks of human perception: generalisation across tasks, robustness to perturbations, and consistency with human judgements. To that end we propose a novel procedure for curating videos, and develop a contrastive framework which learns from the complex transformations therein. This simple paradigm for distilling knowledge from videos, called VITO, yields general representations that far outperform prior video pretraining methods on image understanding tasks, and image pretraining methods on video understanding tasks. Moreover, VITO representations are significantly more robust to natural and synthetic deformations than image-, video-, and adversarially-trained ones. Finally, VITO's predictions are strongly aligned with human judgements, surpassing models that were specifically trained for that purpose. Together, these results suggest that video pretraining could be a simple way of learning unified, robust, and human-aligned representations of the visual world.
abstract_zh: 人类通过观察物体和场景如何随时间演变来学习它们的强大表征。然而，除了需要明确时间理解的特定任务之外，静态图像预训练仍然是学习视觉基础模型的主导范例。我们质疑这种不匹配，并询问视频预训练是否可以产生具有人类感知特征的视觉表示：跨任务的泛化、对扰动的鲁棒性以及与人类判断的一致性。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的视频管理程序，并开发了一个对比框架，可以从其中的复杂转换中学习。这种从视频中提取知识的简单范式称为 VITO，它产生的通用表示远远优于先前在图像理解任务上的视频预训练方法以及在视频理解任务上的图像预训练方法。此外，VITO 表示对于自然和合成变形比图像、视频和对抗训练的表示更加稳健。最后，VITO 的预测与人类的判断高度一致，超越了专门为此目的训练的模型。总之，这些结果表明，视频预训练可能是学习视觉世界的统一、稳健且符合人类特征的表示的一种简单方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为VITO的视频预训练方法，通过自监督学习获得更符合人类感知的视觉表示。
AI.motivation: 当前视觉基础模型主要依赖静态图像预训练，但人类感知依赖于时间动态变化，视频预训练可能更接近人类认知方式。
AI.method: 提出视频筛选方法和对比学习框架VITO，通过学习视频中复杂变换来提炼通用视觉表示。
AI.result: VITO在图像和视频理解任务中均优于现有方法，对形变更鲁棒，且预测结果更符合人类判断。
AI.conclusion: 视频预训练能有效学习统一、鲁棒且符合人类认知的视觉表示，为视觉基础模型提供新范式。

authors_text: Haotao Wang,Junyuan Hong,Aston Zhang,Jiayu Zhou,Zhangyang Wang
title: Trap and Replace: Defending Backdoor Attacks by Trapping Them into an Easy-to-Replace Subnetwork
title_zh: 陷阱和替换：通过将后门攻击捕获到易于替换的子网络中来防御后门攻击
comment: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/Trap-and-Replace-Backdoor-Defense
comments: Accepted by NeurIPS 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/Trap-and-Replace-Backdoor-Defense
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are vulnerable to backdoor attacks. Previous works have shown it extremely challenging to unlearn the undesired backdoor behavior from the network, since the entire network can be affected by the backdoor samples. In this paper, we propose a brand-new backdoor defense strategy, which makes it much easier to remove the harmful influence of backdoor samples from the model. Our defense strategy, \emph{Trap and Replace}, consists of two stages. In the first stage, we bait and trap the backdoors in a small and easy-to-replace subnetwork. Specifically, we add an auxiliary image reconstruction head on top of the stem network shared with a light-weighted classification head. The intuition is that the auxiliary image reconstruction task encourages the stem network to keep sufficient low-level visual features that are hard to learn but semantically correct, instead of overfitting to the easy-to-learn but semantically incorrect backdoor correlations. As a result, when trained on backdoored datasets, the backdoors are easily baited towards the unprotected classification head, since it is much more vulnerable than the shared stem, leaving the stem network hardly poisoned. In the second stage, we replace the poisoned light-weighted classification head with an untainted one, by re-training it from scratch only on a small holdout dataset with clean samples, while fixing the stem network. As a result, both the stem and the classification head in the final network are hardly affected by backdoor training samples. We evaluate our method against ten different backdoor attacks. Our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods by up to $20.57\%$, $9.80\%$, and $13.72\%$ attack success rate and on-average $3.14\%$, $1.80\%$, and $1.21\%$ clean classification accuracy on CIFAR10, GTSRB, and ImageNet-12, respectively. Code is available online.

Our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods by up to $20.57\%$, $9.80\%$, and $13.72\%$ attack success rate and on-average $3.14\%$, $1.80\%$, and $1.21\%$ clean classification accuracy on CIFAR10, GTSRB, and ImageNet-12, respectively. Code is available online.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 很容易受到后门攻击。先前的工作表明，从网络中消除不需要的后门行为极具挑战性，因为整个网络可能会受到后门样本的影响。在本文中，我们提出了一种全新的后门防御策略，可以更轻松地从模型中消除后门样本的有害影响。我们的防御策略，\emph{陷阱和替换}，由两个阶段组成。在第一阶段，我们将后门诱骗并困在一个小型且易于替换的子网络中。具体来说，我们在与轻量级分类头共享的干网络之上添加了一个辅助图像重建头。直觉是，辅助图像重建任务鼓励干网络保留足够的难以学习但语义正确的低级视觉特征，而不是过度拟合易于学习但语义不正确的后门相关性。因此，当在后门数据集上进行训练时，后门很容易被引向未受保护的分类头，因为它比共享主干更容易受到攻击，从而使主干网络几乎不会中毒。在第二阶段，我们用未受污染的轻量级分类头替换了有毒的轻量级分类头，仅在具有干净样本的小型保留数据集上从头开始重新训练，同时修复干网络。因此，最终网络中的茎和分类头几乎不受后门训练样本的影响。我们针对十种不同的后门攻击评估了我们的方法。我们的方法比之前最先进的方法在 CIFAR10、GTSRB 和 ImageNet-12 上的攻击成功率分别高出高达 $20.57\%$、$9.80\%$ 和 $13.72\%$ 的攻击成功率以及平均 $3.14\%$、$1.80\%$ 和 $1.21\%$ 的干净分类精度。代码可在线获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的防御后门攻击的方法，通过将后门困在容易替换的子网络中，从而更有效地移除后门的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有的防御方法难以有效移除后门行为，因为整个网络可能被后门样本污染，而本文旨在通过隔离后门到易替换的子网络来简化移除过程。
AI.method: 方法分为两个阶段：第一阶段通过添加辅助图像重建头，将后门引诱至轻量级分类头；第二阶段用干净样本重新训练该分类头，同时保持主干网络不变。
AI.result: 在CIFAR10、GTSRB和ImageNet-12数据集上，攻击成功率分别降低最多20.57%、9.80%和13.72%，同时干净样本分类准确率平均提升3.14%、1.80%和1.21%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过隔离和替换策略有效防御后门攻击，显著优于现有技术。

search_text: Trap and Replace: Defending Backdoor Attacks by Trapping Them into an Easy-to-Replace Subnetwork 陷阱和替换：通过将后门攻击捕获到易于替换的子网络中来防御后门攻击 Deep neural networks (DNNs) are vulnerable to backdoor attacks. Previous works have shown it extremely challenging to unlearn the undesired backdoor behavior from the network, since the entire network can be affected by the backdoor samples. In this paper, we propose a brand-new backdoor defense strategy, which makes it much easier to remove the harmful influence of backdoor samples from the model. Our defense strategy, \emph{Trap and Replace}, consists of two stages. In the first stage, we bait and trap the backdoors in a small and easy-to-replace subnetwork. Specifically, we add an auxiliary image reconstruction head on top of the stem network shared with a light-weighted classification head. The intuition is that the auxiliary image reconstruction task encourages the stem network to keep sufficient low-level visual features that are hard to learn but semantically correct, instead of overfitting to the easy-to-learn but semantically incorrect backdoor correlations. As a result, when trained on backdoored datasets, the backdoors are easily baited towards the unprotected classification head, since it is much more vulnerable than the shared stem, leaving the stem network hardly poisoned. In the second stage, we replace the poisoned light-weighted classification head with an untainted one, by re-training it from scratch only on a small holdout dataset with clean samples, while fixing the stem network. As a result, both the stem and the classification head in the final network are hardly affected by backdoor training samples. We evaluate our method against ten different backdoor attacks. Our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods by up to $20.57\%$, $9.80\%$, and $13.72\%$ attack success rate and on-average $3.14\%$, $1.80\%$, and $1.21\%$ clean classification accuracy on CIFAR10, GTSRB, and ImageNet-12, respectively. Code is available online. 深度神经网络 (DNN) 很容易受到后门攻击。先前的工作表明，从网络中消除不需要的后门行为极具挑战性，因为整个网络可能会受到后门样本的影响。在本文中，我们提出了一种全新的后门防御策略，可以更轻松地从模型中消除后门样本的有害影响。我们的防御策略，\emph{陷阱和替换}，由两个阶段组成。在第一阶段，我们将后门诱骗并困在一个小型且易于替换的子网络中。具体来说，我们在与轻量级分类头共享的干网络之上添加了一个辅助图像重建头。直觉是，辅助图像重建任务鼓励干网络保留足够的难以学习但语义正确的低级视觉特征，而不是过度拟合易于学习但语义不正确的后门相关性。因此，当在后门数据集上进行训练时，后门很容易被引向未受保护的分类头，因为它比共享主干更容易受到攻击，从而使主干网络几乎不会中毒。在第二阶段，我们用未受污染的轻量级分类头替换了有毒的轻量级分类头，仅在具有干净样本的小型保留数据集上从头开始重新训练，同时修复干网络。因此，最终网络中的茎和分类头几乎不受后门训练样本的影响。我们针对十种不同的后门攻击评估了我们的方法。我们的方法比之前最先进的方法在 CIFAR10、GTSRB 和 ImageNet-12 上的攻击成功率分别高出高达 $20.57\%$、$9.80\%$ 和 $13.72\%$ 的攻击成功率以及平均 $3.14\%$、$1.80\%$ 和 $1.21\%$ 的干净分类精度。代码可在线获取。 本文提出了一种新的防御后门攻击的方法，通过将后门困在容易替换的子网络中，从而更有效地移除后门的影响。 Haotao Wang,Junyuan Hong,Aston Zhang,Jiayu Zhou,Zhangyang Wang cs.CR,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Hongyu Wang,Shuming Ma,Shaohan Huang,Li Dong,Wenhui Wang,Zhiliang Peng,Yu Wu,Payal Bajaj,Saksham Singhal,Alon Benhaim,Barun Patra,Zhun Liu,Vishrav Chaudhary,Xia Song,Furu Wei
title: Foundation Transformers
title_zh: 基础变形金刚
abstract: A big convergence of model architectures across language, vision, speech, and multimodal is emerging. However, under the same name "Transformers", the above areas use different implementations for better performance, e.g., Post-LayerNorm for BERT, and Pre-LayerNorm for GPT and vision Transformers. We call for the development of Foundation Transformer for true general-purpose modeling, which serves as a go-to architecture for various tasks and modalities with guaranteed training stability. In this work, we introduce a Transformer variant, named Magneto, to fulfill the goal. Specifically, we propose Sub-LayerNorm for good expressivity, and the initialization strategy theoretically derived from DeepNet for stable scaling up. Extensive experiments demonstrate its superior performance and better stability than the de facto Transformer variants designed for various applications, including language modeling (i.e., BERT, and GPT), machine translation, vision pretraining (i.e., BEiT), speech recognition, and multimodal pretraining (i.e., BEiT-3).
summary: A big convergence of model architectures across language, vision, speech, and multimodal is emerging. However, under the same name "Transformers", the above areas use different implementations for better performance, e.g., Post-LayerNorm for BERT, and Pre-LayerNorm for GPT and vision Transformers. We call for the development of Foundation Transformer for true general-purpose modeling, which serves as a go-to architecture for various tasks and modalities with guaranteed training stability. In this work, we introduce a Transformer variant, named Magneto, to fulfill the goal. Specifically, we propose Sub-LayerNorm for good expressivity, and the initialization strategy theoretically derived from DeepNet for stable scaling up. Extensive experiments demonstrate its superior performance and better stability than the de facto Transformer variants designed for various applications, including language modeling (i.e., BERT, and GPT), machine translation, vision pretraining (i.e., BEiT), speech recognition, and multimodal pretraining (i.e., BEiT-3).
abstract_zh: 跨语言、视觉、语音和多模式的模型架构的大融合正在出现。然而，在同一个名称“Transformers”下，上述领域使用不​​同的实现来获得更好的性能，例如，BERT 使用 Post-LayerNorm，GPT 和 Vision Transformers 使用 Pre-LayerNorm。我们呼吁开发 Foundation Transformer 来实现真正的通用建模，它可以作为各种任务和模式的首选架构，并保证训练稳定性。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个名为 Magneto 的 Transformer 变体来实现这一目标。具体来说，我们提出了 Sub-LayerNorm 以获得良好的表达能力，并从理论上从 DeepNet 导出初始化策略以实现稳定的扩展。大量实验证明，与为各种应用设计的事实上的 Transformer 变体相比，它具有卓越的性能和更好的稳定性，包括语言建模（即 BERT 和 GPT）、机器翻译、视觉预训练（即 BEiT）、语音识别和多模态预训练（即 BEiT-3）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Magneto的通用Transformer架构，旨在实现跨语言、视觉、语音和多模态任务的稳定训练与优越性能。
AI.motivation: 当前不同领域（如NLP、CV）的Transformer变体（如BERT用Post-LayerNorm，GPT用Pre-LayerNorm）缺乏统一标准，导致训练稳定性与通用性不足。
AI.method: 提出Sub-LayerNorm增强模型表达能力，并基于DeepNet理论推导初始化策略，确保大规模扩展时的稳定性。
AI.result: 在语言建模（BERT/GPT）、机器翻译、视觉预训练（BEiT）、语音识别及多模态预训练（BEiT-3）等任务中，Magneto均展现优于现有方法的性能与稳定性。
AI.conclusion: Magneto作为首个通用Foundation Transformer，为多模态与跨任务建模提供了稳定且高效的架构基础。

authors_text: Andrew M. Childs,Robin Kothari,Matt Kovacs-Deak,Aarthi Sundaram,Daochen Wang
title: Quantum divide and conquer
title_zh: 量子分而治之
abstract: The divide-and-conquer framework, used extensively in classical algorithm design, recursively breaks a problem of size $n$ into smaller subproblems (say, $a$ copies of size $n/b$ each), along with some auxiliary work of cost $C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$, to give a recurrence relation $$C(n) \leq a \, C(n/b) + C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$$ for the classical complexity $C(n)$. We describe a quantum divide-and-conquer framework that, in certain cases, yields an analogous recurrence relation $$C_Q(n) \leq \sqrt{a} \, C_Q(n/b) + O(C^{\textrm{aux}}_Q(n))$$ that characterizes the quantum query complexity. We apply this framework to obtain near-optimal quantum query complexities for various string problems, such as (i) recognizing regular languages; (ii) decision versions of String Rotation and String Suffix; and natural parameterized versions of (iii) Longest Increasing Subsequence and (iv) Longest Common Subsequence.
summary: The divide-and-conquer framework, used extensively in classical algorithm design, recursively breaks a problem of size $n$ into smaller subproblems (say, $a$ copies of size $n/b$ each), along with some auxiliary work of cost $C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$, to give a recurrence relation $$C(n) \leq a \, C(n/b) + C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$$ for the classical complexity $C(n)$. We describe a quantum divide-and-conquer framework that, in certain cases, yields an analogous recurrence relation $$C_Q(n) \leq \sqrt{a} \, C_Q(n/b) + O(C^{\textrm{aux}}_Q(n))$$ that characterizes the quantum query complexity. We apply this framework to obtain near-optimal quantum query complexities for various string problems, such as (i) recognizing regular languages; (ii) decision versions of String Rotation and String Suffix; and natural parameterized versions of (iii) Longest Increasing Subsequence and (iv) Longest Common Subsequence.
abstract_zh: 经典算法设计中广泛使用的分而治之框架，递归地将大小为 $n$ 的问题分解为较小的子问题（例如，每个大小为 $n/b$ 的 $a$ 副本），以及成本 $C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$ 的一些辅助工作，以给出经典算法的递归关系 $$C(n) \leq a \, C(n/b) + C^{\textrm{aux}}(n)$$复杂度$C(n)$。我们描述了一个量子分治框架，在某些情况下，产生一个类似的递归关系 $$C_Q(n) \leq \sqrt{a} \, C_Q(n/b) + O(C^{\textrm{aux}}_Q(n))$$ 来表征量子查询复杂性。我们应用这个框架来获得各种字符串问题的接近最优的量子查询复杂性，例如（i）识别常规语言； (ii) 字符串旋转和字符串后缀的决策版本；以及 (iii) 最长递增子序列和 (iv) 最长公共子序列的自然参数化版本。

AI.tldr: 提出量子分治框架，用于解决多个字符串问题，降低量子查询复杂度。
AI.motivation: 经典分治框架在经典算法中广泛应用，但量子计算可能提供更优的复杂度，需探索量子分治方法。
AI.method: 提出量子分治框架，将经典递推式转化为量子查询复杂度递推式，其中a替换为√a，并分析辅助成本。
