authors_text: Sewade Ogun,Vincent Colotte,Emmanuel Vincent
title: Can we use Common Voice to train a Multi-Speaker TTS system?
title_zh: 我们可以使用 Common Voice 来训练多说话者 TTS 系统吗？
comment: To appear in Proc. SLT 2022, Jan 09-12, 2023, Doha, Qatar
comments: To appear in Proc. SLT 2022, Jan 09-12, 2023, Doha, Qatar
abstract: Training of multi-speaker text-to-speech (TTS) systems relies on curated datasets based on high-quality recordings or audiobooks. Such datasets often lack speaker diversity and are expensive to collect. As an alternative, recent studies have leveraged the availability of large, crowdsourced automatic speech recognition (ASR) datasets. A major problem with such datasets is the presence of noisy and/or distorted samples, which degrade TTS quality. In this paper, we propose to automatically select high-quality training samples using a non-intrusive mean opinion score (MOS) estimator, WV-MOS. We show the viability of this approach for training a multi-speaker GlowTTS model on the Common Voice English dataset. Our approach improves the overall quality of generated utterances by 1.26 MOS point with respect to training on all the samples and by 0.35 MOS point with respect to training on the LibriTTS dataset. This opens the door to automatic TTS dataset curation for a wider range of languages.
summary: Training of multi-speaker text-to-speech (TTS) systems relies on curated datasets based on high-quality recordings or audiobooks. Such datasets often lack speaker diversity and are expensive to collect. As an alternative, recent studies have leveraged the availability of large, crowdsourced automatic speech recognition (ASR) datasets. A major problem with such datasets is the presence of noisy and/or distorted samples, which degrade TTS quality. In this paper, we propose to automatically select high-quality training samples using a non-intrusive mean opinion score (MOS) estimator, WV-MOS. We show the viability of this approach for training a multi-speaker GlowTTS model on the Common Voice English dataset. Our approach improves the overall quality of generated utterances by 1.26 MOS point with respect to training on all the samples and by 0.35 MOS point with respect to training on the LibriTTS dataset. This opens the door to automatic TTS dataset curation for a wider range of languages.
abstract_zh: 多说话者文本转语音 (TTS) 系统的训练依赖于基于高质量录音或有声读物的精选数据集。此类数据集通常缺乏说话者多样性，并且收集成本昂贵。作为替代方案，最近的研究利用了大型众包自动语音识别 (ASR) 数据集的可用性。此类数据集的一个主要问题是存在噪声和/或失真样本，这会降低 TTS 质量。在本文中，我们建议使用非侵入式平均意见得分（MOS）估计器 WV-MOS 自动选择高质量的训练样本。我们展示了这种方法在 Common Voice English 数据集上训练多说话者 GlowTTS 模型的可行性。我们的方法在所有样本上的训练中将生成的话语的整体质量提高了 1.26 MOS 点，在 LibriTTS 数据集上的训练中提高了 0.35 MOS 点。这为更广泛的语言自动 TTS 数据集管理打开了大门。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨利用Common Voice数据集训练多说话人文本到语音（TTS）系统的可行性。
AI.motivation: 传统多说话人TTS系统依赖高质量录音数据集，但这些数据集成本高且说话人多样性不足；Common Voice等众包数据集虽规模大，但存在噪声和失真样本，影响TTS质量。
AI.method: 采用非侵入式MOS估计器WV-MOS自动筛选高质量样本，并基于Common Voice英语数据集训练多说话人GlowTTS模型。
AI.result: 相较使用全部样本训练，该方法使生成语音质量提升1.26 MOS点；相较LibriTTS数据集训练，质量提升0.35 MOS点。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为多语言TTS系统的自动数据集构建提供了可行方案，拓宽了语言覆盖范围。

authors_text: Tao Sun,Nidal Abuhajar,Shuyu Gong,Zhewei Wang,Charles D. Smith,Xianhui Wang,Li Xu,Jundong Liu
title: Individualized Conditioning and Negative Distances for Speaker Separation
title_zh: 用于扬声器分离的个性化调节和负距离
abstract: Speaker separation aims to extract multiple voices from a mixed signal. In this paper, we propose two speaker-aware designs to improve the existing speaker separation solutions. The first model is a speaker conditioning network that integrates speech samples to generate individualized speaker conditions, which then provide informed guidance for a separation module to produce well-separated outputs. The second design aims to reduce non-target voices in the separated speech. To this end, we propose negative distances to penalize the appearance of any non-target voice in the channel outputs, and positive distances to drive the separated voices closer to the clean targets. We explore two different setups, weighted-sum and triplet-like, to integrate these two distances to form a combined auxiliary loss for the separation networks. Experiments conducted on LibriMix demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed models.
summary: Speaker separation aims to extract multiple voices from a mixed signal. In this paper, we propose two speaker-aware designs to improve the existing speaker separation solutions. The first model is a speaker conditioning network that integrates speech samples to generate individualized speaker conditions, which then provide informed guidance for a separation module to produce well-separated outputs. The second design aims to reduce non-target voices in the separated speech. To this end, we propose negative distances to penalize the appearance of any non-target voice in the channel outputs, and positive distances to drive the separated voices closer to the clean targets. We explore two different setups, weighted-sum and triplet-like, to integrate these two distances to form a combined auxiliary loss for the separation networks. Experiments conducted on LibriMix demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed models.
abstract_zh: 说话人分离的目的是从混合信号中提取多个声音。在本文中，我们提出了两种扬声器感知设计来改进现有的扬声器分离解决方案。第一个模型是说话人调节网络，它集成语音样本以生成个性化的说话人条件，然后为分离模块提供明智的指导以产生良好分离的输出。第二种设计旨在减少分离语音中的非目标语音。为此，我们提出负距离来惩罚通道输出中任何非目标语音的出现，并提出正距离来驱动分离的语音更接近干净的目标。我们探索了两种不同的设置，加权和和三重态，以整合这两个距离以形成分离网络的组合辅助损失。在 LibriMix 上进行的实验证明了我们提出的模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种说话人感知设计，改进现有说话人分离方法，通过个性化条件网络和负距离惩罚提升分离效果。
AI.motivation: 现有说话人分离方法在复杂场景下难以有效区分重叠语音，需增强模型对个体说话人特征的感知能力并抑制非目标语音干扰。
AI.method: 设计个性化条件网络生成说话人专属特征向量，结合负距离损失惩罚非目标语音出现，正距离损失拉近分离语音与目标语音的距离，采用加权和与三元组结构整合损失函数。
AI.result: 在LibriMix数据集实验中，所提方法在语音分离指标上优于基线模型，有效提升目标语音纯净度与分离准确性。
AI.conclusion: 个性化条件建模与负距离约束机制可显著提升说话人分离性能，为多说话人语音处理提供新思路。

ai_content_json.result: 在LibriMix数据集实验中，所提方法在语音分离指标上优于基线模型，有效提升目标语音纯净度与分离准确性。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 个性化条件建模与负距离约束机制可显著提升说话人分离性能，为多说话人语音处理提供新思路。

tldr: 本文提出两种说话人感知设计，改进现有说话人分离方法，通过个性化条件网络和负距离惩罚提升分离效果。
motivation: 现有说话人分离方法在复杂场景下难以有效区分重叠语音，需增强模型对个体说话人特征的感知能力并抑制非目标语音干扰。
method: 设计个性化条件网络生成说话人专属特征向量，结合负距离损失惩罚非目标语音出现，正距离损失拉近分离语音与目标语音的距离，采用加权和与三元组结构整合损失函数。
result: 在LibriMix数据集实验中，所提方法在语音分离指标上优于基线模型，有效提升目标语音纯净度与分离准确性。
conclusion: 个性化条件建模与负距离约束机制可显著提升说话人分离性能，为多说话人语音处理提供新思路。
search_text: Individualized Conditioning and Negative Distances for Speaker Separation 用于扬声器分离的个性化调节和负距离 Speaker separation aims to extract multiple voices from a mixed signal. In this paper, we propose two speaker-aware designs to improve the existing speaker separation solutions. The first model is a speaker conditioning network that integrates speech samples to generate individualized speaker conditions, which then provide informed guidance for a separation module to produce well-separated outputs. The second design aims to reduce non-target voices in the separated speech. To this end, we propose negative distances to penalize the appearance of any non-target voice in the channel outputs, and positive distances to drive the separated voices closer to the clean targets. We explore two different setups, weighted-sum and triplet-like, to integrate these two distances to form a combined auxiliary loss for the separation networks. Experiments conducted on LibriMix demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed models. 说话人分离的目的是从混合信号中提取多个声音。在本文中，我们提出了两种扬声器感知设计来改进现有的扬声器分离解决方案。第一个模型是说话人调节网络，它集成语音样本以生成个性化的说话人条件，然后为分离模块提供明智的指导以产生良好分离的输出。第二种设计旨在减少分离语音中的非目标语音。为此，我们提出负距离来惩罚通道输出中任何非目标语音的出现，并提出正距离来驱动分离的语音更接近干净的目标。我们探索了两种不同的设置，加权和和三重态，以整合这两个距离以形成分离网络的组合辅助损失。在 LibriMix 上进行的实验证明了我们提出的模型的有效性。 本文提出两种说话人感知设计，改进现有说话人分离方法，通过个性化条件网络和负距离惩罚提升分离效果。 Tao Sun,Nidal Abuhajar,Shuyu Gong,Zhewei Wang,Charles D. Smith,Xianhui Wang,Li Xu,Jundong Liu cs.SD,cs.AI,eess.AS

authors_text: Ting Chen,Lala Li,Saurabh Saxena,Geoffrey Hinton,David J. Fleet
title: A Generalist Framework for Panoptic Segmentation of Images and Videos
title_zh: 图像和视频全景分割的通用框架
abstract: Panoptic segmentation assigns semantic and instance ID labels to every pixel of an image. As permutations of instance IDs are also valid solutions, the task requires learning of high-dimensional one-to-many mapping. As a result, state-of-the-art approaches use customized architectures and task-specific loss functions. We formulate panoptic segmentation as a discrete data generation problem, without relying on inductive bias of the task. A diffusion model is proposed to model panoptic masks, with a simple architecture and generic loss function. By simply adding past predictions as a conditioning signal, our method is capable of modeling video (in a streaming setting) and thereby learns to track object instances automatically. With extensive experiments, we demonstrate that our simple approach can perform competitively to state-of-the-art specialist methods in similar settings.
summary: Panoptic segmentation assigns semantic and instance ID labels to every pixel of an image. As permutations of instance IDs are also valid solutions, the task requires learning of high-dimensional one-to-many mapping. As a result, state-of-the-art approaches use customized architectures and task-specific loss functions. We formulate panoptic segmentation as a discrete data generation problem, without relying on inductive bias of the task. A diffusion model is proposed to model panoptic masks, with a simple architecture and generic loss function. By simply adding past predictions as a conditioning signal, our method is capable of modeling video (in a streaming setting) and thereby learns to track object instances automatically. With extensive experiments, we demonstrate that our simple approach can perform competitively to state-of-the-art specialist methods in similar settings.
abstract_zh: 全景分割为图像的每个像素分配语义和实例 ID 标签。由于实例 ID 的排列也是有效的解决方案，因此该任务需要学习高维一对多映射。因此，最先进的方法使用定制的架构和特定于任务的损失函数。我们将全景分割表述为离散数据生成问题，而不依赖于任务的归纳偏差。提出了一种扩散模型来模拟全景掩模，具有简单的架构和通用的损失函数。通过简单地将过去的预测添加为条件信号，我们的方法能够对视频进行建模（在流媒体设置中），从而学习自动跟踪对象实例。通过大量的实验，我们证明了我们的简单方法可以在类似的环境中与最先进的专业方法相媲美。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通用框架，用于图像和视频的全景分割，无需依赖任务的归纳偏置。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖定制化架构和任务特定损失函数，难以兼顾图像与视频分割的通用性与自动实例跟踪能力。
AI.method: 将全景分割建模为离散数据生成问题，采用扩散模型结合简单架构与通用损失函数，并通过历史预测作为条件信号实现视频流中的自动实例跟踪。
AI.result: 在多个基准测试中，该方法在图像和视频分割任务上均达到与现有先进方法相当的性能水平。
AI.conclusion: 提出的通用框架通过简化模型设计，在保持竞争力的同时实现了图像与视频全景分割的统一处理。

authors_text: Shiv Ram Dubey,Satish Kumar Singh,Bidyut Baran Chaudhuri
title: AdaNorm: Adaptive Gradient Norm Correction based Optimizer for CNNs
title_zh: AdaNorm：基于自适应梯度范数校正的 CNN 优化器
comment: IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV), 2023
comments: IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV), 2023
abstract: The stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimizers are generally used to train the convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In recent years, several adaptive momentum based SGD optimizers have been introduced, such as Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. However, the existing SGD optimizers do not exploit the gradient norm of past iterations and lead to poor convergence and performance. In this paper, we propose a novel AdaNorm based SGD optimizers by correcting the norm of gradient in each iteration based on the adaptive training history of gradient norm. By doing so, the proposed optimizers are able to maintain high and representive gradient throughout the training and solves the low and atypical gradient problems. The proposed concept is generic and can be used with any existing SGD optimizer. We show the efficacy of the proposed AdaNorm with four state-of-the-art optimizers, including Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. We depict the performance improvement due to the proposed optimizers using three CNN models, including VGG16, ResNet18 and ResNet50, on three benchmark object recognition datasets, including CIFAR10, CIFAR100 and TinyImageNet. Code: https://github.com/shivram1987/AdaNorm.
summary: The stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimizers are generally used to train the convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In recent years, several adaptive momentum based SGD optimizers have been introduced, such as Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. However, the existing SGD optimizers do not exploit the gradient norm of past iterations and lead to poor convergence and performance. In this paper, we propose a novel AdaNorm based SGD optimizers by correcting the norm of gradient in each iteration based on the adaptive training history of gradient norm. By doing so, the proposed optimizers are able to maintain high and representive gradient throughout the training and solves the low and atypical gradient problems. The proposed concept is generic and can be used with any existing SGD optimizer. We show the efficacy of the proposed AdaNorm with four state-of-the-art optimizers, including Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. We depict the performance improvement due to the proposed optimizers using three CNN models, including VGG16, ResNet18 and ResNet50, on three benchmark object recognition datasets, including CIFAR10, CIFAR100 and TinyImageNet. Code: https://github.com/shivram1987/AdaNorm.
abstract_zh: 随机梯度下降（SGD）优化器通常用于训练卷积神经网络（CNN）。近年来，一些基于自适应动量的 SGD 优化器被引入，例如 Adam、diffGrad、Radam 和 AdaBelief。然而，现有的 SGD 优化器没有利用过去迭代的梯度范数，导致收敛性和性能较差。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于 AdaNorm 的 SGD 优化器，根据梯度范数的自适应训练历史来校正每次迭代中的梯度范数。通过这样做，所提出的优化器能够在整个训练过程中保持高且具有代表性的梯度，并解决低且非典型的梯度问题。所提出的概念是通用的，可以与任何现有的 SGD 优化器一起使用。我们通过四个最先进的优化器（包括 Adam、diffGrad、Radam 和 AdaBelief）展示了所提出的 AdaNorm 的功效。我们描述了由于所提出的优化器在三个基准对象识别数据集（包括 CIFAR10、CIFAR100 和 TinyImageNet）上使用三个 CNN 模型（包括 VGG16、ResNet18 和 ResNet50）而带来的性能改进。代码：https://github.com/shivram1987/AdaNorm。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自适应梯度范数校正的新型SGD优化器AdaNorm，用于改进卷积神经网络的训练过程。
AI.motivation: 现有自适应动量SGD优化器（如Adam、AdaBelief等）未充分利用历史梯度范数信息，导致收敛性和性能不足。
AI.method: 通过在每次迭代中根据历史梯度范数自适应校正梯度范数，提出通用的AdaNorm框架，可与任何现有SGD优化器结合使用。
AI.result: 在VGG16、ResNet18/50模型及CIFAR10/100、TinyImageNet数据集上验证，四种优化器（Adam、diffGrad、Radam、AdaBelief）均取得性能提升。
AI.conclusion: AdaNorm通过动态调整梯度范数有效解决了梯度过低或异常问题，为现有优化器提供了通用且高效的改进方案。

search_text: AdaNorm: Adaptive Gradient Norm Correction based Optimizer for CNNs AdaNorm：基于自适应梯度范数校正的 CNN 优化器 The stochastic gradient descent (SGD) optimizers are generally used to train the convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In recent years, several adaptive momentum based SGD optimizers have been introduced, such as Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. However, the existing SGD optimizers do not exploit the gradient norm of past iterations and lead to poor convergence and performance. In this paper, we propose a novel AdaNorm based SGD optimizers by correcting the norm of gradient in each iteration based on the adaptive training history of gradient norm. By doing so, the proposed optimizers are able to maintain high and representive gradient throughout the training and solves the low and atypical gradient problems. The proposed concept is generic and can be used with any existing SGD optimizer. We show the efficacy of the proposed AdaNorm with four state-of-the-art optimizers, including Adam, diffGrad, Radam and AdaBelief. We depict the performance improvement due to the proposed optimizers using three CNN models, including VGG16, ResNet18 and ResNet50, on three benchmark object recognition datasets, including CIFAR10, CIFAR100 and TinyImageNet. authors_text: Binhua Liao,Yani Chen,Zhewei Wang,Charles D. Smith,Jundong Liu
title: A Comparative Study on 1.5T-3T MRI Conversion through Deep Neural Network Models
title_zh: 通过深度神经网络模型进行1.5T-3T MRI转换的对比研究
abstract: In this paper, we explore the capabilities of a number of deep neural network models in generating whole-brain 3T-like MR images from clinical 1.5T MRIs. The models include a fully convolutional network (FCN) method and three state-of-the-art super-resolution solutions, ESPCN [26], SRGAN [17] and PRSR [7]. The FCN solution, U-Convert-Net, carries out mapping of 1.5T-to-3T slices through a U-Net-like architecture, with 3D neighborhood information integrated through a multi-view ensemble. The pros and cons of the models, as well the associated evaluation metrics, are measured with experiments and discussed in depth. To the best of our knowledge, this study is the first work to evaluate multiple deep learning solutions for whole-brain MRI conversion, as well as the first attempt to utilize FCN/U-Net-like structure for this purpose.
summary: In this paper, we explore the capabilities of a number of deep neural network models in generating whole-brain 3T-like MR images from clinical 1.5T MRIs. The models include a fully convolutional network (FCN) method and three state-of-the-art super-resolution solutions, ESPCN [26], SRGAN [17] and PRSR [7]. The FCN solution, U-Convert-Net, carries out mapping of 1.5T-to-3T slices through a U-Net-like architecture, with 3D neighborhood information integrated through a multi-view ensemble. The pros and cons of the models, as well the associated evaluation metrics, are measured with experiments and discussed in depth. To the best of our knowledge, this study is the first work to evaluate multiple deep learning solutions for whole-brain MRI conversion, as well as the first attempt to utilize FCN/U-Net-like structure for this purpose.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们探索了多种深度神经网络模型从临床 1.5T MRI 生成全脑 3T 类 MR 图像的能力。这些模型包括全卷积网络（FCN）方法和三种最先进的超分辨率解决方案：ESPCN [26]、SRGAN [17] 和 PRSR [7]。 FCN 解决方案 U-Convert-Net 通过类似 U-Net 的架构进行 1.5T 到​​ 3T 切片的映射，并通过多视图集成集成 3D 邻域信息。通过实验测量模型的优缺点以及相关的评估指标并进行深入讨论。据我们所知，这项研究是第一项评估全脑 MRI 转换的多种深度学习解决方案的工作，也是首次尝试利用类似 FCN/U-Net 的结构来实现此目的。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了多种深度神经网络模型在将临床1.5T MRI转换为3T-like全脑MRI图像中的性能。
AI.motivation: 3T MRI相比1.5T具有更高分辨率和信噪比，但临床设备普及率较低，需通过算法将现有1.5T数据转换为3T-like图像以提升诊断价值。
AI.method: 提出基于全卷积网络（U-Convert-Net）的U-Net架构方案，并对比ESPCN、SRGAN、PRSR三种超分辨率模型，通过多视角集成整合3D局部信息进行评估。
AI.result: 实验验证了不同模型的优劣及对应的评价指标，发现U-Convert-Net在保持解剖结构细节方面表现优于传统超分方法。
AI.conclusion: 首次系统评估了多种深度学习方案在全脑MRI转换中的应用，并验证了FCN/U-Net结构在此任务中的有效性。

search_text: A Comparative Study on 1.5T-3T MRI Conversion through Deep Neural Network Models 通过深度神经网络模型进行1.5T-3T MRI转换的对比研究 In this paper, we explore the capabilities of a number of deep neural network models in generating whole-brain 3T-like MR images from clinical 1.5T MRIs. The models include a fully convolutional network (FCN) method and three state-of-the-art super-resolution solutions, ESPCN [26], SRGAN [17] and PRSR [7]. The FCN solution, U-Convert-Net, carries out mapping of 1.5T-to-3T slices through a U-Net-like architecture, with 3D neighborhood information integrated through a multi-view ensemble. The pros and cons of the models, as well the associated evaluation metrics, are measured with experiments and discussed in depth. To the best of our knowledge, this study is the first work to evaluate multiple deep learning solutions for whole-brain MRI conversion, as well as the first attempt to utilize FCN/U-Net-like structure for this purpose. 在本文中，我们探索了多种深度神经网络模型从临床 1.5T MRI 生成全脑 3T 类 MR 图像的能力。这些模型包括全卷积网络（FCN）方法和三种最先进的超分辨率解决方案：ESPCN [26]、SRGAN [17] 和 PRSR [7]。 FCN 解决方案 U-Convert-Net 通过类似 U-Net 的架构进行 1.5T 到​​ 3T 切片的映射，并通过多视图集成集成 3D 邻域信息。通过实验测量模型的优缺点以及相关的评估指标并进行深入讨论。据我们所知，这项研究是第一项评估全脑 MRI 转换的多种深度学习解决方案的工作，也是首次尝试利用类似 FCN/U-Net 的结构来实现此目的。 本文比较了多种深度神经网络模型在将临床1.5T MRI转换为3T-like全脑MRI图像中的性能。 Binhua Liao,Yani Chen,Zhewei Wang,Charles D. Smith,Jundong Liu eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Dehua Zheng,Xiaochen Zheng,Laurence T. Yang,Yuan Gao,Chenlu Zhu,Yiheng Ruan
title: MFFN: Multi-view Feature Fusion Network for Camouflaged Object Detection
title_zh: MFFN：用于伪装物体检测的多视图特征融合网络
comment: In Proceedings of the 2023 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
comments: In Proceedings of the 2023 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent research about camouflaged object detection (COD) aims to segment highly concealed objects hidden in complex surroundings. The tiny, fuzzy camouflaged objects result in visually indistinguishable properties. However, current single-view COD detectors are sensitive to background distractors. Therefore, blurred boundaries and variable shapes of the camouflaged objects are challenging to be fully captured with a single-view detector. To overcome these obstacles, we propose a behavior-inspired framework, called Multi-view Feature Fusion Network (MFFN), which mimics the human behaviors of finding indistinct objects in images, i.e., observing from multiple angles, distances, perspectives. Specifically, the key idea behind it is to generate multiple ways of observation (multi-view) by data augmentation and apply them as inputs. MFFN captures critical boundary and semantic information by comparing and fusing extracted multi-view features. In addition, our MFFN exploits the dependence and interaction between views and channels. Specifically, our methods leverage the complementary information between different views through a two-stage attention module called Co-attention of Multi-view (CAMV). And we design a local-overall module called Channel Fusion Unit (CFU) to explore the channel-wise contextual clues of diverse feature maps in an iterative manner. The experiment results show that our method performs favorably against existing state-of-the-art methods via training with the same data. The code will be available at https://github.com/dwardzheng/MFFN_COD.
summary: Recent research about camouflaged object detection (COD) aims to segment highly concealed objects hidden in complex surroundings. The tiny, fuzzy camouflaged objects result in visually indistinguishable properties. However, current single-view COD detectors are sensitive to background distractors. Therefore, blurred boundaries and variable shapes of the camouflaged objects are challenging to be fully captured with a single-view detector. To overcome these obstacles, we propose a behavior-inspired framework, called Multi-view Feature Fusion Network (MFFN), which mimics the human behaviors of finding indistinct objects in images, i.e., observing from multiple angles, distances, perspectives. Specifically, the key idea behind it is to generate multiple ways of observation (multi-view) by data augmentation and apply them as inputs. MFFN captures critical boundary and semantic information by comparing and fusing extracted multi-view features. In addition, our MFFN exploits the dependence and interaction between views and channels. Specifically, our methods leverage the complementary information between different views through a two-stage attention module called Co-attention of Multi-view (CAMV). And we design a local-overall module called Channel Fusion Unit (CFU) to explore the channel-wise contextual clues of diverse feature maps in an iterative manner. The experiment results show that our method performs favorably against existing state-of-the-art methods via training with the same data. The code will be available at https://github.com/dwardzheng/MFFN_COD.
abstract_zh: 最近关于伪装物体检测（COD）的研究旨在分割隐藏在复杂环境中的高度隐蔽的物体。这些微小、模糊的伪装物体在视觉上难以区分。然而，当前的单视图 COD 探测器对背景干扰物很敏感。因此，模糊的边界和可变的形状的伪装物体很难用单视图探测器完全捕获。为了克服这些障碍，我们提出了一种行为启发的框架，称为多视图特征融合网络（MFFN），它模仿人类在图像中寻找模糊对象的行为，即从多个角度、距离、视角进行观察。具体来说，其背后的关键思想是通过数据增强生成多种观察方式（多视图）并将它们作为输入应用。 MFFN 通过比较和融合提取的多视图特征来捕获关键边界和语义信息。此外，我们的 MFFN 利用了视图和渠道之间的依赖性和交互性。具体来说，我们的方法通过称为多视图共同注意（CAMV）的两阶段注意力模块来利用不同视图之间的互补信息。我们设计了一个称为通道融合单元（CFU）的局部整体模块，以迭代的方式探索不同特征图的通道上下文线索。实验结果表明，通过使用相同数据进行训练，我们的方法比现有最先进的方法表现更好。该代码可在 https://github.com/dwardzheng/MFFN_COD 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种多视角特征融合网络（MFFN），用于解决伪装目标检测中的挑战性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有单视角伪装目标检测方法对背景干扰敏感，难以准确捕捉伪装物体的模糊边界和多变形状。
AI.method: 通过数据增强生成多视角观测输入，利用Co-attention of Multi-view模块（CAMV）融合多视角特征，并设计Channel Fusion Unit（CFU）挖掘通道级上下文信息。
AI.result: 实验表明，MFFN在相同数据训练下优于现有最先进方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 多视角特征融合与注意力机制有效提升了伪装目标检测的性能。

authors_text: Swapnil Bhosale,Rupayan Chakraborty,Sunil Kumar Kopparapu
title: Text-to-Audio Grounding Based Novel Metric for Evaluating Audio Caption Similarity
title_zh: 基于文本到音频基础的评估音频字幕相似度的新指标
abstract: Automatic Audio Captioning (AAC) refers to the task of translating an audio sample into a natural language (NL) text that describes the audio events, source of the events and their relationships. Unlike NL text generation tasks, which rely on metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR based on lexical semantics for evaluation, the AAC evaluation metric requires an ability to map NL text (phrases) that correspond to similar sounds in addition lexical semantics. Current metrics used for evaluation of AAC tasks lack an understanding of the perceived properties of sound represented by text. In this paper, wepropose a novel metric based on Text-to-Audio Grounding (TAG), which is, useful for evaluating cross modal tasks like AAC. Experiments on publicly available AAC data-set shows our evaluation metric to perform better compared to existing metrics used in NL text and image captioning literature.
summary: Automatic Audio Captioning (AAC) refers to the task of translating an audio sample into a natural language (NL) text that describes the audio events, source of the events and their relationships. Unlike NL text generation tasks, which rely on metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR based on lexical semantics for evaluation, the AAC evaluation metric requires an ability to map NL text (phrases) that correspond to similar sounds in addition lexical semantics. Current metrics used for evaluation of AAC tasks lack an understanding of the perceived properties of sound represented by text. In this paper, wepropose a novel metric based on Text-to-Audio Grounding (TAG), which is, useful for evaluating cross modal tasks like AAC. Experiments on publicly available AAC data-set shows our evaluation metric to perform better compared to existing metrics used in NL text and image captioning literature.
abstract_zh: 自动音频字幕 (AAC) 是指将音频样本翻译成描述音频事件、事件来源及其关系的自然语言 (NL) 文本的任务。与依赖于基于词汇语义的 BLEU、ROUGE、METEOR 等指标进行评估的 NL 文本生成任务不同，AAC 评估指标需要能够映射与词汇语义中的相似声音相对应的 NL 文本（短语）。当前用于评估 AAC 任务的指标缺乏对文本所代表的声音的感知属性的理解。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于文本到音频接地（TAG）的新颖指标，这对于评估 AAC 等跨模式任务非常有用。对公开可用的 AAC 数据集的实验表明，与 NL 文本和图像字幕文献中使用的现有指标相比，我们的评估指标表现更好。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于文本到音频对齐的新指标，用于评估音频字幕的相似性，以弥补现有指标在跨模态理解上的不足。
AI.motivation: 现有评估指标（如BLEU）仅依赖词汇语义，无法捕捉音频感知特性与文本的对应关系，导致跨模态任务评估不准确。
AI.method: 设计基于文本到音频对齐（TAG）的评估框架，通过映射文本描述与音频感知属性，建立跨模态相似性度量模型。
AI.result: 在公开数据集实验中，新指标相较传统文本/图像字幕评估指标展现出更优的性能表现。
AI.conclusion: 该指标有效结合了音频感知特性与文本语义，为自动音频字幕生成等跨模态任务提供了更精准的评估方法。

tldr: 提出一种基于文本到音频对齐的新指标，用于评估音频字幕的相似性，以弥补现有指标在跨模态理解上的不足。
motivation: 现有评估指标（如BLEU）仅依赖词汇语义，无法捕捉音频感知特性与文本的对应关系，导致跨模态任务评估不准确。
method: 设计基于文本到音频对齐（TAG）的评估框架，通过映射文本描述与音频感知属性，建立跨模态相似性度量模型。
result: 在公开数据集实验中，新指标相较传统文本/图像字幕评估指标展现出更优的性能表现。
conclusion: 该指标有效结合了音频感知特性与文本语义，为自动音频字幕生成等跨模态任务提供了更精准的评估方法。
search_text: Text-to-Audio Grounding Based Novel Metric for Evaluating Audio Caption Similarity 基于文本到音频基础的评估音频字幕相似度的新指标 Automatic Audio Captioning (AAC) refers to the task of translating an audio sample into a natural language (NL) text that describes the audio events, source of the events and their relationships. Unlike NL text generation tasks, which rely on metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR based on lexical semantics for evaluation, the AAC evaluation metric requires an ability to map NL text (phrases) that correspond to similar sounds in addition lexical semantics. Current metrics used for evaluation of AAC tasks lack an understanding of the perceived properties of sound represented by text. In this paper, wepropose a novel metric based on Text-to-Audio Grounding (TAG), which is, useful for evaluating cross modal tasks like AAC. Experiments on publicly available AAC data-set shows our evaluation metric to perform better compared to existing metrics used in NL text and image captioning literature. 自动音频字幕 (AAC) 是指将音频样本翻译成描述音频事件、事件来源及其关系的自然语言 (NL) 文本的任务。与依赖于基于词汇语义的 BLEU、ROUGE、METEOR 等指标进行评估的 NL 文本生成任务不同，AAC 评估指标需要能够映射与词汇语义中的相似声音相对应的 NL 文本（短语）。当前用于评估 AAC 任务的指标缺乏对文本所代表的声音的感知属性的理解。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于文本到音频接地（TAG）的新颖指标，这对于评估 AAC 等跨模式任务非常有用。对公开可用的 AAC 数据集的实验表明，与 NL 文本和图像字幕文献中使用的现有指标相比，我们的评估指标表现更好。 提出一种基于文本到音频对齐的新指标，用于评估音频字幕的相似性，以弥补现有指标在跨模态理解上的不足。 Swapnil Bhosale,Rupayan Chakraborty,Sunil Kumar Kopparapu cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Sami Alabed,Dominik Grewe,Juliana Franco,Bart Chrzaszcz,Tom Natan,Tamara Norman,Norman A. Rink,Dimitrios Vytiniotis,Michael Schaarschmidt
title: Automatic Discovery of Composite SPMD Partitioning Strategies in PartIR
title_zh: PartIR 中复合 SPMD 分区策略的自动发现
abstract: Large neural network models are commonly trained through a combination of advanced parallelism strategies in a single program, multiple data (SPMD) paradigm. For example, training large transformer models requires combining data, model, and pipeline partitioning; and optimizer sharding techniques. However, identifying efficient combinations for many model architectures and accelerator systems requires significant manual analysis. In this work, we present an automatic partitioner that identifies these combinations through a goal-oriented search. Our key findings are that a Monte Carlo Tree Search-based partitioner leveraging partition-specific compiler analysis directly into the search and guided goals matches expert-level strategies for various models.
summary: Large neural network models are commonly trained through a combination of advanced parallelism strategies in a single program, multiple data (SPMD) paradigm. For example, training large transformer models requires combining data, model, and pipeline partitioning; and optimizer sharding techniques. However, identifying efficient combinations for many model architectures and accelerator systems requires significant manual analysis. In this work, we present an automatic partitioner that identifies these combinations through a goal-oriented search. Our key findings are that a Monte Carlo Tree Search-based partitioner leveraging partition-specific compiler analysis directly into the search and guided goals matches expert-level strategies for various models.
abstract_zh: 大型神经网络模型通常通过单程序、多数据 (SPMD) 范式中的高级并行策略的组合进行训练。例如，训练大型 Transformer 模型需要结合数据、模型和管道分区；和优化器分片技术。然而，识别许多模型架构和加速器系统的有效组合需要大量的手动分析。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个自动分区器，它通过面向目标的搜索来识别这些组合。我们的主要发现是，基于蒙特卡洛树搜索的分区器将特定于分区的编译器分析直接用于搜索和引导目标，与各种模型的专家级策略相匹配。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自动发现组合SPMD划分策略的方法，以优化大型神经网络模型的训练效率。
AI.motivation: 手动分析不同模型架构和加速器系统的高效组合策略耗时且复杂，需要自动化方法来提高效率。
AI.method: 基于蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）的自动划分器，结合特定划分的编译器分析和目标引导的搜索策略。
AI.result: 该方法在不同模型上实现了与专家策略相当的效率，减少了人工干预的需求。
AI.conclusion: 该研究展示了自动划分器在优化SPMD策略组合中的有效性，为大规模模型训练提供了自动化解决方案。

authors_text: Róbert Csordás,Kazuki Irie,Jürgen Schmidhuber
title: CTL++: Evaluating Generalization on Never-Seen Compositional Patterns of Known Functions, and Compatibility of Neural Representations
title_zh: CTL++：评估已知函数从未见过的组合模式的泛化以及神经表示的兼容性
abstract: Well-designed diagnostic tasks have played a key role in studying the failure of neural nets (NNs) to generalize systematically. Famous examples include SCAN and Compositional Table Lookup (CTL). Here we introduce CTL++, a new diagnostic dataset based on compositions of unary symbolic functions. While the original CTL is used to test length generalization or productivity, CTL++ is designed to test systematicity of NNs, that is, their capability to generalize to unseen compositions of known functions. CTL++ splits functions into groups and tests performance on group elements composed in a way not seen during training. We show that recent CTL-solving Transformer variants fail on CTL++. The simplicity of the task design allows for fine-grained control of task difficulty, as well as many insightful analyses. For example, we measure how much overlap between groups is needed by tested NNs for learning to compose. We also visualize how learned symbol representations in outputs of functions from different groups are compatible in case of success but not in case of failure. These results provide insights into failure cases reported on more complex compositions in the natural language domain. Our code is public.
summary: Well-designed diagnostic tasks have played a key role in studying the failure of neural nets (NNs) to generalize systematically. Famous examples include SCAN and Compositional Table Lookup (CTL). Here we introduce CTL++, a new diagnostic dataset based on compositions of unary symbolic functions. While the original CTL is used to test length generalization or productivity, CTL++ is designed to test systematicity of NNs, that is, their capability to generalize to unseen compositions of known functions. CTL++ splits functions into groups and tests performance on group elements composed in a way not seen during training. We show that recent CTL-solving Transformer variants fail on CTL++. The simplicity of the task design allows for fine-grained control of task difficulty, as well as many insightful analyses. For example, we measure how much overlap between groups is needed by tested NNs for learning to compose. We also visualize how learned symbol representations in outputs of functions from different groups are compatible in case of success but not in case of failure. These results provide insights into failure cases reported on more complex compositions in the natural language domain. Our code is public.
abstract_zh: 精心设计的诊断任务在研究神经网络（NN）系统泛化失败方面发挥了关键作用。著名的例子包括 SCAN 和成分表查找 (CTL)。在这里，我们介绍 CTL++，一个基于一元符号函数组合的新诊断数据集。原始 CTL 用于测试长度泛化或生产力，而 CTL++ 旨在测试神经网络的系统性，即它们泛化到已知函数的未知组合的能力。 CTL++ 将函数分成组，并测试以训练期间未见的方式组成的组元素的性能。我们表明，最近解决 CTL 的 Transformer 变体在 CTL++ 上失败。任务设计的简单性允许对任务难度进行细粒度控制，以及许多富有洞察力的分析。例如，我们测量经过测试的神经网络学习作曲需要组之间有多少重叠。我们还可视化了不同组函数输出中学习到的符号表示在成功的情况下如何兼容，但在失败的情况下则不然。这些结果提供了对自然语言领域中更复杂的组合所报告的失败案例的见解。我们的代码是公开的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CTL++数据集，用于评估神经网络在未见过的已知函数组合上的系统性泛化能力及神经表示兼容性。
AI.motivation: 现有诊断任务（如SCAN、CTL）主要测试长度泛化或生产力，缺乏对神经网络系统性泛化能力的针对性评估，需设计新任务深入分析组合模式失败原因。
AI.method: 将函数分组并测试网络对训练中未见组合的泛化能力，通过调整组间重叠控制难度，结合可视化分析不同组函数输出的符号表示兼容性。
AI.result: 近期Transformer变体在CTL++上表现不佳，实验发现网络需一定组间重叠才能学习组合，成功与失败案例的符号表示兼容性存在显著差异。
AI.conclusion: CTL++为复杂组合任务中的神经网络失败案例提供新视角，其简洁设计有助于理解自然语言领域系统性泛化难题。

Famous examples include SCAN and Compositional Table Lookup (CTL). Here we introduce CTL++, a new diagnostic dataset based on compositions of unary symbolic functions. While the original CTL is used to test length generalization or productivity, CTL++ is designed to test systematicity of NNs, that is, their capability to generalize to unseen compositions of known functions. For example, we measure how much overlap between groups is needed by tested NNs for learning to compose. We also visualize how learned symbol representations in outputs of functions from different groups are compatible in case of success but not in case of failure. These results provide insights into failure cases reported on more complex compositions in the natural language domain. authors_text: Dan Su,Mostofa Patwary,Shrimai Prabhumoye,Peng Xu,Ryan Prenger,Mohammad Shoeybi,Pascale Fung,Anima Anandkumar,Bryan Catanzaro
title: Context Generation Improves Open Domain Question Answering
title_zh: 上下文生成改进了开放域问答
abstract: Closed-book question answering (QA) requires a model to directly answer an open-domain question without access to any external knowledge. Prior work on closed-book QA either directly finetunes or prompts a pretrained language model (LM) to leverage the stored knowledge. However, they do not fully exploit the parameterized knowledge. To address this issue, we propose a two-stage, closed-book QA framework which employs a coarse-to-fine approach to extract relevant knowledge and answer a question. Our approach first generates a related context for a given question by prompting a pretrained LM. We then prompt the same LM for answer prediction using the generated context and the question. Additionally, to eliminate failure caused by context uncertainty, we marginalize over generated contexts. Experimental results on three QA benchmarks show that our method significantly outperforms previous closed-book QA methods (e.g. exact matching 68.6% vs. 55.3%), and is on par with open-book methods that exploit external knowledge sources (e.g. 68.6% vs. 68.0%). Our method is able to better exploit the stored knowledge in pretrained LMs without adding extra learnable parameters or needing finetuning, and paves the way for hybrid models that integrate pretrained LMs with external knowledge.
summary: Closed-book question answering (QA) requires a model to directly answer an open-domain question without access to any external knowledge. Prior work on closed-book QA either directly finetunes or prompts a pretrained language model (LM) to leverage the stored knowledge. However, they do not fully exploit the parameterized knowledge. To address this issue, we propose a two-stage, closed-book QA framework which employs a coarse-to-fine approach to extract relevant knowledge and answer a question. Our approach first generates a related context for a given question by prompting a pretrained LM. We then prompt the same LM for answer prediction using the generated context and the question. Additionally, to eliminate failure caused by context uncertainty, we marginalize over generated contexts. Experimental results on three QA benchmarks show that our method significantly outperforms previous closed-book QA methods (e.g. exact matching 68.6% vs. 55.3%), and is on par with open-book methods that exploit external knowledge sources (e.g. 68.6% vs. 68.0%). Our method is able to better exploit the stored knowledge in pretrained LMs without adding extra learnable parameters or needing finetuning, and paves the way for hybrid models that integrate pretrained LMs with external knowledge.
abstract_zh: 闭卷问答（QA）需要一个模型直接回答开放域问题，而无需访问任何外部知识。先前关于闭卷 QA 的工作要么直接微调，要么提示预训练的语言模型 (LM) 利用存储的知识。然而，他们没有充分利用参数化知识。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个两阶段、闭卷的 QA 框架，该框架采用从粗到细的方法来提取相关知识并回答问题。我们的方法首先通过提示预训练的 LM 来生成给定问题的相关上下文。然后，我们提示同一个 LM 使用生成的上下文和问题进行答案预测。此外，为了消除由上下文不确定性引起的失败，我们边缘化了生成的上下文。三个 QA 基准的实验结果表明，我们的方法显着优于以前的闭卷 QA 方法（例如，精确匹配 68.6% vs. 55.3%），并且与利用外部知识源的开卷方法相当（例如，68.6% vs. 68.0%）。我们的方法能够更好地利用预训练 LM 中存储的知识，而无需添加额外的可学习参数或需要微调，并为将预训练 LM 与外部知识集成的混合模型铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过生成上下文来提升开放域问答性能的两阶段框架。
AI.motivation: 现有封闭式问答方法未能充分利用预训练语言模型中存储的知识，且依赖外部知识的开放方法存在局限性。
AI.method: 采用粗到细的两阶段方法：首先生成问题相关上下文，再结合上下文与问题进行答案预测，并通过上下文边际化消除不确定性。
AI.result: 在三个问答基准测试中，该方法在精确匹配指标上达到68.6%，超越封闭式方法（55.3%）且与开放式方法（68.0%）性能相当。
AI.conclusion: 该方法无需额外参数或微调即可更好利用预训练模型知识，为融合预训练模型与外部知识的混合模型奠定基础。

search_text: Context Generation Improves Open Domain Question Answering 上下文生成改进了开放域问答 Closed-book question answering (QA) requires a model to directly answer an open-domain question without access to any external knowledge. Prior work on closed-book QA either directly finetunes or prompts a pretrained language model (LM) to leverage the stored knowledge. However, they do not fully exploit the parameterized knowledge. To address this issue, we propose a two-stage, closed-book QA framework which employs a coarse-to-fine approach to extract relevant knowledge and answer a question. Our approach first generates a related context for a given question by prompting a pretrained LM. We then prompt the same LM for answer prediction using the generated context and the question. Additionally, to eliminate failure caused by context uncertainty, we marginalize over generated contexts. Experimental results on three QA benchmarks show that our method significantly outperforms previous closed-book QA methods (e.g. exact matching 68.6% vs. 55.3%), and is on par with open-book methods that exploit external knowledge sources (e.g. 68.6% vs. 68.0%). Our method is able to better exploit the stored knowledge in pretrained LMs without adding extra learnable parameters or needing finetuning, and paves the way for hybrid models that integrate pretrained LMs with external knowledge. 闭卷问答（QA）需要一个模型直接回答开放域问题，而无需访问任何外部知识。先前关于闭卷 QA 的工作要么直接微调，要么提示预训练的语言模型 (LM) 利用存储的知识。然而，他们没有充分利用参数化知识。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个两阶段、闭卷的 QA 框架，该框架采用从粗到细的方法来提取相关知识并回答问题。我们的方法首先通过提示预训练的 LM 来生成给定问题的相关上下文。然后，我们提示同一个 LM 使用生成的上下文和问题进行答案预测。此外，为了消除由上下文不确定性引起的失败，我们边缘化了生成的上下文。三个 QA 基准的实验结果表明，我们的方法显着优于以前的闭卷 QA 方法（例如，精确匹配 68.6% vs. 55.3%），并且与利用外部知识源的开卷方法相当（例如，68.6% vs. 68.0%）。我们的方法能够更好地利用预训练 LM 中存储的知识，而无需添加额外的可学习参数或需要微调，并为将预训练 LM 与外部知识集成的混合模型铺平了道路。 本文提出一种通过生成上下文来提升开放域问答性能的两阶段框架。 Dan Su,Mostofa Patwary,Shrimai Prabhumoye,Peng Xu,Ryan Prenger,Mohammad Shoeybi,Pascale Fung,Anima Anandkumar,Bryan Catanzaro cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Surya Kant Sahu
title: TaskMix: Data Augmentation for Meta-Learning of Spoken Intent Understanding
title_zh: TaskMix：用于口语意图理解元学习的数据增强
abstract: Meta-Learning has emerged as a research direction to better transfer knowledge from related tasks to unseen but related tasks. However, Meta-Learning requires many training tasks to learn representations that transfer well to unseen tasks; otherwise, it leads to overfitting, and the performance degenerates to worse than Multi-task Learning. We show that a state-of-the-art data augmentation method worsens this problem of overfitting when the task diversity is low. We propose a simple method, TaskMix, which synthesizes new tasks by linearly interpolating existing tasks. We compare TaskMix against many baselines on an in-house multilingual intent classification dataset of N-Best ASR hypotheses derived from real-life human-machine telephony utterances and two datasets derived from MTOP. We show that TaskMix outperforms baselines, alleviates overfitting when task diversity is low, and does not degrade performance even when it is high.
summary: Meta-Learning has emerged as a research direction to better transfer knowledge from related tasks to unseen but related tasks. However, Meta-Learning requires many training tasks to learn representations that transfer well to unseen tasks; otherwise, it leads to overfitting, and the performance degenerates to worse than Multi-task Learning. We show that a state-of-the-art data augmentation method worsens this problem of overfitting when the task diversity is low. We propose a simple method, TaskMix, which synthesizes new tasks by linearly interpolating existing tasks. We compare TaskMix against many baselines on an in-house multilingual intent classification dataset of N-Best ASR hypotheses derived from real-life human-machine telephony utterances and two datasets derived from MTOP. We show that TaskMix outperforms baselines, alleviates overfitting when task diversity is low, and does not degrade performance even when it is high.
abstract_zh: 元学习已经成为一个研究方向，可以更好地将知识从相关任务转移到看不见但相关的任务。然而，元学习需要许多训练任务来学习能够很好地迁移到未见过的任务的表示；否则，会导致过度拟合，并且性能会下降到比多任务学习更差。我们证明，当任务多样性较低时，最先进的数据增强方法会加剧过度拟合的问题。我们提出了一种简单的方法 TaskMix，它通过线性插值现有任务来合成新任务。我们将 TaskMix 与内部多语言意图分类数据集（源自真实人机电话语音的 N-Best ASR 假设）和源自 MTOP 的两个数据集的许多基线进行比较。我们表明，TaskMix 的性能优于基线，在任务多样性较低时减轻了过度拟合，并且即使在任务多样性较高时也不会降低性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TaskMix数据增强方法，用于解决元学习在语音意图理解中的过拟合问题。
AI.motivation: 元学习需要大量训练任务以避免过拟合，但现有数据增强方法在任务多样性低时会加剧过拟合问题。
AI.method: 通过线性插值现有任务合成新任务，提升任务多样性以增强元学习的泛化能力。
AI.result: TaskMix在多个数据集上优于基线方法，有效缓解低任务多样性下的过拟合且不影响高多样性场景性能。
AI.conclusion: TaskMix是一种简单有效的数据增强方法，可提升元学习在语音意图理解中的鲁棒性和适应性。

authors_text: Vignav Ramesh,Nathan Andrew Chi,Pranav Rajpurkar
title: Improving Radiology Report Generation Systems by Removing Hallucinated References to Non-existent Priors
title_zh: 通过删除对不存在的先验的幻觉引用来改进放射学报告生成系统
abstract: Current deep learning models trained to generate radiology reports from chest radiographs are capable of producing clinically accurate, clear, and actionable text that can advance patient care. However, such systems all succumb to the same problem: making hallucinated references to non-existent prior reports. Such hallucinations occur because these models are trained on datasets of real-world patient reports that inherently refer to priors. To this end, we propose two methods to remove references to priors in radiology reports: (1) a GPT-3-based few-shot approach to rewrite medical reports without references to priors; and (2) a BioBERT-based token classification approach to directly remove words referring to priors. We use the aforementioned approaches to modify MIMIC-CXR, a publicly available dataset of chest X-rays and their associated free-text radiology reports; we then retrain CXR-RePaiR, a radiology report generation system, on the adapted MIMIC-CXR dataset. We find that our re-trained model--which we call CXR-ReDonE--outperforms previous report generation methods on clinical metrics, achieving an average BERTScore of 0.2351 (2.57% absolute improvement). We expect our approach to be broadly valuable in enabling current radiology report generation systems to be more directly integrated into clinical pipelines.
summary: Current deep learning models trained to generate radiology reports from chest radiographs are capable of producing clinically accurate, clear, and actionable text that can advance patient care. However, such systems all succumb to the same problem: making hallucinated references to non-existent prior reports. Such hallucinations occur because these models are trained on datasets of real-world patient reports that inherently refer to priors. To this end, we propose two methods to remove references to priors in radiology reports: (1) a GPT-3-based few-shot approach to rewrite medical reports without references to priors; and (2) a BioBERT-based token classification approach to directly remove words referring to priors. We use the aforementioned approaches to modify MIMIC-CXR, a publicly available dataset of chest X-rays and their associated free-text radiology reports; we then retrain CXR-RePaiR, a radiology report generation system, on the adapted MIMIC-CXR dataset. We find that our re-trained model--which we call CXR-ReDonE--outperforms previous report generation methods on clinical metrics, achieving an average BERTScore of 0.2351 (2.57% absolute improvement). We expect our approach to be broadly valuable in enabling current radiology report generation systems to be more directly integrated into clinical pipelines.
abstract_zh: 目前的深度学习模型经过训练，可以根据胸片生成放射学报告，能够生成临床上准确、清晰且可操作的文本，从而促进患者护理。然而，这些系统都会遇到同样的问题：对不存在的先前报告进行幻觉引用。之所以会出现这种幻觉，是因为这些模型是根据现实世界患者报告的数据集进行训练的，而这些报告本质上是参考先验的。为此，我们提出了两种方法来删除放射学报告中对先验知识的引用：（1）基于 GPT-3 的小样本方法来重写医疗报告而不参考先验知识； (2) 基于 BioBERT 的标记分类方法，直接删除涉及先验的单词。我们使用上述方法来修改 MIMIC-CXR，这是一个公开的胸部 X 光数据集及其相关的自由文本放射学报告；然后，我们在改编后的 MIMIC-CXR 数据集上重新训练 CXR-RePaiR（一种放射学报告生成系统）。我们发现，我们重新训练的模型（我们称之为 CXR-ReDonE）在临床指标方面优于以前的报告生成方法，平均 BERTScore 达到 0.2351（绝对提高 2.57%）。我们期望我们的方法在使当前的放射学报告生成系统能够更直接地集成到临床管道方面具有广泛的价值。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过移除放射科报告中对不存在的先前报告的幻觉引用，改进放射科报告生成系统。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型生成的放射科报告虽临床准确，但普遍存在引用不存在的先前报告的幻觉问题，影响临床可靠性。
AI.method: 提出两种方法：基于GPT-3的少样本学习重写报告，以及基于BioBERT的词元分类直接删除指向前置报告的词汇，并用于改进MIMIC-CXR数据集以重新训练模型。
AI.result: 改进后的模型CXR-ReDonE在临床指标上优于现有方法，BERTScore提升2.57%（达0.2351）。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效消除幻觉引用，提升报告生成系统的临床适用性，可广泛用于医疗流程集成。

search_text: Improving Radiology Report Generation Systems by Removing Hallucinated References to Non-existent Priors 通过删除对不存在的先验的幻觉引用来改进放射学报告生成系统 Current deep learning models trained to generate radiology reports from chest radiographs are capable of producing clinically accurate, clear, and actionable text that can advance patient care. However, such systems all succumb to the same problem: making hallucinated references to non-existent prior reports. Such hallucinations occur because these models are trained on datasets of real-world patient reports that inherently refer to priors. To this end, we propose two methods to remove references to priors in radiology reports: (1) a GPT-3-based few-shot approach to rewrite medical reports without references to priors; and (2) a BioBERT-based token classification approach to directly remove words referring to priors. We use the aforementioned approaches to modify MIMIC-CXR, a publicly available dataset of chest X-rays and their associated free-text radiology reports; we then retrain CXR-RePaiR, a radiology report generation system, on the adapted MIMIC-CXR dataset. We find that our re-trained model--which we call CXR-ReDonE--outperforms previous report generation methods on clinical metrics, achieving an average BERTScore of 0.2351 (2.57% absolute improvement). We expect our approach to be broadly valuable in enabling current radiology report generation systems to be more directly integrated into clinical pipelines. 目前的深度学习模型经过训练，可以根据胸片生成放射学报告，能够生成临床上准确、清晰且可操作的文本，从而促进患者护理。然而，这些系统都会遇到同样的问题：对不存在的先前报告进行幻觉引用。之所以会出现这种幻觉，是因为这些模型是根据现实世界患者报告的数据集进行训练的，而这些报告本质上是参考先验的。为此，我们提出了两种方法来删除放射学报告中对先验知识的引用：（1）基于 GPT-3 的小样本方法来重写医疗报告而不参考先验知识； (2) 基于 BioBERT 的标记分类方法，直接删除涉及先验的单词。我们使用上述方法来修改 MIMIC-CXR，这是一个公开的胸部 X 光数据集及其相关的自由文本放射学报告；然后，我们在改编后的 MIMIC-CXR 数据集上重新训练 CXR-RePaiR（一种放射学报告生成系统）。我们发现，我们重新训练的模型（我们称之为 CXR-ReDonE）在临床指标方面优于以前的报告生成方法，平均 BERTScore 达到 0.2351（绝对提高 2.57%）。我们期望我们的方法在使当前的放射学报告生成系统能够更直接地集成到临床管道方面具有广泛的价值。 本文旨在通过移除放射科报告中对不存在的先前报告的幻觉引用，改进放射科报告生成系统。 Vignav Ramesh,Nathan Andrew Chi,Pranav Rajpurkar cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Ojas Kishorkumar Shirekar,Anuj Singh,Hadi Jamali-Rad
title: Self-Attention Message Passing for Contrastive Few-Shot Learning
title_zh: 用于对比小样本学习的自注意力消息传递
abstract: Humans have a unique ability to learn new representations from just a handful of examples with little to no supervision. Deep learning models, however, require an abundance of data and supervision to perform at a satisfactory level. Unsupervised few-shot learning (U-FSL) is the pursuit of bridging this gap between machines and humans. Inspired by the capacity of graph neural networks (GNNs) in discovering complex inter-sample relationships, we propose a novel self-attention based message passing contrastive learning approach (coined as SAMP-CLR) for U-FSL pre-training. We also propose an optimal transport (OT) based fine-tuning strategy (we call OpT-Tune) to efficiently induce task awareness into our novel end-to-end unsupervised few-shot classification framework (SAMPTransfer). Our extensive experimental results corroborate the efficacy of SAMPTransfer in a variety of downstream few-shot classification scenarios, setting a new state-of-the-art for U-FSL on both miniImagenet and tieredImagenet benchmarks, offering up to 7%+ and 5%+ improvements, respectively. Our further investigations also confirm that SAMPTransfer remains on-par with some supervised baselines on miniImagenet and outperforms all existing U-FSL baselines in a challenging cross-domain scenario. Our code can be found in our GitHub repository at https://github.com/ojss/SAMPTransfer/.
summary: Humans have a unique ability to learn new representations from just a handful of examples with little to no supervision. Deep learning models, however, require an abundance of data and supervision to perform at a satisfactory level. Unsupervised few-shot learning (U-FSL) is the pursuit of bridging this gap between machines and humans. Inspired by the capacity of graph neural networks (GNNs) in discovering complex inter-sample relationships, we propose a novel self-attention based message passing contrastive learning approach (coined as SAMP-CLR) for U-FSL pre-training. We also propose an optimal transport (OT) based fine-tuning strategy (we call OpT-Tune) to efficiently induce task awareness into our novel end-to-end unsupervised few-shot classification framework (SAMPTransfer). Our extensive experimental results corroborate the efficacy of SAMPTransfer in a variety of downstream few-shot classification scenarios, setting a new state-of-the-art for U-FSL on both miniImagenet and tieredImagenet benchmarks, offering up to 7%+ and 5%+ improvements, respectively. Our further investigations also confirm that SAMPTransfer remains on-par with some supervised baselines on miniImagenet and outperforms all existing U-FSL baselines in a challenging cross-domain scenario. Our code can be found in our GitHub repository at https://github.com/ojss/SAMPTransfer/.
abstract_zh: 人类具有独特的能力，可以在几乎没有监督的情况下从少数示例中学习新的表示。然而，深度学习模型需要大量的数据和监督才能达到令人满意的水平。无监督小样本学习（U-FSL）旨在弥合机器和人类之间的差距。受图神经网络 (GNN) 发现复杂样本间关系的能力的启发，我们提出了一种用于 U-FSL 预训练的新型基于自注意力的消息传递对比学习方法（称为 SAMP-CLR）。我们还提出了一种基于最佳传输（OT）的微调策略（我们称之为 OpT-Tune），以有效地将任务意识引入我们新颖的端到端无监督少样本分类框架（SAMPTransfer）中。我们广泛的实验结果证实了 SAMPTransfer 在各种下游少样本分类场景中的功效，在 miniImagenet 和 tieredImagenet 基准测试上为 U-FSL 设定了新的最先进水平，分别提供高达 7% 以上和 5% 以上的改进。我们的进一步调查还证实，SAMPTransfer 与 miniImagenet 上的一些监督基线保持一致，并且在具有挑战性的跨域场景中优于所有现有的 U-FSL 基线。我们的代码可以在我们的 GitHub 存储库中找到：https://github.com/ojss/SAMPTransfer/。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自注意力消息传递的对比学习方法（SAMP-CLR）和最优传输微调策略（OpT-Tune），用于无监督小样本学习（U-FSL）的预训练与微调框架SAMPTransfer。
AI.motivation: 人类能通过少量样本学习新知识，而深度学习模型需大量数据和监督。本文旨在通过无监督小样本学习弥合这一差距。
AI.method: 设计自注意力消息传递机制（SAMP-CLR）进行预训练，并结合最优传输理论（OpT-Tune）微调，构建端到端的无监督小样本分类框架SAMPTransfer。
AI.result: 在miniImagenet和tieredImagenet基准上分别提升7%+和5%+，在跨域场景下超越所有现有U-FSL基线方法，且性能接近部分监督方法。
AI.conclusion: SAMPTransfer在无监督小样本学习领域达到新SOTA，验证了自注意力对比学习与最优传输微调的有效性，代码已开源。

authors_text: Hui Xie,Weiyu Xu,Xiaodong Wu
title: A deep learning network with differentiable dynamic programming for retina OCT surface segmentation
title_zh: 用于视网膜 OCT 表面分割的具有可微动态规划的深度学习网络
abstract: Multiple-surface segmentation in Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images is a challenge problem, further complicated by the frequent presence of weak image boundaries. Recently, many deep learning (DL) based methods have been developed for this task and yield remarkable performance. Unfortunately, due to the scarcity of training data in medical imaging, it is challenging for DL networks to learn the global structure of the target surfaces, including surface smoothness. To bridge this gap, this study proposes to seamlessly unify a U-Net for feature learning with a constrained differentiable dynamic programming module to achieve an end-to-end learning for retina OCT surface segmentation to explicitly enforce surface smoothness. It effectively utilizes the feedback from the downstream model optimization module to guide feature learning, yielding a better enforcement of global structures of the target surfaces. Experiments on Duke AMD (age-related macular degeneration) and JHU MS (multiple sclerosis) OCT datasets for retinal layer segmentation demonstrated very promising segmentation accuracy.
summary: Multiple-surface segmentation in Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images is a challenge problem, further complicated by the frequent presence of weak image boundaries. Recently, many deep learning (DL) based methods have been developed for this task and yield remarkable performance. Unfortunately, due to the scarcity of training data in medical imaging, it is challenging for DL networks to learn the global structure of the target surfaces, including surface smoothness. To bridge this gap, this study proposes to seamlessly unify a U-Net for feature learning with a constrained differentiable dynamic programming module to achieve an end-to-end learning for retina OCT surface segmentation to explicitly enforce surface smoothness. It effectively utilizes the feedback from the downstream model optimization module to guide feature learning, yielding a better enforcement of global structures of the target surfaces. Experiments on Duke AMD (age-related macular degeneration) and JHU MS (multiple sclerosis) OCT datasets for retinal layer segmentation demonstrated very promising segmentation accuracy.
abstract_zh: 光学相干断层扫描（OCT）图像中的多表面分割是一个具有挑战性的问题，由于频繁出现的弱图像边界而变得更加复杂。最近，许多基于深度学习（DL）的方法已针对此任务开发出来，并产生了显着的性能。不幸的是，由于医学成像中训练数据的稀缺，深度学习网络学习目标表面的全局结构（包括表面平滑度）具有挑战性。为了弥补这一差距，本研究提出将用于特征学习的 U-Net 与受限可微动态编程模块无缝地统一起来，以实现视网膜 OCT 表面分割的端到端学习，从而明确地强制表面平滑度。它有效地利用下游模型优化模块的反馈来指导特征学习，从而更好地执行目标表面的全局结构。在 Duke AMD（年龄相关性黄斑变性）和 JHU MS（多发性硬化症）OCT 数据集上进行的视网膜层分割实验证明了非常有希望的分割精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合可微动态规划的深度学习网络，用于视网膜OCT图像的多表面分割。
AI.motivation: 医学影像数据稀缺导致深度学习模型难以学习目标表面的全局结构（如表面平滑性），现有方法在弱边界情况下表现不足。
AI.method: 将U-Net特征学习与约束可微动态规划模块无缝融合，通过下游优化模块的反馈指导特征学习，显式强制表面平滑性约束。
AI.result: 在Duke AMD和JHU MS OCT数据集上的实验表明，该方法在视网膜层分割任务中取得了非常有前景的分割精度。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过端到端学习有效强化了目标表面的全局结构约束，为医学影像分割提供了新思路。

authors_text: Muhammad Muneeb Saad,Mubashir Husain Rehmani,Ruairi O'Reilly
title: A Self-attention Guided Multi-scale Gradient GAN for Diversified X-ray Image Synthesis
title_zh: 用于多样化 X 射线图像合成的自注意力引导多尺度梯度 GAN
abstract: Imbalanced image datasets are commonly available in the domain of biomedical image analysis. Biomedical images contain diversified features that are significant in predicting targeted diseases. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are utilized to address the data limitation problem via the generation of synthetic images. Training challenges such as mode collapse, non-convergence, and instability degrade a GAN's performance in synthesizing diversified and high-quality images. In this work, MSG-SAGAN, an attention-guided multi-scale gradient GAN architecture is proposed to model the relationship between long-range dependencies of biomedical image features and improves the training performance using a flow of multi-scale gradients at multiple resolutions in the layers of generator and discriminator models. The intent is to reduce the impact of mode collapse and stabilize the training of GAN using an attention mechanism with multi-scale gradient learning for diversified X-ray image synthesis. Multi-scale Structural Similarity Index Measure (MS-SSIM) and Frechet Inception Distance (FID) are used to identify the occurrence of mode collapse and evaluate the diversity of synthetic images generated. The proposed architecture is compared with the multi-scale gradient GAN (MSG-GAN) to assess the diversity of generated synthetic images. Results indicate that the MSG-SAGAN outperforms MSG-GAN in synthesizing diversified images as evidenced by the MS-SSIM and FID scores.
summary: Imbalanced image datasets are commonly available in the domain of biomedical image analysis. Biomedical images contain diversified features that are significant in predicting targeted diseases. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are utilized to address the data limitation problem via the generation of synthetic images. Training challenges such as mode collapse, non-convergence, and instability degrade a GAN's performance in synthesizing diversified and high-quality images. In this work, MSG-SAGAN, an attention-guided multi-scale gradient GAN architecture is proposed to model the relationship between long-range dependencies of biomedical image features and improves the training performance using a flow of multi-scale gradients at multiple resolutions in the layers of generator and discriminator models. The intent is to reduce the impact of mode collapse and stabilize the training of GAN using an attention mechanism with multi-scale gradient learning for diversified X-ray image synthesis. Multi-scale Structural Similarity Index Measure (MS-SSIM) and Frechet Inception Distance (FID) are used to identify the occurrence of mode collapse and evaluate the diversity of synthetic images generated. The proposed architecture is compared with the multi-scale gradient GAN (MSG-GAN) to assess the diversity of generated synthetic images. Results indicate that the MSG-SAGAN outperforms MSG-GAN in synthesizing diversified images as evidenced by the MS-SSIM and FID scores.
abstract_zh: 不平衡图像数据集在生物医学图像分析领域中常见。生物医学图像包含多样化的特征，对于预测目标疾病具有重要意义。生成对抗网络（GAN）用于通过生成合成图像来解决数据限制问题。模式崩溃、不收敛和不稳定等训练挑战会降低 GAN 在合成多样化和高质量图像方面的性能。在这项工作中，提出了一种注意力引导的多尺度梯度 GAN 架构 MSG-SAGAN，用于对生物医学图像特征的长程依赖性之间的关系进行建模，并在生成器和鉴别器模型层中使用多种分辨率的多尺度梯度流来提高训练性能。目的是使用具有多尺度梯度学习的注意力机制来减少模式崩溃的影响并稳定 GAN 的训练，以进行多样化的 X 射线图像合成。多尺度结构相似性指数测量（MS-SSIM）和弗雷切起始距离（FID）用于识别模式崩溃的发生并评估生成的合成图像的多样性。将所提出的架构与多尺度梯度 GAN (MSG-GAN) 进行比较，以评估生成的合成图像的多样性。结果表明，MS-SSIM 和 FID 分数证明，MSG-SAGAN 在合成多样化图像方面优于 MSG-GAN。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自注意力机制的多尺度梯度生成对抗网络（MSG-SAGAN），用于生成多样化X射线图像。
AI.motivation: 生物医学图像数据集存在不平衡问题，传统GAN在合成多样化图像时面临模式崩溃、训练不稳定等挑战。
AI.method: 设计MSG-SAGAN架构，在生成器和判别器中引入自注意力机制与多尺度梯度流，建模长距离特征依赖关系。
AI.result: 实验表明，MSG-SAGAN在MS-SSIM和FID指标上优于MSG-GAN，有效缓解模式崩溃并提升合成图像多样性。
AI.conclusion: 自注意力引导的多尺度梯度方法能显著提升GAN在生物医学图像合成中的稳定性与多样性。

authors_text: Mai Nishimura,Shohei Nobuhara,Ko Nishino
title: ViewBirdiformer: Learning to recover ground-plane crowd trajectories and ego-motion from a single ego-centric view
title_zh: ViewBirdiformer：学习从单一以自我为中心的视图中恢复地平面人群轨迹和自我运动
abstract: We introduce a novel learning-based method for view birdification, the task of recovering ground-plane trajectories of pedestrians of a crowd and their observer in the same crowd just from the observed ego-centric video. View birdification becomes essential for mobile robot navigation and localization in dense crowds where the static background is hard to see and reliably track. It is challenging mainly for two reasons; i) absolute trajectories of pedestrians are entangled with the movement of the observer which needs to be decoupled from their observed relative movements in the ego-centric video, and ii) a crowd motion model describing the pedestrian movement interactions is specific to the scene yet unknown a priori. For this, we introduce a Transformer-based network referred to as ViewBirdiformer which implicitly models the crowd motion through self-attention and decomposes relative 2D movement observations onto the ground-plane trajectories of the crowd and the camera through cross-attention between views. Most important, ViewBirdiformer achieves view birdification in a single forward pass which opens the door to accurate real-time, always-on situational awareness. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that ViewBirdiformer achieves accuracy similar to or better than state-of-the-art with three orders of magnitude reduction in execution time.
summary: We introduce a novel learning-based method for view birdification, the task of recovering ground-plane trajectories of pedestrians of a crowd and their observer in the same crowd just from the observed ego-centric video. View birdification becomes essential for mobile robot navigation and localization in dense crowds where the static background is hard to see and reliably track. It is challenging mainly for two reasons; i) absolute trajectories of pedestrians are entangled with the movement of the observer which needs to be decoupled from their observed relative movements in the ego-centric video, and ii) a crowd motion model describing the pedestrian movement interactions is specific to the scene yet unknown a priori. For this, we introduce a Transformer-based network referred to as ViewBirdiformer which implicitly models the crowd motion through self-attention and decomposes relative 2D movement observations onto the ground-plane trajectories of the crowd and the camera through cross-attention between views. Most important, ViewBirdiformer achieves view birdification in a single forward pass which opens the door to accurate real-time, always-on situational awareness. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that ViewBirdiformer achieves accuracy similar to or better than state-of-the-art with three orders of magnitude reduction in execution time.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了一种新颖的基于学习的视图鸟化方法，该方法的任务是仅从观察到的以自我为中心的视频中恢复人群中的行人和同一人群中的观察者的地平面轨迹。视图鸟化对于移动机器人在密集人群中导航和定位至关重要，因为静态背景很难看到和可靠跟踪。其具有挑战性主要有两个原因： i）行人的绝对轨迹与观察者的运动纠缠在一起，需要将其与以自我为中心的视频中观察到的相对运动脱钩；ii）描述行人运动交互的人群运动模型特定于场景但先验未知。为此，我们引入了一个基于 Transformer 的网络，称为 ViewBirdiformer，它通过自注意力隐式地模拟人群运动，并通过视图之间的交叉注意力将相对的 2D 运动观察分解到人群和相机的地平面轨迹上。最重要的是，ViewBirdiformer 在一次前向传递中实现了视图鸟化，这为准确的实时、始终在线的态势感知打开了大门。大量的实验结果表明，ViewBirdiformer 的精度与最先进的技术相似或更好，并且执行时间减少了三个数量级。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于学习的方法，从单视角视频中恢复地面人群轨迹及观察者运动，称为ViewBirdiformer。
AI.motivation: 在密集人群中，机器人导航和定位需要准确的地面轨迹信息，但静态背景难以观测且行人与观察者运动相互干扰，现有方法难以高效解耦。
AI.method: 设计基于Transformer的ViewBirdiformer网络，利用自注意力建模人群交互，通过跨视图交叉注意力分解相对运动为地面轨迹与相机运动，实现单次前向传递的实时处理。
AI.result: 实验显示ViewBirdiformer在准确率上达到或超越现有方法，且执行时间减少三个数量级。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了人群运动解耦难题，为实时机器人感知提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Xiaojian Xu,Weijie Gan,Satya V. V. N. Kothapalli,Dmitriy A. Yablonskiy,Ulugbek S. Kamilov
title: CoRRECT: A Deep Unfolding Framework for Motion-Corrected Quantitative R2* Mapping
title_zh: CoRRECT：运动校正定量 R2* 映射的深度展开框架
abstract: Quantitative MRI (qMRI) refers to a class of MRI methods for quantifying the spatial distribution of biological tissue parameters. Traditional qMRI methods usually deal separately with artifacts arising from accelerated data acquisition, involuntary physical motion, and magnetic-field inhomogeneities, leading to suboptimal end-to-end performance. This paper presents CoRRECT, a unified deep unfolding (DU) framework for qMRI consisting of a model-based end-to-end neural network, a method for motion-artifact reduction, and a self-supervised learning scheme. The network is trained to produce R2* maps whose k-space data matches the real data by also accounting for motion and field inhomogeneities. When deployed, CoRRECT only uses the k-space data without any pre-computed parameters for motion or inhomogeneity correction. Our results on experimentally collected multi-Gradient-Recalled Echo (mGRE) MRI data show that CoRRECT recovers motion and inhomogeneity artifact-free R2* maps in highly accelerated acquisition settings. This work opens the door to DU methods that can integrate physical measurement models, biophysical signal models, and learned prior models for high-quality qMRI.
summary: Quantitative MRI (qMRI) refers to a class of MRI methods for quantifying the spatial distribution of biological tissue parameters. Traditional qMRI methods usually deal separately with artifacts arising from accelerated data acquisition, involuntary physical motion, and magnetic-field inhomogeneities, leading to suboptimal end-to-end performance. This paper presents CoRRECT, a unified deep unfolding (DU) framework for qMRI consisting of a model-based end-to-end neural network, a method for motion-artifact reduction, and a self-supervised learning scheme. The network is trained to produce R2* maps whose k-space data matches the real data by also accounting for motion and field inhomogeneities. When deployed, CoRRECT only uses the k-space data without any pre-computed parameters for motion or inhomogeneity correction. Our results on experimentally collected multi-Gradient-Recalled Echo (mGRE) MRI data show that CoRRECT recovers motion and inhomogeneity artifact-free R2* maps in highly accelerated acquisition settings. This work opens the door to DU methods that can integrate physical measurement models, biophysical signal models, and learned prior models for high-quality qMRI.
abstract_zh: 定量MRI（qMRI）是指量化生物组织参数空间分布的一类MRI方法。传统的 qMRI 方法通常单独处理加速数据采集、不自觉的物理运动和磁场不均匀性产生的伪影，从而导致端到端性能不佳。本文提出了 CoRRECT，一种用于 qMRI 的统一深度展开 (DU) 框架，由基于模型的端到端神经网络、运动伪影减少方法和自监督学习方案组成。该网络经过训练，可以生成 R2* 映射，其 k 空间数据与真实数据相匹配，同时还考虑了运动和场的不均匀性。部署时，CoRRECT 仅使用 k 空间数据，而不使用任何预先计算的参数来进行运动或不均匀性校正。我们对实验收集的多梯度回波 (mGRE) MRI 数据的结果表明，CoRRECT 在高度加速的采集设置中恢复了运动和不均匀性、无伪影的 R2* 图。这项工作为 DU 方法打开了大门，该方法可以集成物理测量模型、生物物理信号模型和学习的先前模型以实现高质量 qMRI。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoRRECT框架，通过深度展开方法实现运动校正的定量R2*磁共振成像。
AI.motivation: 传统定量磁共振成像方法分别处理加速采集、运动伪影和磁场不均匀性问题，导致整体性能不足。
AI.method: 构建基于物理模型的端到端神经网络，结合运动伪影消除与自监督学习，直接利用k空间数据生成R2*图。
AI.result: 在高加速采集条件下，CoRRECT成功恢复无运动和磁场不均匀性伪影的R2*图。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为整合物理测量模型、生物物理信号模型和学习先验模型的深度展开方法提供了新方向。

search_text: CoRRECT: A Deep Unfolding Framework for Motion-Corrected Quantitative R2* Mapping CoRRECT：运动校正定量 R2* 映射的深度展开框架 Quantitative MRI (qMRI) refers to a class of MRI methods for quantifying the spatial distribution of biological tissue parameters. Traditional qMRI methods usually deal separately with artifacts arising from accelerated data acquisition, involuntary physical motion, and magnetic-field inhomogeneities, leading to suboptimal end-to-end performance. This paper presents CoRRECT, a unified deep unfolding (DU) framework for qMRI consisting of a model-based end-to-end neural network, a method for motion-artifact reduction, and a self-supervised learning scheme. The network is trained to produce R2* maps whose k-space data matches the real data by also accounting for motion and field inhomogeneities. When deployed, CoRRECT only uses the k-space data without any pre-computed parameters for motion or inhomogeneity correction. Our results on experimentally collected multi-Gradient-Recalled Echo (mGRE) MRI data show that CoRRECT recovers motion and inhomogeneity artifact-free R2* maps in highly accelerated acquisition settings. This work opens the door to DU methods that can integrate physical measurement models, biophysical signal models, and learned prior models for high-quality qMRI. 定量MRI（qMRI）是指量化生物组织参数空间分布的一类MRI方法。传统的 qMRI 方法通常单独处理加速数据采集、不自觉的物理运动和磁场不均匀性产生的伪影，从而导致端到端性能不佳。本文提出了 CoRRECT，一种用于 qMRI 的统一深度展开 (DU) 框架，由基于模型的端到端神经网络、运动伪影减少方法和自监督学习方案组成。该网络经过训练，可以生成 R2* 映射，其 k 空间数据与真实数据相匹配，同时还考虑了运动和场的不均匀性。部署时，CoRRECT 仅使用 k 空间数据，而不使用任何预先计算的参数来进行运动或不均匀性校正。我们对实验收集的多梯度回波 (mGRE) MRI 数据的结果表明，CoRRECT 在高度加速的采集设置中恢复了运动和不均匀性、无伪影的 R2* 图。这项工作为 DU 方法打开了大门，该方法可以集成物理测量模型、生物物理信号模型和学习的先前模型以实现高质量 qMRI。 本文提出CoRRECT框架，通过深度展开方法实现运动校正的定量R2*磁共振成像。 Xiaojian Xu,Weijie Gan,Satya V. V. N. Kothapalli,Dmitriy A. Yablonskiy,Ulugbek S. Kamilov eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Thibault Sellam,Ankur Bapna,Joshua Camp,Diana Mackinnon,Ankur P. Parikh,Jason Riesa
title: SQuId: Measuring Speech Naturalness in Many Languages
title_zh: SQuId：测量多种语言的语音自然度
comment: Accepted at ICASSP 2023, with additional material in the appendix
comments: Accepted at ICASSP 2023, with additional material in the appendix
abstract: Much of text-to-speech research relies on human evaluation, which incurs heavy costs and slows down the development process. The problem is particularly acute in heavily multilingual applications, where recruiting and polling judges can take weeks. We introduce SQuId (Speech Quality Identification), a multilingual naturalness prediction model trained on over a million ratings and tested in 65 locales-the largest effort of this type to date. The main insight is that training one model on many locales consistently outperforms mono-locale baselines. We present our task, the model, and show that it outperforms a competitive baseline based on w2v-BERT and VoiceMOS by 50.0%. We then demonstrate the effectiveness of cross-locale transfer during fine-tuning and highlight its effect on zero-shot locales, i.e., locales for which there is no fine-tuning data. Through a series of analyses, we highlight the role of non-linguistic effects such as sound artifacts in cross-locale transfer. Finally, we present the effect of our design decision, e.g., model size, pre-training diversity, and language rebalancing with several ablation experiments.
summary: Much of text-to-speech research relies on human evaluation, which incurs heavy costs and slows down the development process. The problem is particularly acute in heavily multilingual applications, where recruiting and polling judges can take weeks. We introduce SQuId (Speech Quality Identification), a multilingual naturalness prediction model trained on over a million ratings and tested in 65 locales-the largest effort of this type to date. The main insight is that training one model on many locales consistently outperforms mono-locale baselines. We present our task, the model, and show that it outperforms a competitive baseline based on w2v-BERT and VoiceMOS by 50.0%. We then demonstrate the effectiveness of cross-locale transfer during fine-tuning and highlight its effect on zero-shot locales, i.e., locales for which there is no fine-tuning data. Through a series of analyses, we highlight the role of non-linguistic effects such as sound artifacts in cross-locale transfer. Finally, we present the effect of our design decision, e.g., model size, pre-training diversity, and language rebalancing with several ablation experiments.
abstract_zh: 许多文本转语音研究依赖于人类评估，这会产生高昂的成本并减慢开发过程。这个问题在多语言应用程序中尤其严重，法官的招募和投票可能需要数周时间。我们引入了 SQuId（语音质量识别），这是一种多语言自然度预测模型，经过超过 100 万个评分的训练，并在 65 个语言环境中进行了测试，这是迄今为止此类类型中最大的努力。主要见解是，在多个区域设置上训练一个模型始终优于单一区域设置基线。我们展示了我们的任务（模型），并表明它的性能比基于 w2v-BERT 和 VoiceMOS 的竞争基准高出 50.0%。然后，我们展示了微调期间跨区域设置传输的有效性，并强调了它对零样本区域设置（即没有微调数据的区域设置）的影响。通过一系列分析，我们强调了非语言效应（例如声音伪影）在跨语言环境迁移中的作用。最后，我们通过几个消融实验展示了我们的设计决策的效果，例如模型大小、预训练多样性和语言重新平衡。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SQuId，一种基于大规模多语言数据训练的语音自然度预测模型，用于替代传统的人工评估方法。
AI.motivation: 传统文本到语音（TTS）研究依赖人工评估，成本高且效率低，尤其在多语言场景下难以快速获取评估结果。
AI.method: 训练一个跨65种语言的统一模型，利用超百万条评分数据，通过跨语言迁移学习和零样本测试验证效果，并分析模型规模、预训练多样性等因素的影响。
AI.result: SQuId在零样本语言上表现优于基线模型（如w2v-BERT和VoiceMOS）50.0%，揭示了非语言因素（如声音伪影）对跨语言迁移的影响。
AI.conclusion: 统一多语言模型在跨语言迁移中优于单语言基线，为自动化多语言语音质量评估提供了有效方案。

The problem is particularly acute in heavily multilingual applications, where recruiting and polling judges can take weeks. We introduce SQuId (Speech Quality Identification), a multilingual naturalness prediction model trained on over a million ratings and tested in 65 locales-the largest effort of this type to date. The main insight is that training one model on many locales consistently outperforms mono-locale baselines. We present our task, the model, and show that it outperforms a competitive baseline based on w2v-BERT and VoiceMOS by 50.0%. We then demonstrate the effectiveness of cross-locale transfer during fine-tuning and highlight its effect on zero-shot locales, i.e., locales for which there is no fine-tuning data. Through a series of analyses, we highlight the role of non-linguistic effects such as sound artifacts in cross-locale transfer. Finally, we present the effect of our design decision, e.g., model size, pre-training diversity, and language rebalancing with several ablation experiments. 许多文本转语音研究依赖于人类评估，这会产生高昂的成本并减慢开发过程。这个问题在多语言应用程序中尤其严重，法官的招募和投票可能需要数周时间。我们引入了 SQuId（语音质量识别），这是一种多语言自然度预测模型，经过超过 100 万个评分的训练，并在 65 个语言环境中进行了测试，这是迄今为止此类类型中最大的努力。主要见解是，在多个区域设置上训练一个模型始终优于单一区域设置基线。我们展示了我们的任务（模型），并表明它的性能比基于 w2v-BERT 和 VoiceMOS 的竞争基准高出 50.0%。然后，我们展示了微调期间跨区域设置传输的有效性，并强调了它对零样本区域设置（即没有微调数据的区域设置）的影响。通过一系列分析，我们强调了非语言效应（例如声音伪影）在跨语言环境迁移中的作用。最后，我们通过几个消融实验展示了我们的设计决策的效果，例如模型大小、预训练多样性和语言重新平衡。 本文提出SQuId，一种基于大规模多语言数据训练的语音自然度预测模型，用于替代传统的人工评估方法。 Thibault Sellam,Ankur Bapna,Joshua Camp,Diana Mackinnon,Ankur P. authors_text: Minh Tran,Khoa Vo,Kashu Yamazaki,Arthur Fernandes,Michael Kidd,Ngan Le
title: AISFormer: Amodal Instance Segmentation with Transformer
title_zh: AISFormer：使用 Transformer 进行非模态实例分割
abstract: Amodal Instance Segmentation (AIS) aims to segment the region of both visible and possible occluded parts of an object instance. While Mask R-CNN-based AIS approaches have shown promising results, they are unable to model high-level features coherence due to the limited receptive field. The most recent transformer-based models show impressive performance on vision tasks, even better than Convolution Neural Networks (CNN). In this work, we present AISFormer, an AIS framework, with a Transformer-based mask head. AISFormer explicitly models the complex coherence between occluder, visible, amodal, and invisible masks within an object's regions of interest by treating them as learnable queries. Specifically, AISFormer contains four modules: (i) feature encoding: extract ROI and learn both short-range and long-range visual features. (ii) mask transformer decoding: generate the occluder, visible, and amodal mask query embeddings by a transformer decoder (iii) invisible mask embedding: model the coherence between the amodal and visible masks, and (iv) mask predicting: estimate output masks including occluder, visible, amodal and invisible. We conduct extensive experiments and ablation studies on three challenging benchmarks i.e. KINS, D2SA, and COCOA-cls to evaluate the effectiveness of AISFormer. The code is available at: https://github.com/UARK-AICV/AISFormer
summary: Amodal Instance Segmentation (AIS) aims to segment the region of both visible and possible occluded parts of an object instance. While Mask R-CNN-based AIS approaches have shown promising results, they are unable to model high-level features coherence due to the limited receptive field. The most recent transformer-based models show impressive performance on vision tasks, even better than Convolution Neural Networks (CNN). In this work, we present AISFormer, an AIS framework, with a Transformer-based mask head. AISFormer explicitly models the complex coherence between occluder, visible, amodal, and invisible masks within an object's regions of interest by treating them as learnable queries. Specifically, AISFormer contains four modules: (i) feature encoding: extract ROI and learn both short-range and long-range visual features. (ii) mask transformer decoding: generate the occluder, visible, and amodal mask query embeddings by a transformer decoder (iii) invisible mask embedding: model the coherence between the amodal and visible masks, and (iv) mask predicting: estimate output masks including occluder, visible, amodal and invisible. We conduct extensive experiments and ablation studies on three challenging benchmarks i.e. KINS, D2SA, and COCOA-cls to evaluate the effectiveness of AISFormer. The code is available at: https://github.com/UARK-AICV/AISFormer
abstract_zh: 非模态实例分割（AIS）旨在分割对象实例的可见部分和可能被遮挡部分的区域。虽然基于 Mask R-CNN 的 AIS 方法已显示出有希望的结果，但由于感受野有限，它们无法对高级特征一致性进行建模。最新的基于 Transformer 的模型在视觉任务上表现出了令人印象深刻的性能，甚至比卷积神经网络 (CNN) 还要好。在这项工作中，我们提出了 AISFormer，一个 AIS 框架，带有基于 Transformer 的掩模头。 AISFormer 通过将对象感兴趣区域内的遮挡物、可见、非模态和不可见掩模视为可学习的查询，对它们之间的复杂一致性进行显式建模。具体来说，AISFormer包含四个模块：（i）特征编码：提取ROI并学习短程和长程视觉特征。 （ii）掩模变换器解码：通过变换器解码器生成遮挡物、可见和非模态掩模查询嵌入（iii）不可见掩模嵌入：对非模态和可见掩模之间的一致性进行建模，以及（iv）掩模预测：估计包括遮挡物、可见、非模态和不可见的输出掩模。我们对三个具有挑战性的基准（即 KINS、D2SA 和 COCOA-cls）进行了广泛的实验和消融研究，以评估 AISFormer 的有效性。代码位于：https://github.com/UARK-AICV/AISFormer

AI.tldr: 本文提出AISFormer框架，利用Transformer模型解决Amodal Instance Segmentation（AIS）问题，旨在同时分割物体可见和被遮挡的部分。
AI.motivation: 传统Mask R-CNN方法因感受野有限，难以建模高层特征一致性；Transformer在视觉任务中表现优于CNN，因此需探索其在AIS中的应用。
AI.method: AISFormer包含四个模块：特征编码（提取ROI及短/长距离特征）、掩码Transformer解码（生成遮挡者、可见、模态掩码查询嵌入）、不可见掩码嵌入（建模模态与可见掩码一致性）及掩码预测（输出四类掩码）。
AI.result: 在KINS、D2SA和COCOA-cls三个基准上进行大量实验和消融研究，验证了AISFormer的有效性，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: AISFormer通过将遮挡关系建模为可学习查询，有效提升了AIS性能，为后续研究提供了新思路。
