search_text: Convolutional Neural Network-Based Image Watermarking using Discrete Wavelet Transform 使用离散小波变换的基于卷积神经网络的图像水印 With the growing popularity of the Internet, digital images are used and transferred more frequently. Although this phenomenon facilitates easy access to information, it also creates security concerns and violates intellectual property rights by allowing illegal use, copying, and digital content theft. Using watermarks in digital images is one of the most common ways to maintain security. Watermarking is proving and declaring ownership of an image by adding a digital watermark to the original image. Watermarks can be either text or an image placed overtly or covertly in an image and are expected to be challenging to remove. This paper proposes a combination of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and wavelet transforms to obtain a watermarking network for embedding and extracting watermarks. The network is independent of the host image resolution, can accept all kinds of watermarks, and has only 11 layers while keeping performance. Performance is measured by two terms; the similarity between the extracted watermark and the original one and the similarity between the host image and the watermarked one. 随着互联网的日益普及，数字图像的使用和传输更加频繁。尽管这种现象方便了信息的获取，但它也造成了安全问题，并允许非法使用、复制和盗窃数字内容，从而侵犯了知识产权。在数字图像中使用水印是维护安全性的最常见方法之一。水印是通过在原始图像上添加数字水印来证明和声明图像的所有权。水印可以是文本，也可以是公开或隐蔽地放置在图像中的图像，并且预计很难删除。本文提出了卷积神经网络（CNN）和小波变换的结合，以获得用于嵌入和提取水印的水印网络。该网络与主机图像分辨率无关，可以接受各种水印，并且在保持性能的情况下只有11层。绩效通过两个术语来衡量；提取的水印与原始水印之间的相似度以及宿主图像与加水印的水印之间的相似度。 本文提出一种结合卷积神经网络（CNN）和离散小波变换（DWT）的图像水印方法，实现水印的嵌入与提取。 Alireza Tavakoli,Zahra Honjani,Hedieh Sajedi eess.IV,cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Junjie Li,Meng Ge,Zexu Pan,Longbiao Wang,Jianwu Dang
title: VCSE: Time-Domain Visual-Contextual Speaker Extraction Network
title_zh: VCSE：时域视觉上下文说话人提取网络
abstract: Speaker extraction seeks to extract the target speech in a multi-talker scenario given an auxiliary reference. Such reference can be auditory, i.e., a pre-recorded speech, visual, i.e., lip movements, or contextual, i.e., phonetic sequence. References in different modalities provide distinct and complementary information that could be fused to form top-down attention on the target speaker. Previous studies have introduced visual and contextual modalities in a single model. In this paper, we propose a two-stage time-domain visual-contextual speaker extraction network named VCSE, which incorporates visual and self-enrolled contextual cues stage by stage to take full advantage of every modality. In the first stage, we pre-extract a target speech with visual cues and estimate the underlying phonetic sequence. In the second stage, we refine the pre-extracted target speech with the self-enrolled contextual cues. Experimental results on the real-world Lip Reading Sentences 3 (LRS3) database demonstrate that our proposed VCSE network consistently outperforms other state-of-the-art baselines.
summary: Speaker extraction seeks to extract the target speech in a multi-talker scenario given an auxiliary reference. Such reference can be auditory, i.e., a pre-recorded speech, visual, i.e., lip movements, or contextual, i.e., phonetic sequence. References in different modalities provide distinct and complementary information that could be fused to form top-down attention on the target speaker. Previous studies have introduced visual and contextual modalities in a single model. In this paper, we propose a two-stage time-domain visual-contextual speaker extraction network named VCSE, which incorporates visual and self-enrolled contextual cues stage by stage to take full advantage of every modality. In the first stage, we pre-extract a target speech with visual cues and estimate the underlying phonetic sequence. In the second stage, we refine the pre-extracted target speech with the self-enrolled contextual cues. Experimental results on the real-world Lip Reading Sentences 3 (LRS3) database demonstrate that our proposed VCSE network consistently outperforms other state-of-the-art baselines.
abstract_zh: 说话人提取旨在在给定辅助参考的情况下提取多说话者场景中的目标语音。这种参考可以是听觉的，即预先录制的语音，视觉的，即嘴唇的动作，或上下文的，即语音序列。不同方式的参考提供了独特且互补的信息，这些信息可以融合起来形成对目标说话者的自上而下的关注。之前的研究已经在单一模型中引入了视觉和情境模式。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为 VCSE 的两阶段时域视觉上下文说话人提取网络，该网络逐步结合视觉和自我注册的上下文线索，以充分利用每种模态。在第一阶段，我们预先提取带有视觉提示的目标语音并估计潜在的语音序列。在第二阶段，我们使用自行注册的上下文线索完善预先提取的目标语音。现实世界唇读句子 3 (LRS3) 数据库的实验结果表明，我们提出的 VCSE 网络始终优于其他最先进的基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为VCSE的两阶段时域视觉-上下文说话人提取网络，旨在通过结合视觉线索和自注册上下文信息，从多说话人场景中提取目标语音。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通常单独使用视觉或上下文模态，未能充分融合多模态信息。本文通过分阶段整合视觉与上下文线索，以提升目标语音提取的准确性。
AI.method: VCSE网络分为两阶段：第一阶段利用视觉线索预提取目标语音并估计音素序列；第二阶段通过自注册上下文信息优化预提取结果，实现多模态信息的逐步融合。
AI.result: 在Lip Reading Sentences 3 (LRS3)真实数据集上的实验表明，VCSE在多个指标上均优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 两阶段分步融合视觉与上下文模态的策略能有效提升多说话人场景下的目标语音提取性能，为跨模态语音处理提供新思路。

authors_text: Zih-Ching Chen,Chin-Lun Fu,Chih-Ying Liu,Shang-Wen Li,Hung-yi Lee
title: Exploring Efficient-tuning Methods in Self-supervised Speech Models
title_zh: 探索自监督语音模型中的高效调整方法
abstract: In this study, we aim to explore efficient tuning methods for speech self-supervised learning. Recent studies show that self-supervised learning (SSL) can learn powerful representations for different speech tasks. However, fine-tuning pre-trained models for each downstream task is parameter-inefficient since SSL models are notoriously large with millions of parameters. Adapters are lightweight modules commonly used in NLP to solve this problem. In downstream tasks, the parameters of SSL models are frozen, and only the adapters are trained. Given the lack of studies generally exploring the effectiveness of adapters for self-supervised speech tasks, we intend to fill this gap by adding various adapter modules in pre-trained speech SSL models. We show that the performance parity can be achieved with over 90% parameter reduction, and discussed the pros and cons of efficient tuning techniques. This is the first comprehensive investigation of various adapter types across speech tasks.
summary: In this study, we aim to explore efficient tuning methods for speech self-supervised learning. Recent studies show that self-supervised learning (SSL) can learn powerful representations for different speech tasks. However, fine-tuning pre-trained models for each downstream task is parameter-inefficient since SSL models are notoriously large with millions of parameters. Adapters are lightweight modules commonly used in NLP to solve this problem. In downstream tasks, the parameters of SSL models are frozen, and only the adapters are trained. Given the lack of studies generally exploring the effectiveness of adapters for self-supervised speech tasks, we intend to fill this gap by adding various adapter modules in pre-trained speech SSL models. We show that the performance parity can be achieved with over 90% parameter reduction, and discussed the pros and cons of efficient tuning techniques. This is the first comprehensive investigation of various adapter types across speech tasks.
abstract_zh: 在本研究中，我们旨在探索语音自监督学习的有效调整方法。最近的研究表明，自我监督学习（SSL）可以学习不同语音任务的强大表示。然而，为每个下游任务微调预训练模型的参数效率很低，因为 SSL 模型非常庞大，具有数百万个参数。适配器是 NLP 中常用来解决这个问题的轻量级模块。在下游任务中，SSL模型的参数被冻结，并且仅训练适配器。鉴于缺乏普遍探索适配器在自监督语音任务中的有效性的研究，我们打算通过在预训练的语音 SSL 模型中添加各种适配器模块来填补这一空白。我们证明了可以通过减少 90% 以上的参数来实现性能同等，并讨论了高效调优技术的优缺点。这是对跨语音任务的各种适配器类型的首次全面调查。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在探索在自监督语音模型中使用适配器模块进行高效微调的方法，以减少参数量同时保持模型性能。
AI.motivation: 尽管自监督学习（SSL）能学习强大的语音表示，但微调预训练模型时参数效率低下。现有研究缺乏对适配器模块在语音任务中有效性的系统探索，需寻找更高效的微调方法。
AI.method: 在预训练的自监督语音模型中插入多种适配器模块，冻结主模型参数，仅训练适配器模块，并对比分析不同适配器结构在下游任务中的表现。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用适配器模块可在减少超过90%参数的情况下实现与全参数微调相当的性能，并系统比较了不同高效微调技术的优缺点。
AI.conclusion: 本研究首次全面评估了多种适配器结构在自监督语音任务中的有效性，为高效微调提供了理论依据和实践指导，填补了该领域的研究空白。

search_text: Exploring Efficient-tuning Methods in Self-supervised Speech Models 探索自监督语音模型中的高效调整方法 In this study, we aim to explore efficient tuning methods for speech self-supervised learning. Recent studies show that self-supervised learning (SSL) can learn powerful representations for different speech tasks. However, fine-tuning pre-trained models for each downstream task is parameter-inefficient since SSL models are notoriously large with millions of parameters. Adapters are lightweight modules commonly used in NLP to solve this problem. In downstream tasks, the parameters of SSL models are frozen, and only the adapters are trained. Given the lack of studies generally exploring the effectiveness of adapters for self-supervised speech tasks, we intend to fill this gap by adding various adapter modules in pre-trained speech SSL models. We show that the performance parity can be achieved with over 90% parameter reduction, and discussed the pros and cons of efficient tuning techniques. This is the first comprehensive investigation of various adapter types across speech tasks. 在本研究中，我们旨在探索语音自监督学习的有效调整方法。最近的研究表明，自我监督学习（SSL）可以学习不同语音任务的强大表示。然而，为每个下游任务微调预训练模型的参数效率很低，因为 SSL 模型非常庞大，具有数百万个参数。适配器是 NLP 中常用来解决这个问题的轻量级模块。在下游任务中，SSL模型的参数被冻结，并且仅训练适配器。鉴于缺乏普遍探索适配器在自监督语音任务中的有效性的研究，我们打算通过在预训练的语音 SSL 模型中添加各种适配器模块来填补这一空白。我们证明了可以通过减少 90% 以上的参数来实现性能同等，并讨论了高效调优技术的优缺点。这是对跨语音任务的各种适配器类型的首次全面调查。 本文旨在探索在自监督语音模型中使用适配器模块进行高效微调的方法，以减少参数量同时保持模型性能。 Zih-Ching Chen,Chin-Lun Fu,Chih-Ying Liu,Shang-Wen Li,Hung-yi Lee eess.AS,cs.LG,cs.SD

authors_text: Jonathan C Balloch,Julia Kim,and Jessica L Inman,Mark O Riedl
title: The Role of Exploration for Task Transfer in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 任务迁移探索在强化学习中的作用
abstract: The exploration--exploitation trade-off in reinforcement learning (RL) is a well-known and much-studied problem that balances greedy action selection with novel experience, and the study of exploration methods is usually only considered in the context of learning the optimal policy for a single learning task. However, in the context of online task transfer, where there is a change to the task during online operation, we hypothesize that exploration strategies that anticipate the need to adapt to future tasks can have a pronounced impact on the efficiency of transfer. As such, we re-examine the exploration--exploitation trade-off in the context of transfer learning. In this work, we review reinforcement learning exploration methods, define a taxonomy with which to organize them, analyze these methods' differences in the context of task transfer, and suggest avenues for future investigation.
summary: The exploration--exploitation trade-off in reinforcement learning (RL) is a well-known and much-studied problem that balances greedy action selection with novel experience, and the study of exploration methods is usually only considered in the context of learning the optimal policy for a single learning task. However, in the context of online task transfer, where there is a change to the task during online operation, we hypothesize that exploration strategies that anticipate the need to adapt to future tasks can have a pronounced impact on the efficiency of transfer. As such, we re-examine the exploration--exploitation trade-off in the context of transfer learning. In this work, we review reinforcement learning exploration methods, define a taxonomy with which to organize them, analyze these methods' differences in the context of task transfer, and suggest avenues for future investigation.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）中的探索-利用权衡是一个众所周知且被广泛研究的问题，它平衡贪婪的动作选择与新颖的经验，而探索方法的研究通常只在学习单个学习任务的最优策略的背景下考虑。然而，在在线任务转移的背景下，在线操作期间任务发生变化，我们假设预测适应未来任务需要的探索策略会对转移效率产生显着影响。因此，我们重新审视迁移学习背景下的探索与利用权衡。在这项工作中，我们回顾了强化学习探索方法，定义了组织它们的分类法，分析了这些方法在任务转移背景下的差异，并为未来的研究提出了途径。

AI.tldr: 探讨强化学习中探索策略在任务迁移中的作用，并重新审视探索-利用权衡。
AI.motivation: 传统探索方法仅针对单一任务，而在线任务迁移需要适应未来任务，需研究新的探索策略以提高迁移效率。
AI.method: 回顾RL探索方法，定义分类法，分析其在任务迁移中的差异，并提出未来研究方向。
AI.result: 未进行具体实验，但系统分析了现有探索方法在任务迁移中的差异，并提出未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 探索策略对任务迁移效率有显著影响，需在迁移学习中重新审视探索-利用权衡。

authors_text: Qiming Peng,Yinxu Pan,Wenjin Wang,Bin Luo,Zhenyu Zhang,Zhengjie Huang,Teng Hu,Weichong Yin,Yongfeng Chen,Yin Zhang,Shikun Feng,Yu Sun,Hao Tian,Hua Wu,Haifeng Wang
title: ERNIE-Layout: Layout Knowledge Enhanced Pre-training for Visually-rich Document Understanding
title_zh: ERNIE-Layout：布局知识增强预训练，实现视觉丰富的文档理解
abstract: Recent years have witnessed the rise and success of pre-training techniques in visually-rich document understanding. However, most existing methods lack the systematic mining and utilization of layout-centered knowledge, leading to sub-optimal performances. In this paper, we propose ERNIE-Layout, a novel document pre-training solution with layout knowledge enhancement in the whole workflow, to learn better representations that combine the features from text, layout, and image. Specifically, we first rearrange input sequences in the serialization stage, and then present a correlative pre-training task, reading order prediction, to learn the proper reading order of documents. To improve the layout awareness of the model, we integrate a spatial-aware disentangled attention into the multi-modal transformer and a replaced regions prediction task into the pre-training phase. Experimental results show that ERNIE-Layout achieves superior performance on various downstream tasks, setting new state-of-the-art on key information extraction, document image classification, and document question answering datasets. The code and models are publicly available at http://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleNLP/tree/develop/model_zoo/ernie-layout.
summary: Recent years have witnessed the rise and success of pre-training techniques in visually-rich document understanding. However, most existing methods lack the systematic mining and utilization of layout-centered knowledge, leading to sub-optimal performances. In this paper, we propose ERNIE-Layout, a novel document pre-training solution with layout knowledge enhancement in the whole workflow, to learn better representations that combine the features from text, layout, and image. Specifically, we first rearrange input sequences in the serialization stage, and then present a correlative pre-training task, reading order prediction, to learn the proper reading order of documents. To improve the layout awareness of the model, we integrate a spatial-aware disentangled attention into the multi-modal transformer and a replaced regions prediction task into the pre-training phase. Experimental results show that ERNIE-Layout achieves superior performance on various downstream tasks, setting new state-of-the-art on key information extraction, document image classification, and document question answering datasets. The code and models are publicly available at http://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleNLP/tree/develop/model_zoo/ernie-layout.
abstract_zh: 近年来，预训练技术在视觉丰富的文档理解方面的兴起和成功。然而，大多数现有方法缺乏对以布局为中心的知识的系统挖掘和利用，导致性能不佳。在本文中，我们提出了 ERNIE-Layout，这是一种新颖的文档预训练解决方案，在整个工作流程中增强布局知识，以学习结合文本、布局和图像特征的更好表示。具体来说，我们首先在序列化阶段重新排列输入序列，然后提出相关的预训练任务，即阅读顺序预测，以学习文档的正确阅读顺序。为了提高模型的布局意识，我们将空间感知的解缠注意力集成到多模态转换器中，并将替换区域预测任务集成到预训练阶段。实验结果表明，ERNIE-Layout 在各种下游任务上实现了卓越的性能，在关键信息提取、文档图像分类和文档问答数据集方面树立了新的最先进水平。代码和模型可在 http://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleNLP/tree/develop/model_zoo/ernie-layout 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ERNIE-Layout，一种通过增强布局知识的预训练方法，结合文本、布局和图像特征以提升视觉丰富文档的理解。
AI.motivation: 现有预训练方法未能系统挖掘布局知识，导致在视觉丰富文档任务中表现不足。
AI.method: 通过重新排列输入序列、引入阅读顺序预测任务，并在多模态Transformer中集成空间感知分离注意力与替换区域预测任务。
AI.result: 在关键信息抽取、文档图像分类和文档问答任务中达到新SOTA，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 布局知识增强的预训练方法有效提升了视觉文档理解任务的性能。

authors_text: Fernando Moral-Andrés,Elena Merino-Gómez,Pedro Reviriego,Fabrizio Lombardi
title: Can Artificial Intelligence Reconstruct Ancient Mosaics?
title_zh: 人工智能能否重建古代马赛克？
abstract: A large number of ancient mosaics have not reached us because they have been destroyed by erosion, earthquakes, looting or even used as materials in newer construction. To make things worse, among the small fraction of mosaics that we have been able to recover, many are damaged or incomplete. Therefore, restoration and reconstruction of mosaics play a fundamental role to preserve cultural heritage and to understand the role of mosaics in ancient cultures. This reconstruction has traditionally been done manually and more recently using computer graphics programs but always by humans. In the last years, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has made impressive progress in the generation of images from text descriptions and reference images. State of the art AI tools such as DALL-E2 can generate high quality images from text prompts and can take a reference image to guide the process. In august 2022, DALL-E2 launched a new feature called outpainting that takes as input an incomplete image and a text prompt and then generates a complete image filling the missing parts. In this paper, we explore whether this innovative technology can be used to reconstruct mosaics with missing parts. Hence a set of ancient mosaics have been used and reconstructed using DALL-E2; results are promising showing that AI is able to interpret the key features of the mosaics and is able to produce reconstructions that capture the essence of the scene. However, in some cases AI fails to reproduce some details, geometric forms or introduces elements that are not consistent with the rest of the mosaic. This suggests that as AI image generation technology matures in the next few years, it could be a valuable tool for mosaic reconstruction going forward.
summary: A large number of ancient mosaics have not reached us because they have been destroyed by erosion, earthquakes, looting or even used as materials in newer construction. To make things worse, among the small fraction of mosaics that we have been able to recover, many are damaged or incomplete. Therefore, restoration and reconstruction of mosaics play a fundamental role to preserve cultural heritage and to understand the role of mosaics in ancient cultures. This reconstruction has traditionally been done manually and more recently using computer graphics programs but always by humans. In the last years, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has made impressive progress in the generation of images from text descriptions and reference images. State of the art AI tools such as DALL-E2 can generate high quality images from text prompts and can take a reference image to guide the process. In august 2022, DALL-E2 launched a new feature called outpainting that takes as input an incomplete image and a text prompt and then generates a complete image filling the missing parts. In this paper, we explore whether this innovative technology can be used to reconstruct mosaics with missing parts. Hence a set of ancient mosaics have been used and reconstructed using DALL-E2; results are promising showing that AI is able to interpret the key features of the mosaics and is able to produce reconstructions that capture the essence of the scene. However, in some cases AI fails to reproduce some details, geometric forms or introduces elements that are not consistent with the rest of the mosaic. This suggests that as AI image generation technology matures in the next few years, it could be a valuable tool for mosaic reconstruction going forward.
abstract_zh: 大量古代马赛克尚未到达我们手中，因为它们已被侵蚀、地震、抢劫所破坏，甚至被用作新建筑的材料。更糟糕的是，在我们能够恢复的一小部分马赛克中，许多都已损坏或不完整。因此，马赛克的修复和重建对于保护文化遗产和了解马赛克在古代文化中的作用发挥着基础作用。这种重建传统上是手动完成的，最近使用计算机图形程序来完成，但总是由人类完成。在过去的几年里，人工智能 (AI) 在根据文本描述和参考图像生成图像方面取得了令人瞩目的进展。 DALL-E2 等最先进的人工智能工具可以根据文本提示生成高质量图像，并可以采用参考图像来指导该过程。 2022 年 8 月，DALL-E2 推出了一项名为“outpainting”的新功能，该功能将不完整的图像和文本提示作为输入，然后生成填充缺失部分的完整图像。在本文中，我们探讨了这种创新技术是否可以用于重建缺失部分的马赛克。因此，使用了一组古代马赛克并使用 DALL-E2 进行了重建；结果令人鼓舞，表明人工智能能够解释马赛克的关键特征，并能够生成捕捉场景本质的重建。然而，在某些情况下，人工智能无法重现一些细节、几何形​​式或引入与马赛克其余部分不一致的元素。这表明，随着人工智能图像生成技术在未来几年的成熟，它可能成为未来马赛克重建的宝贵工具。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨利用人工智能技术（如DALL-E2）重建残缺古代镶嵌画的可行性。
AI.motivation: 由于侵蚀、地震等因素，大量古代镶嵌画遗失或损坏，传统人工修复方法效率有限，需探索AI辅助重建技术以保护文化遗产。
AI.method: 使用DALL-E2的图像生成功能，输入残缺镶嵌画图像及文本提示，生成缺失部分的重建结果。
AI.result: AI能捕捉镶嵌画核心特征并生成合理重建图像，但在细节还原、几何形态及元素一致性方面存在不足。
AI.conclusion: AI图像生成技术有望成为镶嵌画重建的有效工具，但需进一步优化以提高细节准确性与历史真实性。

authors_text: Zhanyu Wang,Guang Cheng,Jordan Awan
title: Differentially Private Bootstrap: New Privacy Analysis and Inference Strategies
title_zh: 差分隐私引导：新的隐私分析和推理策略
comment: 22 pages before appendices and references. 50 pages total
comments: 22 pages before appendices and references. 50 pages total
abstract: Differentially private (DP) mechanisms protect individual-level information by introducing randomness into the statistical analysis procedure. Despite the availability of numerous DP tools, there remains a lack of general techniques for conducting statistical inference under DP. We examine a DP bootstrap procedure that releases multiple private bootstrap estimates to infer the sampling distribution and construct confidence intervals (CIs). Our privacy analysis presents new results on the privacy cost of a single DP bootstrap estimate, applicable to any DP mechanism, and identifies some misapplications of the bootstrap in the existing literature. For the composition of the DP bootstrap, we present a numerical method to compute the exact privacy cost of releasing multiple DP bootstrap estimates, and using the Gaussian-DP (GDP) framework (Dong et al., 2022), we show that the release of $B$ DP bootstrap estimates from mechanisms satisfying $(μ/\sqrt{(2-2/\mathrm{e})B})$-GDP asymptotically satisfies $μ$-GDP as $B$ goes to infinity. Then, we perform private statistical inference by post-processing the DP bootstrap estimates. We prove that our point estimates are consistent, our standard CIs are asymptotically valid, and both enjoy optimal convergence rates. To further improve the finite performance, we use deconvolution with DP bootstrap estimates to accurately infer the sampling distribution. We derive CIs for tasks such as population mean estimation, logistic regression, and quantile regression, and we compare them to existing methods using simulations and real-world experiments on 2016 Canada Census data. Our private CIs achieve the nominal coverage level and offer the first approach to private inference for quantile regression.
summary: Differentially private (DP) mechanisms protect individual-level information by introducing randomness into the statistical analysis procedure. Despite the availability of numerous DP tools, there remains a lack of general techniques for conducting statistical inference under DP. We examine a DP bootstrap procedure that releases multiple private bootstrap estimates to infer the sampling distribution and construct confidence intervals (CIs). Our privacy analysis presents new results on the privacy cost of a single DP bootstrap estimate, applicable to any DP mechanism, and identifies some misapplications of the bootstrap in the existing literature. For the composition of the DP bootstrap, we present a numerical method to compute the exact privacy cost of releasing multiple DP bootstrap estimates, and using the Gaussian-DP (GDP) framework (Dong et al., 2022), we show that the release of $B$ DP bootstrap estimates from mechanisms satisfying $(μ/\sqrt{(2-2/\mathrm{e})B})$-GDP asymptotically satisfies $μ$-GDP as $B$ goes to infinity. Then, we perform private statistical inference by post-processing the DP bootstrap estimates. We prove that our point estimates are consistent, our standard CIs are asymptotically valid, and both enjoy optimal convergence rates. To further improve the finite performance, we use deconvolution with DP bootstrap estimates to accurately infer the sampling distribution. We derive CIs for tasks such as population mean estimation, logistic regression, and quantile regression, and we compare them to existing methods using simulations and real-world experiments on 2016 Canada Census data. Our private CIs achieve the nominal coverage level and offer the first approach to private inference for quantile regression.
abstract_zh: 差分隐私（DP）机制通过在统计分析过程中引入随机性来保护个人级别的信息。尽管有许多DP工具可用，但仍然缺乏在DP下进行统计推断的通用技术。我们检查了 DP 引导程序，该程序发布多个私有引导程序估计来推断采样分布并构建置信区间 (CI)。我们的隐私分析提出了关于单个 DP bootstrap 估计的隐私成本的新结果，适用于任何 DP 机制，并识别了现有文献中 bootstrap 的一些误用。对于 DP bootstrap 的组成，我们提出了一种数值方法来计算发布多个 DP bootstrap 估计的确切隐私成本，并使用 Gaussian-DP (GDP) 框架（Dong et al., 2022），我们表明从满足 $(μ/\sqrt{(2-2/\mathrm{e})B})$-GDP 的机制中发布 $B$ DP bootstrap 估计渐近满足$μ$-GDP 随着 $B$ 趋于无穷大。然后，我们通过对 DP bootstrap 估计进行后处理来执行私人统计推断。我们证明我们的点估计是一致的，我们的标准 CI 是渐近有效的，并且都具有最佳收敛率。为了进一步提高有限性能，我们使用 DP bootstrap 估计的反卷积来准确推断采样分布。我们针对人口均值估计、逻辑回归和分位数回归等任务推导 CI，并使用 2016 年加拿大人口普查数据的模拟和真实实验将它们与现有方法进行比较。我们的私人 CI 达到了名义覆盖水平，并提供了第一种用于分位数回归的私人推理方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种差分隐私引导方法，通过释放多个隐私保护的引导估计量来推断抽样分布并构建置信区间，解决差分隐私下的统计推断问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管存在大量差分隐私工具，但缺乏通用的差分隐私统计推断技术，现有文献中引导方法存在误用，需改进隐私成本分析和推断策略。
AI.method: 提出差分隐私引导程序，结合隐私成本计算方法和高斯差分隐私框架，通过后处理引导估计量实现统计推断，并利用解卷积提升有限样本性能。
AI.result: 证明点估计一致性及置信区间的渐近有效性，通过模拟和加拿大人口普查数据实验证明私有置信区间达到标称覆盖率，首次实现量化回归的私有推断。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法为差分隐私统计推断提供有效框架，尤其适用于量化回归，在隐私保护与统计精度间取得平衡。

authors_text: Hongyuan Yu,Ting Li,Weichen Yu,Jianguo Li,Yan Huang,Liang Wang,Alex Liu
title: Regularized Graph Structure Learning with Semantic Knowledge for Multi-variates Time-Series Forecasting
title_zh: 具有语义知识的正则化图结构学习用于多变量时间序列预测
abstract: Multivariate time-series forecasting is a critical task for many applications, and graph time-series network is widely studied due to its capability to capture the spatial-temporal correlation simultaneously. However, most existing works focus more on learning with the explicit prior graph structure, while ignoring potential information from the implicit graph structure, yielding incomplete structure modeling. Some recent works attempt to learn the intrinsic or implicit graph structure directly while lacking a way to combine explicit prior structure with implicit structure together. In this paper, we propose Regularized Graph Structure Learning (RGSL) model to incorporate both explicit prior structure and implicit structure together, and learn the forecasting deep networks along with the graph structure. RGSL consists of two innovative modules. First, we derive an implicit dense similarity matrix through node embedding, and learn the sparse graph structure using the Regularized Graph Generation (RGG) based on the Gumbel Softmax trick. Second, we propose a Laplacian Matrix Mixed-up Module (LM3) to fuse the explicit graph and implicit graph together. We conduct experiments on three real-word datasets. Results show that the proposed RGSL model outperforms existing graph forecasting algorithms with a notable margin, while learning meaningful graph structure simultaneously. Our code and models are made publicly available at https://github.com/alipay/RGSL.git.
summary: Multivariate time-series forecasting is a critical task for many applications, and graph time-series network is widely studied due to its capability to capture the spatial-temporal correlation simultaneously. However, most existing works focus more on learning with the explicit prior graph structure, while ignoring potential information from the implicit graph structure, yielding incomplete structure modeling. Some recent works attempt to learn the intrinsic or implicit graph structure directly while lacking a way to combine explicit prior structure with implicit structure together. In this paper, we propose Regularized Graph Structure Learning (RGSL) model to incorporate both explicit prior structure and implicit structure together, and learn the forecasting deep networks along with the graph structure. RGSL consists of two innovative modules. First, we derive an implicit dense similarity matrix through node embedding, and learn the sparse graph structure using the Regularized Graph Generation (RGG) based on the Gumbel Softmax trick. Second, we propose a Laplacian Matrix Mixed-up Module (LM3) to fuse the explicit graph and implicit graph together. We conduct experiments on three real-word datasets. Results show that the proposed RGSL model outperforms existing graph forecasting algorithms with a notable margin, while learning meaningful graph structure simultaneously. Our code and models are made publicly available at https://github.com/alipay/RGSL.git.
abstract_zh: 多元时间序列预测是许多应用的关键任务，图时间序列网络由于其同时捕获时空相关性的能力而被广泛研究。然而，大多数现有的工作更多地关注于使用显式先验图结构进行学习，而忽略了隐式图结构中的潜在信息，从而产生不完整的结构建模。最近的一些工作尝试直接学习内在或隐式图结构，但缺乏将显式先验结构与隐式结构结合在一起的方法。在本文中，我们提出了正则化图结构学习（RGSL）模型，将显式先验结构和隐式结构结合在一起，并学习预测深层网络和图结构。 RGSL 由两个创新模块组成。首先，我们通过节点嵌入导出隐式稠密相似度矩阵，并使用基于 Gumbel Softmax 技巧的正则化图生成（RGG）来学习稀疏图结构。其次，我们提出了拉普拉斯矩阵混合模块（LM3）将显式图和隐式图融合在一起。我们在三个真实数据集上进行了实验。结果表明，所提出的 RGSL 模型明显优于现有的图预测算法，同时学习有意义的图结构。我们的代码和模型已在 https://github.com/alipay/RGSL.git 上公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合显式先验结构和隐式结构的正则化图结构学习模型（RGSL），用于改进多变量时间序列预测任务。
AI.motivation: 现有图时间序列网络过度依赖显式先验图结构，忽视隐式图结构的潜在信息，导致结构建模不完整；同时缺乏有效融合显式与隐式结构的方法。
AI.method: 提出RGSL模型包含两个创新模块：1）通过节点嵌入生成隐式密集相似矩阵，基于Gumbel Softmax的正则化图生成（RGG）学习稀疏图结构；2）设计拉普拉斯矩阵混合模块（LM3）融合显式与隐式图结构。
AI.result: 在三个真实数据集上的实验表明，RGSL在预测精度上显著优于现有图预测算法，同时学习到有意义的图结构。
AI.conclusion: RGSL通过有效结合显式先验知识与隐式语义信息，实现了更准确的多变量时间序列预测，并提供了可解释的图结构学习框架。

However, most existing works focus more on learning with the explicit prior graph structure, while ignoring potential information from the implicit graph structure, yielding incomplete structure modeling. Some recent works attempt to learn the intrinsic or implicit graph structure directly while lacking a way to combine explicit prior structure with implicit structure together. In this paper, we propose Regularized Graph Structure Learning (RGSL) model to incorporate both explicit prior structure and implicit structure together, and learn the forecasting deep networks along with the graph structure. RGSL consists of two innovative modules. First, we derive an implicit dense similarity matrix through node embedding, and learn the sparse graph structure using the Regularized Graph Generation (RGG) based on the Gumbel Softmax trick. Second, we propose a Laplacian Matrix Mixed-up Module (LM3) to fuse the explicit graph and implicit graph together. We conduct experiments on three real-word datasets. Results show that the proposed RGSL model outperforms existing graph forecasting algorithms with a notable margin, while learning meaningful graph structure simultaneously. authors_text: Changkun Ye,Nick Barnes,Lars Petersson,Russell Tsuchida
title: Efficient Gaussian Process Model on Class-Imbalanced Datasets for Generalized Zero-Shot Learning
title_zh: 用于广义零样本学习的类不平衡数据集的高效高斯过程模型
abstract: Zero-Shot Learning (ZSL) models aim to classify object classes that are not seen during the training process. However, the problem of class imbalance is rarely discussed, despite its presence in several ZSL datasets. In this paper, we propose a Neural Network model that learns a latent feature embedding and a Gaussian Process (GP) regression model that predicts latent feature prototypes of unseen classes. A calibrated classifier is then constructed for ZSL and Generalized ZSL tasks. Our Neural Network model is trained efficiently with a simple training strategy that mitigates the impact of class-imbalanced training data. The model has an average training time of 5 minutes and can achieve state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on imbalanced ZSL benchmark datasets like AWA2, AWA1 and APY, while having relatively good performance on the SUN and CUB datasets.
summary: Zero-Shot Learning (ZSL) models aim to classify object classes that are not seen during the training process. However, the problem of class imbalance is rarely discussed, despite its presence in several ZSL datasets. In this paper, we propose a Neural Network model that learns a latent feature embedding and a Gaussian Process (GP) regression model that predicts latent feature prototypes of unseen classes. A calibrated classifier is then constructed for ZSL and Generalized ZSL tasks. Our Neural Network model is trained efficiently with a simple training strategy that mitigates the impact of class-imbalanced training data. The model has an average training time of 5 minutes and can achieve state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on imbalanced ZSL benchmark datasets like AWA2, AWA1 and APY, while having relatively good performance on the SUN and CUB datasets.
abstract_zh: 零样本学习 (ZSL) 模型旨在对训练过程中未见过的对象类进行分类。然而，类别不平衡问题很少被讨论，尽管它存在于多个 ZSL 数据集中。在本文中，我们提出了一个学习潜在特征嵌入的神经网络模型和一个预测未见过的类的潜在特征原型的高斯过程（GP）回归模型。然后为 ZSL 和广义 ZSL 任务构建校准分类器。我们的神经网络模型通过简单的训练策略进行有效训练，减轻了类别不平衡训练数据的影响。该模型的平均训练时间为 5 分钟，可以在 AWA2、AWA1 和 APY 等不平衡 ZSL 基准数据集上实现最先进 (SOTA) 的性能，同时在 SUN 和 CUB 数据集上具有相对较好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种高效的高斯过程模型，用于解决类别不平衡数据集上的广义零样本学习问题。
AI.motivation: 现有零样本学习研究忽视了类别不平衡问题，而实际数据集中普遍存在此类问题，影响模型性能。
AI.method: 结合神经网络学习潜在特征嵌入与高斯过程回归预测未见类特征原型，采用简单训练策略缓解类别不平衡影响。
AI.result: 在AWA2/AWA1/APY等不平衡数据集上达到SOTA性能，训练时间仅需5分钟，SUN/CUB数据集表现稳定。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效解决了类别不平衡下的零样本学习难题，为实际应用提供了高效可靠的解决方案。

authors_text: Yu Zheng,Jinghan Peng,Miao Zhao,Yufeng Ma,Min Liu,Xinyue Ma,Tianyu Liang,Tianlong Kong,Liang He,Minqiang Xu
title: THUEE system description for NIST 2020 SRE CTS challenge
title_zh: NIST 2020 SRE CTS 挑战赛的 THUEE 系统描述
comment: 3 pages, 1 table; System desciption of NIST 2020 SRE CTS challenge
comments: 3 pages, 1 table; System desciption of NIST 2020 SRE CTS challenge
abstract: This paper presents the system description of the THUEE team for the NIST 2020 Speaker Recognition Evaluation (SRE) conversational telephone speech (CTS) challenge. The subsystems including ResNet74, ResNet152, and RepVGG-B2 are developed as speaker embedding extractors in this evaluation. We used combined AM-Softmax and AAM-Softmax based loss functions, namely CM-Softmax. We adopted a two-staged training strategy to further improve system performance. We fused all individual systems as our final submission. Our approach leads to excellent performance and ranks 1st in the challenge.
summary: This paper presents the system description of the THUEE team for the NIST 2020 Speaker Recognition Evaluation (SRE) conversational telephone speech (CTS) challenge. The subsystems including ResNet74, ResNet152, and RepVGG-B2 are developed as speaker embedding extractors in this evaluation. We used combined AM-Softmax and AAM-Softmax based loss functions, namely CM-Softmax. We adopted a two-staged training strategy to further improve system performance. We fused all individual systems as our final submission. Our approach leads to excellent performance and ranks 1st in the challenge.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 THUEE 团队针对 NIST 2020 说话人识别评估 (SRE) 会话电话语音 (CTS) 挑战赛的系统描述。在本次评估中，包括 ResNet74、ResNet152 和 RepVGG-B2 在内的子系统被开发为说话人嵌入提取器。我们使用基于 AM-Softmax 和 AAM-Softmax 的组合损失函数，即 CM-Softmax。我们采用了两阶段的训练策略来进一步提高系统性能。我们融合了所有单独的系统作为我们的最终提交。我们的方法带来了出色的表现，并在挑战中排名第一。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了THUEE团队在NIST 2020说话人识别评估CTS挑战中的系统设计，旨在通过优化说话人嵌入提取和训练策略提升识别性能。
AI.motivation: 对话电话语音（CTS）因环境噪声和说话人状态变化导致识别难度高，需探索更鲁棒的嵌入提取方法和训练策略。
AI.method: 采用ResNet74、ResNet152和RepVGG-B2作为说话人嵌入提取器，结合AM-Softmax与AAM-Softmax损失函数（CM-Softmax），使用两阶段训练策略并融合多系统输出。
AI.result: THUEE团队方法在挑战赛中排名第一，验证了模型融合与优化损失函数的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过深度神经网络架构创新、损失函数改进及多阶段训练策略，显著提升了CTS场景下的说话人识别性能，为后续研究提供参考。

tldr: 本文介绍了THUEE团队在NIST 2020说话人识别评估CTS挑战中的系统设计，旨在通过优化说话人嵌入提取和训练策略提升识别性能。
motivation: 对话电话语音（CTS）因环境噪声和说话人状态变化导致识别难度高，需探索更鲁棒的嵌入提取方法和训练策略。
method: 采用ResNet74、ResNet152和RepVGG-B2作为说话人嵌入提取器，结合AM-Softmax与AAM-Softmax损失函数（CM-Softmax），使用两阶段训练策略并融合多系统输出。
result: THUEE团队方法在挑战赛中排名第一，验证了模型融合与优化损失函数的有效性。
conclusion: 通过深度神经网络架构创新、损失函数改进及多阶段训练策略，显著提升了CTS场景下的说话人识别性能，为后续研究提供参考。
search_text: THUEE system description for NIST 2020 SRE CTS challenge NIST 2020 SRE CTS 挑战赛的 THUEE 系统描述 This paper presents the system description of the THUEE team for the NIST 2020 Speaker Recognition Evaluation (SRE) conversational telephone speech (CTS) challenge. The subsystems including ResNet74, ResNet152, and RepVGG-B2 are developed as speaker embedding extractors in this evaluation. We used combined AM-Softmax and AAM-Softmax based loss functions, namely CM-Softmax. We adopted a two-staged training strategy to further improve system performance. We fused all individual systems as our final submission. Our approach leads to excellent performance and ranks 1st in the challenge. 本文介绍了 THUEE 团队针对 NIST 2020 说话人识别评估 (SRE) 会话电话语音 (CTS) 挑战赛的系统描述。在本次评估中，包括 ResNet74、ResNet152 和 RepVGG-B2 在内的子系统被开发为说话人嵌入提取器。我们使用基于 AM-Softmax 和 AAM-Softmax 的组合损失函数，即 CM-Softmax。我们采用了两阶段的训练策略来进一步提高系统性能。我们融合了所有单独的系统作为我们的最终提交。我们的方法带来了出色的表现，并在挑战中排名第一。 本文介绍了THUEE团队在NIST 2020说话人识别评估CTS挑战中的系统设计，旨在通过优化说话人嵌入提取和训练策略提升识别性能。 Yu Zheng,Jinghan Peng,Miao Zhao,Yufeng Ma,Min Liu,Xinyue Ma,Tianyu Liang,Tianlong Kong,Liang He,Minqiang Xu cs.SD,cs.AI,eess.AS,eess.SP

authors_text: Moritz Einfalt,Katja Ludwig,Rainer Lienhart
title: Uplift and Upsample: Efficient 3D Human Pose Estimation with Uplifting Transformers
title_zh: 提升和上采样：使用 Uplifting Transformer 进行高效 3D 人体姿势估计
abstract: The state-of-the-art for monocular 3D human pose estimation in videos is dominated by the paradigm of 2D-to-3D pose uplifting. While the uplifting methods themselves are rather efficient, the true computational complexity depends on the per-frame 2D pose estimation. In this paper, we present a Transformer-based pose uplifting scheme that can operate on temporally sparse 2D pose sequences but still produce temporally dense 3D pose estimates. We show how masked token modeling can be utilized for temporal upsampling within Transformer blocks. This allows to decouple the sampling rate of input 2D poses and the target frame rate of the video and drastically decreases the total computational complexity. Additionally, we explore the option of pre-training on large motion capture archives, which has been largely neglected so far. We evaluate our method on two popular benchmark datasets: Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP. With an MPJPE of 45.0 mm and 46.9 mm, respectively, our proposed method can compete with the state-of-the-art while reducing inference time by a factor of 12. This enables real-time throughput with variable consumer hardware in stationary and mobile applications. We release our code and models at https://github.com/goldbricklemon/uplift-upsample-3dhpe
summary: The state-of-the-art for monocular 3D human pose estimation in videos is dominated by the paradigm of 2D-to-3D pose uplifting. While the uplifting methods themselves are rather efficient, the true computational complexity depends on the per-frame 2D pose estimation. In this paper, we present a Transformer-based pose uplifting scheme that can operate on temporally sparse 2D pose sequences but still produce temporally dense 3D pose estimates. We show how masked token modeling can be utilized for temporal upsampling within Transformer blocks. This allows to decouple the sampling rate of input 2D poses and the target frame rate of the video and drastically decreases the total computational complexity. Additionally, we explore the option of pre-training on large motion capture archives, which has been largely neglected so far. We evaluate our method on two popular benchmark datasets: Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP. With an MPJPE of 45.0 mm and 46.9 mm, respectively, our proposed method can compete with the state-of-the-art while reducing inference time by a factor of 12. This enables real-time throughput with variable consumer hardware in stationary and mobile applications. We release our code and models at https://github.com/goldbricklemon/uplift-upsample-3dhpe
abstract_zh: 视频中单目 3D 人体姿势估计的最新技术以 2D 到 3D 姿势提升的范式为主。虽然提升方法本身相当高效，但真正的计算复杂度取决于每帧 2D 姿态估计。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 Transformer 的姿态提升方案，该方案可以对时间稀疏的 2D 姿态序列进行操作，但仍然产生时间密集的 3D 姿态估计。我们展示了如何利用屏蔽令牌建模在 Transformer 块内进行时间上采样。这允许将输入 2D 姿势的采样率和视频的目标帧率解耦，并大大降低总计算复杂度。此外，我们还探索了对大型动作捕捉档案进行预训练的选项，到目前为止，这在很大程度上被忽视了。我们在两个流行的基准数据集上评估我们的方法：Human3.6M 和 MPI-INF-3DHP。我们提出的方法的 MPJPE 分别为 45.0 mm 和 46.9 mm，可以与最先进的方法竞争，同时将推理时间减少 12 倍。这使得固定和移动应用中的可变消费硬件能够实现实时吞吐量。我们在 https://github.com/goldbricklemon/uplift-upsample-3dhpe 发布了我们的代码和模型

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer的3D人体姿态估计方法，通过时序稀疏2D姿态序列生成密集3D姿态估计，提升计算效率。
AI.motivation: 现有单目视频3D姿态估计方法依赖2D到3D的提升技术，但每帧2D姿态估计的计算复杂度较高。本文旨在通过降低输入2D姿态的采样率并利用Transformer进行时序上采样，显著减少计算量。
AI.method: 设计基于Transformer的时序上采样架构，利用掩码标记建模实现稀疏2D姿态到密集3D姿态的转换，并探索在大规模动作捕捉数据集上的预训练策略。
AI.result: 在Human3.6M和MPI-INF-3DHP数据集上分别达到45.0mm和46.9mm的MPJPE误差，推理速度较现有方法提升12倍，支持实时应用。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过解耦输入采样率与输出帧率，显著降低计算复杂度，结合预训练策略实现高效实时的3D姿态估计，并开源代码与模型。

authors_text: Xuan Gao,Chenglai Zhong,Jun Xiang,Yang Hong,Yudong Guo,Juyong Zhang
title: Reconstructing Personalized Semantic Facial NeRF Models From Monocular Video
title_zh: 从单目视频重建个性化语义面部 NeRF 模型
comment: Accepted by SIGGRAPH Asia 2022 (Journal Track). Project page: https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/
comments: Accepted by SIGGRAPH Asia 2022 (Journal Track). Project page: https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/
abstract: We present a novel semantic model for human head defined with neural radiance field. The 3D-consistent head model consist of a set of disentangled and interpretable bases, and can be driven by low-dimensional expression coefficients. Thanks to the powerful representation ability of neural radiance field, the constructed model can represent complex facial attributes including hair, wearings, which can not be represented by traditional mesh blendshape. To construct the personalized semantic facial model, we propose to define the bases as several multi-level voxel fields. With a short monocular RGB video as input, our method can construct the subject's semantic facial NeRF model with only ten to twenty minutes, and can render a photo-realistic human head image in tens of miliseconds with a given expression coefficient and view direction. With this novel representation, we apply it to many tasks like facial retargeting and expression editing. Experimental results demonstrate its strong representation ability and training/inference speed. Demo videos and released code are provided in our project page: https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/
summary: We present a novel semantic model for human head defined with neural radiance field. The 3D-consistent head model consist of a set of disentangled and interpretable bases, and can be driven by low-dimensional expression coefficients. Thanks to the powerful representation ability of neural radiance field, the constructed model can represent complex facial attributes including hair, wearings, which can not be represented by traditional mesh blendshape. To construct the personalized semantic facial model, we propose to define the bases as several multi-level voxel fields. With a short monocular RGB video as input, our method can construct the subject's semantic facial NeRF model with only ten to twenty minutes, and can render a photo-realistic human head image in tens of miliseconds with a given expression coefficient and view direction. With this novel representation, we apply it to many tasks like facial retargeting and expression editing. Experimental results demonstrate its strong representation ability and training/inference speed. Demo videos and released code are provided in our project page: https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用神经辐射场定义的新型人体头部语义模型。 3D一致头部模型由一组解开且可解释的基础组成，并且可以由低维表达系数驱动。得益于神经辐射场强大的表示能力，所构建的模型可以表示传统网格混合形状无法表示的复杂面部属性，包括头发、穿着等。为了构建个性化语义面部模型，我们建议将基础定义为多个多级体素字段。以短单目 RGB 视频作为输入，我们的方法只需 10 到 20 分钟即可构建主体的语义面部 NeRF 模型，并可以在给定表情系数和视图方向的情况下在数十毫秒内渲染出逼真的人体头部图像。通过这种新颖的表示，我们将其应用于许多任务，例如面部重定向和表情编辑。实验结果证明了其强大的表示能力和训练/推理速度。我们的项目页面提供了演示视频和发布的代码：https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经辐射场（NeRF）的个性化语义人脸建模方法，仅需单目视频即可快速生成可驱动的3D人脸模型。
AI.motivation: 传统网格混合形状模型难以表达复杂面部属性（如头发、服饰），且个性化建模效率低，亟需更高效且具表现力的面部建模方案。
AI.method: 定义多级体素场作为解耦语义基底，结合NeRF的强表达能力，通过单目视频输入实现端到端训练，利用低维表情系数驱动模型生成逼真人脸。
AI.result: 模型可在10-20分钟内完成个性化建模，渲染速度达毫秒级，支持面部重定向和表情编辑等任务，实验验证了其表征能力与效率优势。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过NeRF与体素场结合，实现了高效、高精度的个性化语义人脸建模，为面部动画与虚拟人应用提供了新思路。

search_text: Reconstructing Personalized Semantic Facial NeRF Models From Monocular Video 从单目视频重建个性化语义面部 NeRF 模型 We present a novel semantic model for human head defined with neural radiance field. The 3D-consistent head model consist of a set of disentangled and interpretable bases, and can be driven by low-dimensional expression coefficients. Thanks to the powerful representation ability of neural radiance field, the constructed model can represent complex facial attributes including hair, wearings, which can not be represented by traditional mesh blendshape. To construct the personalized semantic facial model, we propose to define the bases as several multi-level voxel fields. With a short monocular RGB video as input, our method can construct the subject's semantic facial NeRF model with only ten to twenty minutes, and can render a photo-realistic human head image in tens of miliseconds with a given expression coefficient and view direction. With this novel representation, we apply it to many tasks like facial retargeting and expression editing. Experimental results demonstrate its strong representation ability and training/inference speed. Demo videos and released code are provided in our project page: https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/ 我们提出了一种用神经辐射场定义的新型人体头部语义模型。 3D一致头部模型由一组解开且可解释的基础组成，并且可以由低维表达系数驱动。得益于神经辐射场强大的表示能力，所构建的模型可以表示传统网格混合形状无法表示的复杂面部属性，包括头发、穿着等。为了构建个性化语义面部模型，我们建议将基础定义为多个多级体素字段。以短单目 RGB 视频作为输入，我们的方法只需 10 到 20 分钟即可构建主体的语义面部 NeRF 模型，并可以在给定表情系数和视图方向的情况下在数十毫秒内渲染出逼真的人体头部图像。通过这种新颖的表示，我们将其应用于许多任务，例如面部重定向和表情编辑。实验结果证明了其强大的表示能力和训练/推理速度。我们的项目页面提供了演示视频和发布的代码：https://ustc3dv.github.io/NeRFBlendShape/ 本文提出一种基于神经辐射场（NeRF）的个性化语义人脸建模方法，仅需单目视频即可快速生成可驱动的3D人脸模型。 Xuan Gao,Chenglai Zhong,Jun Xiang,Yang Hong,Yudong Guo,Juyong Zhang cs.GR,cs.CV

authors_text: Anthony Knittel,Majd Hawasly,Stefano V. Albrecht,John Redford,Subramanian Ramamoorthy
title: DiPA: Probabilistic Multi-Modal Interactive Prediction for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: DiPA：自动驾驶概率多模态交互式预测
comment: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 8, no. 8, pp. 4887-4894, Aug. 2023
comments: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 8, no. 8, pp. 4887-4894, Aug. 2023
abstract: Accurate prediction is important for operating an autonomous vehicle in interactive scenarios. Prediction must be fast, to support multiple requests from a planner exploring a range of possible futures. The generated predictions must accurately represent the probabilities of predicted trajectories, while also capturing different modes of behaviour (such as turning left vs continuing straight at a junction). To this end, we present DiPA, an interactive predictor that addresses these challenging requirements. Previous interactive prediction methods use an encoding of k-mode-samples, which under-represents the full distribution. Other methods optimise closest-mode evaluations, which test whether one of the predictions is similar to the ground-truth, but allow additional unlikely predictions to occur, over-representing unlikely predictions. DiPA addresses these limitations by using a Gaussian-Mixture-Model to encode the full distribution, and optimising predictions using both probabilistic and closest-mode measures. These objectives respectively optimise probabilistic accuracy and the ability to capture distinct behaviours, and there is a challenging trade-off between them. We are able to solve both together using a novel training regime. DiPA achieves new state-of-the-art performance on the INTERACTION and NGSIM datasets, and improves over the baseline (MFP) when both closest-mode and probabilistic evaluations are used. This demonstrates effective prediction for supporting a planner on interactive scenarios.

Prediction must be fast, to support multiple requests from a planner exploring a range of possible futures. The generated predictions must accurately represent the probabilities of predicted trajectories, while also capturing different modes of behaviour (such as turning left vs continuing straight at a junction). To this end, we present DiPA, an interactive predictor that addresses these challenging requirements. Previous interactive prediction methods use an encoding of k-mode-samples, which under-represents the full distribution. Other methods optimise closest-mode evaluations, which test whether one of the predictions is similar to the ground-truth, but allow additional unlikely predictions to occur, over-representing unlikely predictions. DiPA addresses these limitations by using a Gaussian-Mixture-Model to encode the full distribution, and optimising predictions using both probabilistic and closest-mode measures. These objectives respectively optimise probabilistic accuracy and the ability to capture distinct behaviours, and there is a challenging trade-off between them. We are able to solve both together using a novel training regime. DiPA achieves new state-of-the-art performance on the INTERACTION and NGSIM datasets, and improves over the baseline (MFP) when both closest-mode and probabilistic evaluations are used. This demonstrates effective prediction for supporting a planner on interactive scenarios.
abstract_zh: 准确的预测对于在交互式场景中操作自动驾驶车辆非常重要。预测必须快速，以支持规划者探索一系列可能的未来的多个请求。生成的预测必须准确表示预测轨迹的概率，同时还捕获不同的行为模式（例如在路口左转与继续直行）。为此，我们推出了 DiPA，这是一种交互式预测器，可以解决这些具有挑战性的要求。以前的交互式预测方法使用 k 模式样本的编码，这不足以代表完整的分布。其他方法优化最接近模式评估，测试其中一个预测是否与真实情况相似，但允许发生额外的不太可能的预测，过度代表不太可能的预测。 DiPA 通过使用高斯混合模型对完整分布进行编码，并使用概率和最近模式测量来优化预测，从而解决了这些限制。这些目标分别优化概率准确性和捕获不同行为的能力，并且它们之间存在具有挑战性的权衡。我们能够使用一种新颖的训练机制来同时解决这两个问题。 DiPA 在 INTERACTION 和 NGSIM 数据集上实现了新的最先进的性能，并且在使用最近模式和概率评估时相对于基线 (MFP) 有所改进。这证明了支持交互式场景规划者的有效预测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DiPA方法，用于自动驾驶场景中的多模态交互预测，通过概率模型同时优化轨迹预测的准确性和行为模式多样性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在交互预测中存在分布表示不足或过度生成低概率预测的问题，需开发能同时满足概率准确性和多行为模式捕捉的预测模型。
AI.method: 采用高斯混合模型编码完整轨迹分布，结合概率损失与最近模式损失进行联合优化，并设计新型训练策略平衡两者目标。
AI.result: 在INTERACTION和NGSIM数据集上达到SOTA性能，相比基线方法在概率评估和最近模式评估中均表现更优。
AI.conclusion: DiPA通过概率与模式双目标优化有效解决了交互预测中的分布表示难题，为自动驾驶规划器提供了可靠的支持。

authors_text: Piotr Kawa,Marcin Plata,Piotr Syga
title: SpecRNet: Towards Faster and More Accessible Audio DeepFake Detection
title_zh: SpecRNet：迈向更快、更容易访问的音频 DeepFake 检测
comment: Accepted by TrustCom 2022: The 21st IEEE International Conference on Trust, Security and Privacy in Computing and Communications
comments: Accepted by TrustCom 2022: The 21st IEEE International Conference on Trust, Security and Privacy in Computing and Communications
abstract: Audio DeepFakes are utterances generated with the use of deep neural networks. They are highly misleading and pose a threat due to use in fake news, impersonation, or extortion. In this work, we focus on increasing accessibility to the audio DeepFake detection methods by providing SpecRNet, a neural network architecture characterized by a quick inference time and low computational requirements. Our benchmark shows that SpecRNet, requiring up to about 40% less time to process an audio sample, provides performance comparable to LCNN architecture - one of the best audio DeepFake detection models. Such a method can not only be used by online multimedia services to verify a large bulk of content uploaded daily but also, thanks to its low requirements, by average citizens to evaluate materials on their devices. In addition, we provide benchmarks in three unique settings that confirm the correctness of our model. They reflect scenarios of low-resource datasets, detection on short utterances and limited attacks benchmark in which we take a closer look at the influence of particular attacks on given architectures.
summary: Audio DeepFakes are utterances generated with the use of deep neural networks. They are highly misleading and pose a threat due to use in fake news, impersonation, or extortion. In this work, we focus on increasing accessibility to the audio DeepFake detection methods by providing SpecRNet, a neural network architecture characterized by a quick inference time and low computational requirements. Our benchmark shows that SpecRNet, requiring up to about 40% less time to process an audio sample, provides performance comparable to LCNN architecture - one of the best audio DeepFake detection models. Such a method can not only be used by online multimedia services to verify a large bulk of content uploaded daily but also, thanks to its low requirements, by average citizens to evaluate materials on their devices. In addition, we provide benchmarks in three unique settings that confirm the correctness of our model. They reflect scenarios of low-resource datasets, detection on short utterances and limited attacks benchmark in which we take a closer look at the influence of particular attacks on given architectures.
abstract_zh: 音频 DeepFakes 是使用深度神经网络生成的话语。它们具有高度误导性，并因用于虚假新闻、冒充或勒索而构成威胁。在这项工作中，我们专注于通过提供 SpecRNet（一种具有快速推理时间和低计算要求的神经网络架构）来提高音频 DeepFake 检测方法的可访问性。我们的基准测试表明，SpecRNet 处理音频样本所需的时间减少了约 40%，其性能可与 LCNN 架构（最好的音频 DeepFake 检测模型之一）相媲美。这种方法不仅可以被在线多媒体服务用来验证每天上传的大量内容，而且由于其要求较低，普通公民也可以在他们的设备上评估材料。此外，我们还提供了三种独特设置的基准，以确认我们模型的正确性。它们反映了低资源数据集、短话语检测和有限攻击基准的场景，在这些基准中，我们仔细研究了特定攻击对给定架构的影响。

AI.tldr: 提出一种名为SpecRNet的神经网络架构，用于实现更快速且低资源消耗的音频深度伪造检测。
AI.motivation: 音频深度伪造技术存在安全隐患，但现有检测方法计算成本高且难以普及，需开发更高效且适用于普通用户的检测方案。
AI.method: 设计SpecRNet架构，通过优化模型结构降低计算需求，同时在三种特定场景（低资源数据集、短音频检测、有限攻击基准）中验证其有效性。
AI.result: SpecRNet在保持与LCNN（当前最佳模型）相近检测性能的同时，推理速度提升约40%，且适用于普通设备与大规模内容审核场景。
AI.conclusion: SpecRNet通过高效计算与低资源需求，显著提升了音频深度伪造检测的可访问性，为实际应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Yatin Chaudhary,Pranav Rai,Matthias Schubert,Hinrich Schütze,Pankaj Gupta
title: Federated Continual Learning for Text Classification via Selective Inter-client Transfer
title_zh: 通过选择性客户端间传输进行文本分类的联合持续学习
abstract: In this work, we combine the two paradigms: Federated Learning (FL) and Continual Learning (CL) for text classification task in cloud-edge continuum. The objective of Federated Continual Learning (FCL) is to improve deep learning models over life time at each client by (relevant and efficient) knowledge transfer without sharing data. Here, we address challenges in minimizing inter-client interference while knowledge sharing due to heterogeneous tasks across clients in FCL setup. In doing so, we propose a novel framework, Federated Selective Inter-client Transfer (FedSeIT) which selectively combines model parameters of foreign clients. To further maximize knowledge transfer, we assess domain overlap and select informative tasks from the sequence of historical tasks at each foreign client while preserving privacy. Evaluating against the baselines, we show improved performance, a gain of (average) 12.4\% in text classification over a sequence of tasks using five datasets from diverse domains. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that applies FCL to NLP.
summary: In this work, we combine the two paradigms: Federated Learning (FL) and Continual Learning (CL) for text classification task in cloud-edge continuum. The objective of Federated Continual Learning (FCL) is to improve deep learning models over life time at each client by (relevant and efficient) knowledge transfer without sharing data. Here, we address challenges in minimizing inter-client interference while knowledge sharing due to heterogeneous tasks across clients in FCL setup. In doing so, we propose a novel framework, Federated Selective Inter-client Transfer (FedSeIT) which selectively combines model parameters of foreign clients. To further maximize knowledge transfer, we assess domain overlap and select informative tasks from the sequence of historical tasks at each foreign client while preserving privacy. Evaluating against the baselines, we show improved performance, a gain of (average) 12.4\% in text classification over a sequence of tasks using five datasets from diverse domains. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that applies FCL to NLP.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们结合了两种范式：联邦学习（FL）和持续学习（CL），用于云边缘连续体中的文本分类任务。联合持续学习 (FCL) 的目标是通过（相关且高效的）知识转移而不共享数据，在每个客户的生命周期中改进深度学习模型。在这里，我们解决了在 FCL 设置中由于客户端之间的异构任务而导致的知识共享时最大限度地减少客户端间干扰的挑战。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，即联合选择性客户端间传输（FedSeIT），它有选择地结合外国客户端的模型参数。为了进一步最大化知识转移，我们评估领域重叠，并从每个外国客户的历史任务序列中选择信息丰富的任务，同时保护隐私。根据基线进行评估，我们展示了性能的提高，在使用来自不同领域的五个数据集的一系列任务中，文本分类的增益（平均）为 12.4%。据我们所知，这是第一个将 FCL 应用到 NLP 的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为FedSeIT的联邦持续学习框架，用于在无需共享数据的情况下，通过选择性跨客户端知识转移提升文本分类任务的模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统联邦学习（FL）和持续学习（CL）在处理异构任务和隐私保护方面存在挑战，需在云-边协同场景中实现高效知识共享与模型优化。
AI.method: 设计FedSeIT框架，通过评估领域重叠度选择性整合其他客户端的模型参数，保留隐私的同时最大化知识转移效率。
AI.result: 在五个不同领域的数据集上，文本分类任务的平均性能提升12.4%，优于现有基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 首次将联邦持续学习（FCL）应用于自然语言处理（NLP），验证了跨客户端选择性知识转移在隐私保护下的有效性。

search_text: Federated Continual Learning for Text Classification via Selective Inter-client Transfer 通过选择性客户端间传输进行文本分类的联合持续学习 In this work, we combine the two paradigms: Federated Learning (FL) and Continual Learning (CL) for text classification task in cloud-edge continuum. The objective of Federated Continual Learning (FCL) is to improve deep learning models over life time at each client by (relevant and efficient) knowledge transfer without sharing data. Here, we address challenges in minimizing inter-client interference while knowledge sharing due to heterogeneous tasks across clients in FCL setup. In doing so, we propose a novel framework, Federated Selective Inter-client Transfer (FedSeIT) which selectively combines model parameters of foreign clients. To further maximize knowledge transfer, we assess domain overlap and select informative tasks from the sequence of historical tasks at each foreign client while preserving privacy. Evaluating against the baselines, we show improved performance, a gain of (average) 12.4\% in text classification over a sequence of tasks using five datasets from diverse domains. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that applies FCL to NLP. 在这项工作中，我们结合了两种范式：联邦学习（FL）和持续学习（CL），用于云边缘连续体中的文本分类任务。联合持续学习 (FCL) 的目标是通过（相关且高效的）知识转移而不共享数据，在每个客户的生命周期中改进深度学习模型。在这里，我们解决了在 FCL 设置中由于客户端之间的异构任务而导致的知识共享时最大限度地减少客户端间干扰的挑战。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，即联合选择性客户端间传输（FedSeIT），它有选择地结合外国客户端的模型参数。为了进一步最大化知识转移，我们评估领域重叠，并从每个外国客户的历史任务序列中选择信息丰富的任务，同时保护隐私。根据基线进行评估，我们展示了性能的提高，在使用来自不同领域的五个数据集的一系列任务中，文本分类的增益（平均）为 12.4%。据我们所知，这是第一个将 FCL 应用到 NLP 的工作。 本文提出一种名为FedSeIT的联邦持续学习框架，用于在无需共享数据的情况下，通过选择性跨客户端知识转移提升文本分类任务的模型性能。 Yatin Chaudhary,Pranav Rai,Matthias Schubert,Hinrich Schütze,Pankaj Gupta cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Xinyu Sun,Peihao Chen,Liangwei Chen,Changhao Li,Thomas H. Li,Mingkui Tan,Chuang Gan
title: Masked Motion Encoding for Self-Supervised Video Representation Learning
title_zh: 用于自监督视频表示学习的掩码运动编码
abstract: How to learn discriminative video representation from unlabeled videos is challenging but crucial for video analysis. The latest attempts seek to learn a representation model by predicting the appearance contents in the masked regions. However, simply masking and recovering appearance contents may not be sufficient to model temporal clues as the appearance contents can be easily reconstructed from a single frame. To overcome this limitation, we present Masked Motion Encoding (MME), a new pre-training paradigm that reconstructs both appearance and motion information to explore temporal clues. In MME, we focus on addressing two critical challenges to improve the representation performance: 1) how to well represent the possible long-term motion across multiple frames; and 2) how to obtain fine-grained temporal clues from sparsely sampled videos. Motivated by the fact that human is able to recognize an action by tracking objects' position changes and shape changes, we propose to reconstruct a motion trajectory that represents these two kinds of change in the masked regions. Besides, given the sparse video input, we enforce the model to reconstruct dense motion trajectories in both spatial and temporal dimensions. Pre-trained with our MME paradigm, the model is able to anticipate long-term and fine-grained motion details. Code is available at https://github.com/XinyuSun/MME.
summary: How to learn discriminative video representation from unlabeled videos is challenging but crucial for video analysis. The latest attempts seek to learn a representation model by predicting the appearance contents in the masked regions. However, simply masking and recovering appearance contents may not be sufficient to model temporal clues as the appearance contents can be easily reconstructed from a single frame. To overcome this limitation, we present Masked Motion Encoding (MME), a new pre-training paradigm that reconstructs both appearance and motion information to explore temporal clues. In MME, we focus on addressing two critical challenges to improve the representation performance: 1) how to well represent the possible long-term motion across multiple frames; and 2) how to obtain fine-grained temporal clues from sparsely sampled videos. Motivated by the fact that human is able to recognize an action by tracking objects' position changes and shape changes, we propose to reconstruct a motion trajectory that represents these two kinds of change in the masked regions. Besides, given the sparse video input, we enforce the model to reconstruct dense motion trajectories in both spatial and temporal dimensions. Pre-trained with our MME paradigm, the model is able to anticipate long-term and fine-grained motion details. Code is available at https://github.com/XinyuSun/MME.
abstract_zh: 如何从未标记的视频中学习有区别的视频表示虽然具有挑战性，但对于视频分析至关重要。最新的尝试试图通过预测屏蔽区域中的外观内容来学习表示模型。然而，简单地屏蔽和恢复外观内容可能不足以对时间线索进行建模，因为外观内容可以很容易地从单个帧重建。为了克服这一限制，我们提出了掩蔽运动编码（MME），这是一种新的预训练范例，可以重建外观和运动信息以探索时间线索。在MME中，我们专注于解决两个关键挑战以提高表示性能：1）如何很好地表示跨多个帧可能的长期运动； 2）如何从稀疏采样的视频中获取细粒度的时间线索。受人类能够通过跟踪物体的位置变化和形状变化来识别动作这一事实的启发，我们建议重建代表遮蔽区域中这两种变化的运动轨迹。此外，考虑到稀疏的视频输入，我们强制模型在空间和时间维度上重建密集的运动轨迹。该模型经过我们的 MME 范式预先训练，能够预测长期且细粒度的运动细节。代码可在 https://github.com/XinyuSun/MME 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Masked Motion Encoding (MME)的新预训练范式，用于从未标记视频中学习具有判别性的视频表示。
AI.motivation: 现有方法仅通过预测遮罩区域的外观内容来学习视频表示，可能不足以捕捉时间线索。因此，本文旨在通过同时重建外观和运动信息，以更好地建模时间线索。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种新的预训练范式MME，通过重建遮罩区域的运动轨迹来捕捉物体的位置和形状变化，并在稀疏采样的视频中强制模型重建时空维度上的密集运动轨迹。
AI.result: 使用MME范式预训练的模型能够预测长期和细粒度的运动细节，并在多个视频任务中取得了良好的性能。
AI.conclusion: MME通过重建运动轨迹，有效捕捉了视频中的时间线索，从而提升了视频表示的学习效果。

authors_text: Achraf Ben-Hamadou,Nour Neifar,Ahmed Rekik,Oussama Smaoui,Firas Bouzguenda,Sergi Pujades,Edmond Boyer,Edouard Ladroit
title: Teeth3DS+: An Extended Benchmark for Intraoral 3D Scans Analysis
title_zh: Teeth3DS+：口内 3D 扫描分析的扩展基准
abstract: Intraoral 3D scanning is now widely adopted in modern dentistry and plays a central role in supporting key tasks such as tooth segmentation, detection, labeling, and dental landmark identification. Accurate analysis of these scans is essential for orthodontic and restorative treatment planning, as it enables automated workflows and minimizes the need for manual intervention. However, the development of robust learning-based solutions remains challenging due to the limited availability of high-quality public datasets and standardized benchmarks. This article presents Teeth3DS+, an extended public benchmark dedicated to intraoral 3D scan analysis. Developed in the context of the MICCAI 3DTeethSeg and 3DTeethLand challenges, Teeth3DS+ supports multiple fundamental tasks, including tooth detection, segmentation, labeling, 3D modeling, and dental landmark identification. The dataset consists of rigorously curated intraoral scans acquired using state-of-the-art scanners and validated by experienced orthodontists and dental surgeons. In addition to the data, Teeth3DS+ provides standardized data splits and evaluation protocols to enable fair and reproducible comparison of methods, with the goal of fostering progress in learning-based analysis of 3D dental scans. Detailed instructions for accessing the dataset are available at https://crns-smartvision.github.io/teeth3ds
summary: Intraoral 3D scanning is now widely adopted in modern dentistry and plays a central role in supporting key tasks such as tooth segmentation, detection, labeling, and dental landmark identification. Accurate analysis of these scans is essential for orthodontic and restorative treatment planning, as it enables automated workflows and minimizes the need for manual intervention. However, the development of robust learning-based solutions remains challenging due to the limited availability of high-quality public datasets and standardized benchmarks. This article presents Teeth3DS+, an extended public benchmark dedicated to intraoral 3D scan analysis. Developed in the context of the MICCAI 3DTeethSeg and 3DTeethLand challenges, Teeth3DS+ supports multiple fundamental tasks, including tooth detection, segmentation, labeling, 3D modeling, and dental landmark identification. The dataset consists of rigorously curated intraoral scans acquired using state-of-the-art scanners and validated by experienced orthodontists and dental surgeons. In addition to the data, Teeth3DS+ provides standardized data splits and evaluation protocols to enable fair and reproducible comparison of methods, with the goal of fostering progress in learning-based analysis of 3D dental scans. Detailed instructions for accessing the dataset are available at https://crns-smartvision.github.io/teeth3ds
abstract_zh: 口内 3D 扫描现已在现代牙科中广泛采用，在支持牙齿分割、检测、标记和牙科标志识别等关键任务中发挥着核心作用。对这些扫描的准确分析对于正畸和修复治疗计划至关重要，因为它可以实现自动化工作流程并最大限度地减少手动干预的需要。然而，由于高质量公共数据集和标准化基准的可用性有限，开发基于学习的强大解决方案仍然具有挑战性。本文介绍 Teeth3DS+，这是一个专用于口腔内 3D 扫描分析的扩展公共基准。 Teeth3DS+ 是在 MICCAI 3DTeethSeg 和 3DTeethLand 挑战的背景下开发的，支持多种基本任务，包括牙齿检测、分割、标记、3D 建模和牙齿标志识别。该数据集由经过严格管理的口内扫描组成，这些扫描是使用最先进的扫描仪获取的，并由经验丰富的正牙医生和牙外科医生进行验证。除了数据之外，Teeth3DS+ 还提供标准化的数据分割和评估协议，以实现公平且可重复的方法比较，其目标是促进基于学习的 3D 牙科扫描分析的进展。有关访问数据集的详细说明，请访问 https://crns-smartvision.github.io/teeth3ds

AI.tldr: 本文提出Teeth3DS+，一个用于牙科内窥镜3D扫描分析的扩展基准数据集，支持牙齿分割、检测、标注及牙科标志点识别等任务。
AI.motivation: 现有公开高质量牙科3D扫描数据集不足，标准化基准缺失，限制了基于学习方法的开发与比较。
AI.method: 通过先进扫描仪获取并由正畸专家验证的严格筛选数据，构建包含多种任务的数据集，并提供标准化数据划分与评估协议。
AI.result: 发布Teeth3DS+数据集及评估框架，涵盖牙齿检测、分割、建模等任务，促进算法公平比较与研究进展。
AI.conclusion: Teeth3DS+为牙科3D扫描的自动化分析提供标准化基准，推动基于学习方法的临床应用发展。

search_text: Teeth3DS+: An Extended Benchmark for Intraoral 3D Scans Analysis Teeth3DS+：口内 3D 扫描分析的扩展基准 Intraoral 3D scanning is now widely adopted in modern dentistry and plays a central role in supporting key tasks such as tooth segmentation, detection, labeling, and dental landmark identification. Accurate analysis of these scans is essential for orthodontic and restorative treatment planning, as it enables automated workflows and minimizes the need for manual intervention. However, the development of robust learning-based solutions remains challenging due to the limited availability of high-quality public datasets and standardized benchmarks. This article presents Teeth3DS+, an extended public benchmark dedicated to intraoral 3D scan analysis. Developed in the context of the MICCAI 3DTeethSeg and 3DTeethLand challenges, Teeth3DS+ supports multiple fundamental tasks, including tooth detection, segmentation, labeling, 3D modeling, and dental landmark identification. The dataset consists of rigorously curated intraoral scans acquired using state-of-the-art scanners and validated by experienced orthodontists and dental surgeons. In addition to the data, Teeth3DS+ provides standardized data splits and evaluation protocols to enable fair and reproducible comparison of methods, with the goal of fostering progress in learning-based analysis of 3D dental scans. Detailed instructions for accessing the dataset are available at https://crns-smartvision.github.io/teeth3ds 口内 3D 扫描现已在现代牙科中广泛采用，在支持牙齿分割、检测、标记和牙科标志识别等关键任务中发挥着核心作用。对这些扫描的准确分析对于正畸和修复治疗计划至关重要，因为它可以实现自动化工作流程并最大限度地减少手动干预的需要。然而，由于高质量公共数据集和标准化基准的可用性有限，开发基于学习的强大解决方案仍然具有挑战性。本文介绍 Teeth3DS+，这是一个专用于口腔内 3D 扫描分析的扩展公共基准。 Teeth3DS+ 是在 MICCAI 3DTeethSeg 和 3DTeethLand 挑战的背景下开发的，支持多种基本任务，包括牙齿检测、分割、标记、3D 建模和牙齿标志识别。该数据集由经过严格管理的口内扫描组成，这些扫描是使用最先进的扫描仪获取的，并由经验丰富的正牙医生和牙外科医生进行验证。除了数据之外，Teeth3DS+ 还提供标准化的数据分割和评估协议，以实现公平且可重复的方法比较，其目标是促进基于学习的 3D 牙科扫描分析的进展。有关访问数据集的详细说明，请访问 https://crns-smartvision.github.io/teeth3ds 本文提出Teeth3DS+，一个用于牙科内窥镜3D扫描分析的扩展基准数据集，支持牙齿分割、检测、标注及牙科标志点识别等任务。 Achraf Ben-Hamadou,Nour Neifar,Ahmed Rekik,Oussama Smaoui,Firas Bouzguenda,Sergi Pujades,Edmond Boyer,Edouard Ladroit cs.CV

authors_text: Shuhao Deng,Chengfei Li,Jinfeng Bai,Qingqing Zhang,Wei-Qiang Zhang,Runyan Yang,Gaofeng Cheng,Pengyuan Zhang,Yonghong Yan
title: Summary on the ISCSLP 2022 Chinese-English Code-Switching ASR Challenge
title_zh: ISCSLP 2022中英语码转换ASR挑战赛总结
abstract: Code-switching automatic speech recognition becomes one of the most challenging and the most valuable scenarios of automatic speech recognition, due to the code-switching phenomenon between multilingual language and the frequent occurrence of code-switching phenomenon in daily life. The ISCSLP 2022 Chinese-English Code-Switching Automatic Speech Recognition (CSASR) Challenge aims to promote the development of code-switching automatic speech recognition. The ISCSLP 2022 CSASR challenge provided two training sets, TAL_CSASR corpus and MagicData-RAMC corpus, a development and a test set for participants, which are used for CSASR model training and evaluation. Along with the challenge, we also provide the baseline system performance for reference. As a result, more than 40 teams participated in this challenge, and the winner team achieved 16.70% Mixture Error Rate (MER) performance on the test set and has achieved 9.8% MER absolute improvement compared with the baseline system. In this paper, we will describe the datasets, the associated baselines system and the requirements, and summarize the CSASR challenge results and major techniques and tricks used in the submitted systems.
summary: Code-switching automatic speech recognition becomes one of the most challenging and the most valuable scenarios of automatic speech recognition, due to the code-switching phenomenon between multilingual language and the frequent occurrence of code-switching phenomenon in daily life. The ISCSLP 2022 Chinese-English Code-Switching Automatic Speech Recognition (CSASR) Challenge aims to promote the development of code-switching automatic speech recognition. The ISCSLP 2022 CSASR challenge provided two training sets, TAL_CSASR corpus and MagicData-RAMC corpus, a development and a test set for participants, which are used for CSASR model training and evaluation. Along with the challenge, we also provide the baseline system performance for reference. As a result, more than 40 teams participated in this challenge, and the winner team achieved 16.70% Mixture Error Rate (MER) performance on the test set and has achieved 9.8% MER absolute improvement compared with the baseline system. In this paper, we will describe the datasets, the associated baselines system and the requirements, and summarize the CSASR challenge results and major techniques and tricks used in the submitted systems.
abstract_zh: 由于多语言之间的语码转换现象以及日常生活中语码转换现象的频繁发生，语码转换自动语音识别成为自动语音识别中最具挑战性和最有价值的场景之一。 ISCSLP 2022中英语码转换自动语音识别（CSASR）挑战赛旨在推动语码转换自动语音识别的发展。 ISCSLP 2022 CSASR挑战赛为参赛者提供了TAL_CSASR语料库和MagicData-RAMC语料库两个训练集、一个开发集和一个测试集，用于CSASR模型的训练和评估。除了挑战之外，我们还提供基准系统性能以供参考。结果，超过 40 个团队参与了本次挑战，获胜团队在测试集上实现了 16.70% 的混合错误率 (MER) 性能，并且与基线系统相比实现了 9.8% 的 MER 绝对改进。在本文中，我们将描述数据集、相关基线系统和要求，并总结 CSASR 挑战结果以及提交系统中使用的主要技术和技巧。

AI.tldr: 组织并总结ISCSLP 2022中英代码切换语音识别挑战，包括数据集、基线系统及参赛结果。
AI.motivation: 代码切换现象在日常生活中频繁出现，推动对中英代码切换语音识别技术的研究与优化。
AI.method: 提供两个训练数据集（TAL_CSASR和MagicData-RAMC）及开发/测试集，结合基线系统供参赛者训练与评估模型。
AI.result: 冠军团队在测试集上达到16.70% MER，较基线系统提升9.8%绝对值。
AI.conclusion: 总结挑战赛结果及主要技术，推动代码切换语音识别技术发展，未来需进一步优化模型与数据集。

authors_text: Ruiyuan Kang,Dimitrios C. Kyritsis,Panos Liatsis
title: Self-Validated Physics-Embedding Network: A General Framework for Inverse Modelling
title_zh: 自验证物理嵌入网络：逆向建模的通用框架
abstract: Physics-based inverse modeling techniques are typically restricted to particular research fields, whereas popular machine-learning-based ones are too data-dependent to guarantee the physical compatibility of the solution. In this paper, Self-Validated Physics-Embedding Network (SVPEN), a general neural network framework for inverse modeling is proposed. As its name suggests, the embedded physical forward model ensures that any solution that successfully passes its validation is physically reasonable. SVPEN operates in two modes: (a) the inverse function mode offers rapid state estimation as conventional supervised learning, and (b) the optimization mode offers a way to iteratively correct estimations that fail the validation process. Furthermore, the optimization mode provides SVPEN with reconfigurability i.e., replacing components like neural networks, physical models, and error calculations at will to solve a series of distinct inverse problems without pretraining. More than ten case studies in two highly nonlinear and entirely distinct applications: molecular absorption spectroscopy and Turbofan cycle analysis, demonstrate the generality, physical reliability, and reconfigurability of SVPEN. More importantly, SVPEN offers a solid foundation to use existing physical models within the context of AI, so as to striking a balance between data-driven and physics-driven models.
summary: Physics-based inverse modeling techniques are typically restricted to particular research fields, whereas popular machine-learning-based ones are too data-dependent to guarantee the physical compatibility of the solution. In this paper, Self-Validated Physics-Embedding Network (SVPEN), a general neural network framework for inverse modeling is proposed. As its name suggests, the embedded physical forward model ensures that any solution that successfully passes its validation is physically reasonable. SVPEN operates in two modes: (a) the inverse function mode offers rapid state estimation as conventional supervised learning, and (b) the optimization mode offers a way to iteratively correct estimations that fail the validation process. Furthermore, the optimization mode provides SVPEN with reconfigurability i.e., replacing components like neural networks, physical models, and error calculations at will to solve a series of distinct inverse problems without pretraining. More than ten case studies in two highly nonlinear and entirely distinct applications: molecular absorption spectroscopy and Turbofan cycle analysis, demonstrate the generality, physical reliability, and reconfigurability of SVPEN. More importantly, SVPEN offers a solid foundation to use existing physical models within the context of AI, so as to striking a balance between data-driven and physics-driven models.
abstract_zh: 基于物理的逆向建模技术通常仅限于特定的研究领域，而流行的基于机器学习的技术则过于依赖数据，无法保证解决方案的物理兼容性。本文提出了自验证物理嵌入网络（SVPEN），这是一种用于逆向建模的通用神经网络框架。顾名思义，嵌入式物理正向模型可确保任何成功通过验证的解决方案在物理上都是合理的。 SVPEN 以两种模式运行：(a) 反函数模式提供与传统监督学习一样的快速状态估计，(b) 优化模式提供了一种迭代纠正验证过程失败的估计的方法。此外，优化模式为SVPEN提供了可重构性，即随意更换神经网络、物理模型、误差计算等组件，无需预训练即可解决一系列不同的逆问题。两个高度非线性且完全不同的应用（分子吸收光谱和涡轮风扇循环分析）的十多个案例研究证明了 SVPEN 的通用性、物理可靠性和可重构性。更重要的是，SVPEN 为在人工智能背景下使用现有物理模型提供了坚实的基础，从而在数据驱动和物理驱动模型之间取得平衡。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合物理模型与机器学习的通用逆向建模框架SVPEN，解决传统方法在物理兼容性与数据依赖性之间的矛盾。
AI.motivation: 传统物理逆向建模方法局限于特定领域且难以保证解的物理合理性，而主流机器学习方法过度依赖数据且缺乏物理约束。
AI.method: 设计包含物理前向模型验证模块的神经网络框架SVPEN，通过逆函数模式实现快速状态估计，并通过优化模式迭代修正无效解，支持组件动态替换以适应不同逆问题。
AI.result: 在分子吸收光谱与涡扇循环分析等两个高度非线性场景的十余个案例中验证了SVPEN的通用性、物理可靠性及可重构性。
AI.conclusion: SVPEN为在人工智能中集成物理模型提供了基础，有效平衡了数据驱动与物理驱动方法的优缺点。
