search_text: Image Projective Transformation Rectification with Synthetic Data for Smartphone-captured Chest X-ray Photos Classification 使用合成数据进行图像投影变换校正，用于智能手机捕获的胸部 X 射线照片分类 Classification on smartphone-captured chest X-ray (CXR) photos to detect pathologies is challenging due to the projective transformation caused by the non-ideal camera position. Recently, various rectification methods have been proposed for different photo rectification tasks such as document photos, license plate photos, etc. Unfortunately, we found that none of them is suitable for CXR photos, due to their specific transformation type, image appearance, annotation type, etc. In this paper, we propose an innovative deep learning-based Projective Transformation Rectification Network (PTRN) to automatically rectify CXR photos by predicting the projective transformation matrix. To the best of our knowledge, it is the first work to predict the projective transformation matrix as the learning goal for photo rectification. Additionally, to avoid the expensive collection of natural data, synthetic CXR photos are generated under the consideration of natural perturbations, extra screens, etc. We evaluate the proposed approach in the CheXphoto smartphone-captured CXR photos classification competition hosted by the Stanford University Machine Learning Group, our approach won first place with a huge performance improvement (ours 0.850, second-best 0.762, in AUC). A deeper study demonstrates that the use of PTRN successfully achieves the classification performance on the spatially transformed CXR photos to the same level as on the high-quality digital CXR images, indicating PTRN can eliminate all negative impacts of projective transformation on the CXR photos. 由于不理想的相机位置引起的投影变换，对智能手机拍摄的胸部 X 射线 (CXR) 照片进行分类以检测病理状况具有挑战性。最近，针对文档照片、车牌照片等不同的照片校正任务提出了各种校正方法。不幸的是，由于其特定的变换类型、图像外观、注释类型等，我们发现它们都不适合 CXR 照片。在本文中，我们提出了一种创新的基于深度学习的投影变换校正网络（PTRN），通过预测投影变换矩阵来自动校正 CXR 照片。据我们所知，这是第一个以预测投影变换矩阵作为照片校正的学习目标的工作。此外，为了避免昂贵的自然数据收集，在考虑自然扰动、额外屏幕等的情况下生成合成的 CXR 照片。我们在斯坦福大学机器学习小组主办的 CheXphoto 智能手机捕获的 CXR 照片分类竞赛中评估了所提出的方法，我们的方法以巨大的性能改进赢得了第一名（我们的 0.850，第二好的 0.762，AUC）。更深入的研究表明，使用 PTRN 成功地将空间变换的 CXR 照片的分类性能达到与高质量数字 CXR 图像相同的水平，这表明 PTRN 可以消除投影变换对 CXR 照片的所有负面影响。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的投影变换校正网络（PTRN），用于矫正智能手机拍摄的胸部X光照片，以提高病理分类的准确性。 Chak Fong Chong,Yapeng Wang,Benjamin Ng,Wuman Luo,Xu Yang cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Yanbin Liu,Girish Dwivedi,Farid Boussaid,Mohammed Bennamoun
title: 3D Brain and Heart Volume Generative Models: A Survey
title_zh: 3D 大脑和心脏体积生成模型：调查
abstract: Generative models such as generative adversarial networks and autoencoders have gained a great deal of attention in the medical field due to their excellent data generation capability. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of generative models for three-dimensional (3D) volumes, focusing on the brain and heart. A new and elaborate taxonomy of unconditional and conditional generative models is proposed to cover diverse medical tasks for the brain and heart: unconditional synthesis, classification, conditional synthesis, segmentation, denoising, detection, and registration. We provide relevant background, examine each task and also suggest potential future directions. A list of the latest publications will be updated on Github to keep up with the rapid influx of papers at https://github.com/csyanbin/3D-Medical-Generative-Survey.
summary: Generative models such as generative adversarial networks and autoencoders have gained a great deal of attention in the medical field due to their excellent data generation capability. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of generative models for three-dimensional (3D) volumes, focusing on the brain and heart. A new and elaborate taxonomy of unconditional and conditional generative models is proposed to cover diverse medical tasks for the brain and heart: unconditional synthesis, classification, conditional synthesis, segmentation, denoising, detection, and registration. We provide relevant background, examine each task and also suggest potential future directions. A list of the latest publications will be updated on Github to keep up with the rapid influx of papers at https://github.com/csyanbin/3D-Medical-Generative-Survey.
abstract_zh: 生成对抗网络和自动编码器等生成模型因其出色的数据生成能力而在医学领域获得了广泛关注。本文对三维 (3D) 体积的生成模型进行了全面的调查，重点关注大脑和心脏。提出了一种新的、精心设计的无条件和条件生成模型分类法，以涵盖大脑和心脏的各种医疗任务：无条件合成、分类、条件合成、分割、去噪、检测和配准。我们提供相关背景，检查每项任务，并提出潜在的未来方向。最新出版物列表将在 Github 上更新，以跟上 https://github.com/csyanbin/3D-Medical-Generative-Survey 上论文的快速涌入。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了用于三维脑部和心脏体积生成模型的最新研究，提出了一种新的分类方法并探讨了多种医学任务。
AI.motivation: 生成对抗网络和自编码器在医学领域因强大的数据生成能力受到关注，但缺乏针对三维医学图像的系统性综述。
AI.method: 提出无条件和条件生成模型的新分类体系，涵盖合成、分类、分割、去噪等七类医学任务，并建立GitHub动态更新论文列表。
AI.result: 构建了覆盖脑部和心脏的三维生成模型分类框架，总结了各任务的技术方法与挑战，提出了未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 生成模型在医学影像分析中展现潜力，但需解决数据多样性、模型可解释性等关键问题以推动临床应用。

authors_text: Chenjie Cao,Qiaole Dong,Yanwei Fu
title: ZITS++: Image Inpainting by Improving the Incremental Transformer on Structural Priors
title_zh: ZITS++：通过改进结构先验增量变换器进行图像修复
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2203.00867 accepted by TPAMI2023
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2203.00867 accepted by TPAMI2023
abstract: Image inpainting involves filling missing areas of a corrupted image. Despite impressive results have been achieved recently, restoring images with both vivid textures and reasonable structures remains a significant challenge. Previous methods have primarily addressed regular textures while disregarding holistic structures due to the limited receptive fields of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). To this end, we study learning a Zero-initialized residual addition based Incremental Transformer on Structural priors (ZITS++), an improved model upon our conference work, ZITS. Specifically, given one corrupt image, we present the Transformer Structure Restorer (TSR) module to restore holistic structural priors at low image resolution, which are further upsampled by Simple Structure Upsampler (SSU) module to higher image resolution. To recover image texture details, we use the Fourier CNN Texture Restoration (FTR) module, which is strengthened by Fourier and large-kernel attention convolutions. Furthermore, to enhance the FTR, the upsampled structural priors from TSR are further processed by Structure Feature Encoder (SFE) and optimized with the Zero-initialized Residual Addition (ZeroRA) incrementally. Besides, a new masking positional encoding is proposed to encode the large irregular masks. Compared with ZITS, ZITS++ improves the FTR's stability and inpainting ability with several techniques. More importantly, we comprehensively explore the effects of various image priors for inpainting and investigate how to utilize them to address high-resolution image inpainting with extensive experiments. This investigation is orthogonal to most inpainting approaches and can thus significantly benefit the community. Codes and models will be released in https://github.com/ewrfcas/ZITS-PlusPlus.
summary: Image inpainting involves filling missing areas of a corrupted image. Despite impressive results have been achieved recently, restoring images with both vivid textures and reasonable structures remains a significant challenge. Previous methods have primarily addressed regular textures while disregarding holistic structures due to the limited receptive fields of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). To this end, we study learning a Zero-initialized residual addition based Incremental Transformer on Structural priors (ZITS++), an improved model upon our conference work, ZITS. Specifically, given one corrupt image, we present the Transformer Structure Restorer (TSR) module to restore holistic structural priors at low image resolution, which are further upsampled by Simple Structure Upsampler (SSU) module to higher image resolution. To recover image texture details, we use the Fourier CNN Texture Restoration (FTR) module, which is strengthened by Fourier and large-kernel attention convolutions. Furthermore, to enhance the FTR, the upsampled structural priors from TSR are further processed by Structure Feature Encoder (SFE) and optimized with the Zero-initialized Residual Addition (ZeroRA) incrementally. Besides, a new masking positional encoding is proposed to encode the large irregular masks. Compared with ZITS, ZITS++ improves the FTR's stability and inpainting ability with several techniques. More importantly, we comprehensively explore the effects of various image priors for inpainting and investigate how to utilize them to address high-resolution image inpainting with extensive experiments. This investigation is orthogonal to most inpainting approaches and can thus significantly benefit the community. Codes and models will be released in https://github.com/ewrfcas/ZITS-PlusPlus.
abstract_zh: 图像修复涉及填充损坏图像的缺失区域。尽管最近取得了令人印象深刻的成果，但恢复具有生动纹理和合理结构的图像仍然是一个重大挑战。由于卷积神经网络（CNN）的感受野有限，以前的方法主要解决规则纹理，而忽略整体结构。为此，我们研究学习基于结构先验的零初始化残差加法增量变换器（ZITS++），这是我们会议工作 ZITS 的改进模型。具体来说，给定一张损坏的图像，我们提出了变换器结构恢复器（TSR）模块，以低图像分辨率恢复整体结构先验，并通过简单结构上采样器（SSU）模块进一步上采样到更高的图像分辨率。为了恢复图像纹理细节，我们使用傅里叶 CNN 纹理恢复 (FTR) 模块，该模块通过傅里叶和大内核注意卷积得到加强。此外，为了增强 FTR，来自 TSR 的上采样结构先验由结构特征编码器 (SFE) 进一步处理，并通过零初始化残差加法 (ZeroRA) 增量进行优化。此外，提出了一种新的掩码位置编码来对大的不规则掩码进行编码。与ZITS相比，ZITS++通过多项技术提高了FTR的稳定性和修复能力。更重要的是，我们全面探索了各种图像先验对修复的影响，并通过大量实验研究如何利用它们来解决高分辨率图像修复问题。这项调查与大多数修复方法正交，因此可以使社区受益匪浅。代码和模型将在 https://github.com/ewrfcas/ZITS-PlusPlus 中发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ZITS++模型，通过改进增量Transformer结构，结合结构先验信息，提升高分辨率图像修复效果。
AI.motivation: 现有方法因CNN感受野限制，难以同时恢复图像纹理细节与整体结构，尤其在高分辨率场景下效果不足。
AI.method: 设计Transformer结构修复模块(TSR)与简单结构上采样模块(SSU)恢复结构先验，结合傅里叶CNN纹理修复模块(FTR)与结构特征编码器(SFE)，并引入零初始化残差加法(ZeroRA)和新型掩码位置编码。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上实现优于ZITS及其他SOTA方法的修复效果，尤其在高分辨率图像修复任务中表现显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 本研究通过系统探索图像先验信息的利用方式，为高分辨率图像修复提供了新的技术路径，具有普适性价值。

search_text: ZITS++: Image Inpainting by Improving the Incremental Transformer on Structural Priors ZITS++：通过改进结构先验增量变换器进行图像修复 Image inpainting involves filling missing areas of a corrupted image. Despite impressive results have been achieved recently, restoring images with both vivid textures and reasonable structures remains a significant challenge. Previous methods have primarily addressed regular textures while disregarding holistic structures due to the limited receptive fields of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). To this end, we study learning a Zero-initialized residual addition based Incremental Transformer on Structural priors (ZITS++), an improved model upon our conference work, ZITS. Specifically, given one corrupt image, we present the Transformer Structure Restorer (TSR) module to restore holistic structural priors at low image resolution, which are further upsampled by Simple Structure Upsampler (SSU) module to higher image resolution. To recover image texture details, we use the Fourier CNN Texture Restoration (FTR) module, which is strengthened by Fourier and large-kernel attention convolutions. Furthermore, to enhance the FTR, the upsampled structural priors from TSR are further processed by Structure Feature Encoder (SFE) and optimized with the Zero-initialized Residual Addition (ZeroRA) incrementally. Besides, a new masking positional encoding is proposed to encode the large irregular masks. Compared with ZITS, ZITS++ improves the FTR's stability and inpainting ability with several techniques. More importantly, we comprehensively explore the effects of various image priors for inpainting and investigate how to utilize them to address high-resolution image inpainting with extensive experiments. This investigation is orthogonal to most inpainting approaches and can thus significantly benefit the community. Codes and models will be released in https://github.com/ewrfcas/ZITS-PlusPlus. 图像修复涉及填充损坏图像的缺失区域。尽管最近取得了令人印象深刻的成果，但恢复具有生动纹理和合理结构的图像仍然是一个重大挑战。由于卷积神经网络（CNN）的感受野有限，以前的方法主要解决规则纹理，而忽略整体结构。为此，我们研究学习基于结构先验的零初始化残差加法增量变换器（ZITS++），这是我们会议工作 ZITS 的改进模型。具体来说，给定一张损坏的图像，我们提出了变换器结构恢复器（TSR）模块，以低图像分辨率恢复整体结构先验，并通过简单结构上采样器（SSU）模块进一步上采样到更高的图像分辨率。为了恢复图像纹理细节，我们使用傅里叶 CNN 纹理恢复 (FTR) 模块，该模块通过傅里叶和大内核注意卷积得到加强。此外，为了增强 FTR，来自 TSR 的上采样结构先验由结构特征编码器 (SFE) 进一步处理，并通过零初始化残差加法 (ZeroRA) 增量进行优化。此外，提出了一种新的掩码位置编码来对大的不规则掩码进行编码。与ZITS相比，ZITS++通过多项技术提高了FTR的稳定性和修复能力。更重要的是，我们全面探索了各种图像先验对修复的影响，并通过大量实验研究如何利用它们来解决高分辨率图像修复问题。这项调查与大多数修复方法正交，因此可以使社区受益匪浅。代码和模型将在 https://github.com/ewrfcas/ZITS-PlusPlus 中发布。 本文提出ZITS++模型，通过改进增量Transformer结构，结合结构先验信息，提升高分辨率图像修复效果。 Chenjie Cao,Qiaole Dong,Yanwei Fu cs.CV

authors_text: Wenhui Zhu,Peijie Qiu,Natasha Lepore,Oana M. Dumitrascu,Yalin Wang
title: Self-Supervised Equivariant Regularization Reconciles Multiple Instance Learning: Joint Referable Diabetic Retinopathy Classification and Lesion Segmentation
title_zh: 自监督等变正则化协调多实例学习：联合参考糖尿病视网膜病变分类和病变分割
comment: 7 pages, 2 tables, 3 figures. 18th International Symposium on Medical Information Processing and Analysis
comments: 7 pages, 2 tables, 3 figures. 18th International Symposium on Medical Information Processing and Analysis
abstract: Lesion appearance is a crucial clue for medical providers to distinguish referable diabetic retinopathy (rDR) from non-referable DR. Most existing large-scale DR datasets contain only image-level labels rather than pixel-based annotations. This motivates us to develop algorithms to classify rDR and segment lesions via image-level labels. This paper leverages self-supervised equivariant learning and attention-based multi-instance learning (MIL) to tackle this problem. MIL is an effective strategy to differentiate positive and negative instances, helping us discard background regions (negative instances) while localizing lesion regions (positive ones). However, MIL only provides coarse lesion localization and cannot distinguish lesions located across adjacent patches. Conversely, a self-supervised equivariant attention mechanism (SEAM) generates a segmentation-level class activation map (CAM) that can guide patch extraction of lesions more accurately. Our work aims at integrating both methods to improve rDR classification accuracy. We conduct extensive validation experiments on the Eyepacs dataset, achieving an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AU ROC) of 0.958, outperforming current state-of-the-art algorithms.
summary: Lesion appearance is a crucial clue for medical providers to distinguish referable diabetic retinopathy (rDR) from non-referable DR. Most existing large-scale DR datasets contain only image-level labels rather than pixel-based annotations. This motivates us to develop algorithms to classify rDR and segment lesions via image-level labels. This paper leverages self-supervised equivariant learning and attention-based multi-instance learning (MIL) to tackle this problem. MIL is an effective strategy to differentiate positive and negative instances, helping us discard background regions (negative instances) while localizing lesion regions (positive ones). However, MIL only provides coarse lesion localization and cannot distinguish lesions located across adjacent patches. Conversely, a self-supervised equivariant attention mechanism (SEAM) generates a segmentation-level class activation map (CAM) that can guide patch extraction of lesions more accurately. Our work aims at integrating both methods to improve rDR classification accuracy. We conduct extensive validation experiments on the Eyepacs dataset, achieving an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AU ROC) of 0.958, outperforming current state-of-the-art algorithms.
abstract_zh: 病变外观是医疗服务提供者区分可转诊糖尿病视网膜病变 (rDR) 和不可转诊 DR 的重要线索。大多数现有的大规模 DR 数据集仅包含图像级标签，而不包含基于像素的注释。这促使我们开发算法来通过图像级标签对 rDR 进行分类和分割病变。本文利用自监督等变学习和基于注意力的多实例学习（MIL）来解决这个问题。 MIL 是区分正例和负例的有效策略，帮助我们丢弃背景区域（负例），同时定位病变区域（正例）。然而，MIL 只能提供粗略的病变定位，无法区分位于相邻斑块上的病变。相反，自监督等变注意机制（SEAM）生成分割级别的类激活图（CAM），可以更准确地指导病变的斑块提取。我们的工作旨在整合这两种方法以提高 rDR 分类准确性。我们对 Eyepacs 数据集进行了广泛的验证实验，获得了 0.958 的受试者工作特征曲线下面积 (AU ROC)，优于当前最先进的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合多实例学习（MIL）与自监督等变注意力机制（SEAM），实现可转诊糖尿病视网膜病变（rDR）的分类及病变分割，解决大规模数据集缺乏像素级标注的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有大规模DR数据集仅含图像级标签，缺乏像素级标注，难以直接用于病变分割，需开发基于图像级标签的分类与分割联合算法。
AI.method: 融合多实例学习（MIL）与自监督等变注意力机制（SEAM），MIL用于粗略定位病变区域，SEAM生成分割级类激活图（CAM）精确定位病变，提升分类与分割性能。
AI.result: 在Eyepacs数据集上实现rDR分类AUROC 0.958，优于当前最先进算法，验证方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文通过整合MIL与SEAM，有效提升rDR分类与分割精度，为医疗图像分析提供新思路。

Most existing large-scale DR datasets contain only image-level labels rather than pixel-based annotations. This motivates us to develop algorithms to classify rDR and segment lesions via image-level labels. authors_text: Kehan Li,Zhennan Wang,Zesen Cheng,Runyi Yu,Yian Zhao,Guoli Song,Chang Liu,Li Yuan,Jie Chen
title: ACSeg: Adaptive Conceptualization for Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: ACSeg：无监督语义分割的自适应概念化
abstract: Recently, self-supervised large-scale visual pre-training models have shown great promise in representing pixel-level semantic relationships, significantly promoting the development of unsupervised dense prediction tasks, e.g., unsupervised semantic segmentation (USS). The extracted relationship among pixel-level representations typically contains rich class-aware information that semantically identical pixel embeddings in the representation space gather together to form sophisticated concepts. However, leveraging the learned models to ascertain semantically consistent pixel groups or regions in the image is non-trivial since over/ under-clustering overwhelms the conceptualization procedure under various semantic distributions of different images. In this work, we investigate the pixel-level semantic aggregation in self-supervised ViT pre-trained models as image Segmentation and propose the Adaptive Conceptualization approach for USS, termed ACSeg. Concretely, we explicitly encode concepts into learnable prototypes and design the Adaptive Concept Generator (ACG), which adaptively maps these prototypes to informative concepts for each image. Meanwhile, considering the scene complexity of different images, we propose the modularity loss to optimize ACG independent of the concept number based on estimating the intensity of pixel pairs belonging to the same concept. Finally, we turn the USS task into classifying the discovered concepts in an unsupervised manner. Extensive experiments with state-of-the-art results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ACSeg.
summary: Recently, self-supervised large-scale visual pre-training models have shown great promise in representing pixel-level semantic relationships, significantly promoting the development of unsupervised dense prediction tasks, e.g., unsupervised semantic segmentation (USS). The extracted relationship among pixel-level representations typically contains rich class-aware information that semantically identical pixel embeddings in the representation space gather together to form sophisticated concepts. However, leveraging the learned models to ascertain semantically consistent pixel groups or regions in the image is non-trivial since over/ under-clustering overwhelms the conceptualization procedure under various semantic distributions of different images. In this work, we investigate the pixel-level semantic aggregation in self-supervised ViT pre-trained models as image Segmentation and propose the Adaptive Conceptualization approach for USS, termed ACSeg. Concretely, we explicitly encode concepts into learnable prototypes and design the Adaptive Concept Generator (ACG), which adaptively maps these prototypes to informative concepts for each image. Meanwhile, considering the scene complexity of different images, we propose the modularity loss to optimize ACG independent of the concept number based on estimating the intensity of pixel pairs belonging to the same concept. Finally, we turn the USS task into classifying the discovered concepts in an unsupervised manner. Extensive experiments with state-of-the-art results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ACSeg.
abstract_zh: 最近，自监督大规模视觉预训练模型在表示像素级语义关系方面显示出了巨大的前景，显着促进了无监督密集预测任务的发展，例如无监督语义分割（USS）。提取的像素级表示之间的关系通常包含丰富的类感知信息，表示空间中语义相同的像素嵌入聚集在一起形成复杂的概念。然而，利用学习的模型来确定图像中语义一致的像素组或区域并非易事，因为过度/欠聚类压倒了不同图像的各种语义分布下的概念化过程。在这项工作中，我们研究了自监督 ViT 预训练模型中的像素级语义聚合作为图像分割，并提出了 USS 的自适应概念化方法，称为 ACSeg。具体来说，我们将概念显式编码为可学习的原型，并设计自适应概念生成器（ACG），它自适应地将这些原型映射到每个图像的信息概念。同时，考虑到不同图像的场景复杂性，我们提出了模块化损失，基于估计属于同一概念的像素对的强度，独立于概念数量来优化ACG。最后，我们将 USS 任务转变为以无监督的方式对发现的概念进行分类。广泛的实验和最先进的结果证明了所提出的 ACSeg 的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ACSeg方法，通过自适应概念生成器和模块化损失函数解决无监督语义分割中的聚类不稳定问题。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督模型在像素级语义关系建模中存在过/欠聚类问题，导致不同图像语义分布下的概念化过程困难。
AI.method: 设计自适应概念生成器(ACG)将可学习原型映射为图像特定概念，并引入模块化损失函数优化概念划分。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上实现SOTA性能，验证了ACSeg在无监督语义分割任务中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: ACSeg通过动态概念生成和模块化优化，有效解决了无监督场景下的语义聚类难题。

search_text: ACSeg: Adaptive Conceptualization for Unsupervised Semantic Segmentation ACSeg：无监督语义分割的自适应概念化 Recently, self-supervised large-scale visual pre-training models have shown great promise in representing pixel-level semantic relationships, significantly promoting the development of unsupervised dense prediction tasks, e.g., unsupervised semantic segmentation (USS). The extracted relationship among pixel-level representations typically contains rich class-aware information that semantically identical pixel embeddings in the representation space gather together to form sophisticated concepts. However, leveraging the learned models to ascertain semantically consistent pixel groups or regions in the image is non-trivial since over/ under-clustering overwhelms the conceptualization procedure under various semantic distributions of different images. In this work, we investigate the pixel-level semantic aggregation in self-supervised ViT pre-trained models as image Segmentation and propose the Adaptive Conceptualization approach for USS, termed ACSeg. Concretely, we explicitly encode concepts into learnable prototypes and design the Adaptive Concept Generator (ACG), which adaptively maps these prototypes to informative concepts for each image. Meanwhile, considering the scene complexity of different images, we propose the modularity loss to optimize ACG independent of the concept number based on estimating the intensity of pixel pairs belonging to the same concept. Finally, we turn the USS task into classifying the discovered concepts in an unsupervised manner. Extensive experiments with state-of-the-art results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ACSeg. 最近，自监督大规模视觉预训练模型在表示像素级语义关系方面显示出了巨大的前景，显着促进了无监督密集预测任务的发展，例如无监督语义分割（USS）。提取的像素级表示之间的关系通常包含丰富的类感知信息，表示空间中语义相同的像素嵌入聚集在一起形成复杂的概念。然而，利用学习的模型来确定图像中语义一致的像素组或区域并非易事，因为过度/欠聚类压倒了不同图像的各种语义分布下的概念化过程。在这项工作中，我们研究了自监督 ViT 预训练模型中的像素级语义聚合作为图像分割，并提出了 USS 的自适应概念化方法，称为 ACSeg。具体来说，我们将概念显式编码为可学习的原型，并设计自适应概念生成器（ACG），它自适应地将这些原型映射到每个图像的信息概念。同时，考虑到不同图像的场景复杂性，我们提出了模块化损失，基于估计属于同一概念的像素对的强度，独立于概念数量来优化ACG。最后，我们将 USS 任务转变为以无监督的方式对发现的概念进行分类。广泛的实验和最先进的结果证明了所提出的 ACSeg 的有效性。 本文提出ACSeg方法，通过自适应概念生成器和模块化损失函数解决无监督语义分割中的聚类不稳定问题。 Kehan Li,Zhennan Wang,Zesen Cheng,Runyi Yu,Yian Zhao,Guoli Song,Chang Liu,Li Yuan,Jie Chen cs.CV

authors_text: Donghyeon Baek,Youngmin Oh,Sanghoon Lee,Junghyup Lee,Bumsub Ham
title: Decomposed Knowledge Distillation for Class-Incremental Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 用于类增量语义分割的分解知识蒸馏
abstract: Class-incremental semantic segmentation (CISS) labels each pixel of an image with a corresponding object/stuff class continually. To this end, it is crucial to learn novel classes incrementally without forgetting previously learned knowledge. Current CISS methods typically use a knowledge distillation (KD) technique for preserving classifier logits, or freeze a feature extractor, to avoid the forgetting problem. The strong constraints, however, prevent learning discriminative features for novel classes. We introduce a CISS framework that alleviates the forgetting problem and facilitates learning novel classes effectively. We have found that a logit can be decomposed into two terms. They quantify how likely an input belongs to a particular class or not, providing a clue for a reasoning process of a model. The KD technique, in this context, preserves the sum of two terms (i.e., a class logit), suggesting that each could be changed and thus the KD does not imitate the reasoning process. To impose constraints on each term explicitly, we propose a new decomposed knowledge distillation (DKD) technique, improving the rigidity of a model and addressing the forgetting problem more effectively. We also introduce a novel initialization method to train new classifiers for novel classes. In CISS, the number of negative training samples for novel classes is not sufficient to discriminate old classes. To mitigate this, we propose to transfer knowledge of negatives to the classifiers successively using an auxiliary classifier, boosting the performance significantly. Experimental results on standard CISS benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.
summary: Class-incremental semantic segmentation (CISS) labels each pixel of an image with a corresponding object/stuff class continually. To this end, it is crucial to learn novel classes incrementally without forgetting previously learned knowledge. Current CISS methods typically use a knowledge distillation (KD) technique for preserving classifier logits, or freeze a feature extractor, to avoid the forgetting problem. The strong constraints, however, prevent learning discriminative features for novel classes. We introduce a CISS framework that alleviates the forgetting problem and facilitates learning novel classes effectively. We have found that a logit can be decomposed into two terms. They quantify how likely an input belongs to a particular class or not, providing a clue for a reasoning process of a model. The KD technique, in this context, preserves the sum of two terms (i.e., a class logit), suggesting that each could be changed and thus the KD does not imitate the reasoning process. To impose constraints on each term explicitly, we propose a new decomposed knowledge distillation (DKD) technique, improving the rigidity of a model and addressing the forgetting problem more effectively. We also introduce a novel initialization method to train new classifiers for novel classes. In CISS, the number of negative training samples for novel classes is not sufficient to discriminate old classes. To mitigate this, we propose to transfer knowledge of negatives to the classifiers successively using an auxiliary classifier, boosting the performance significantly. Experimental results on standard CISS benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.
abstract_zh: 类增量语义分割（CISS）连续用相应的对象/东西类标记图像的每个像素。为此，逐步学习新课程而不忘记以前学过的知识至关重要。当前的 CISS 方法通常使用知识蒸馏 (KD) 技术来保留分类器逻辑，或冻结特征提取器，以避免遗忘问题。然而，强大的约束阻碍了学习新类的判别性特征。我们引入了 CISS 框架，可以缓解遗忘问题并促进有效学习新课程。我们发现logit可以分解为两项。它们量化输入属于特定类别或不属于特定类别的可能性，为模型的推理过程提供线索。在这种情况下，KD 技术保留了两项的总和（即类 logit），这表明每一项都可以更改，因此 KD 不会模仿推理过程。为了明确地对每个术语施加约束，我们提出了一种新的分解知识蒸馏（DKD）技术，提高了模型的刚性并更有效地解决了遗忘问题。我们还引入了一种新颖的初始化方法来为新颖的类训练新的分类器。在 CISS 中，新类的负训练样本数量不足以区分旧类。为了缓解这个问题，我们建议使用辅助分类器将负数知识连续转移到分类器，从而显着提高性能。标准 CISS 基准的实验结果证明了我们框架的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分解知识蒸馏框架，用于解决类增量语义分割中的知识遗忘问题，使模型能有效学习新类别而不遗忘旧知识。
AI.motivation: 现有类增量语义分割方法通过冻结特征提取器或保留分类器logit进行知识蒸馏，但这些强约束限制了新类别的判别特征学习，导致遗忘问题难以有效解决。
AI.method: 将logit分解为两个量化项，提出分解知识蒸馏（DKD）技术分别约束两项，增强模型鲁棒性；设计新型分类器初始化方法，并通过辅助分类器传递负样本知识以弥补新类负样本不足。
AI.result: 在标准类增量语义分割基准测试中，所提框架显著优于现有方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 分解知识蒸馏框架通过显式约束logit分解项和负样本知识迁移，有效缓解了类增量语义分割中的遗忘问题，提升了新类别学习性能。

authors_text: Julia Grabinski,Paul Gavrikov,Janis Keuper,Margret Keuper
title: Robust Models are less Over-Confident
title_zh: 稳健的模型不会过度自信
abstract: Despite the success of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in many academic benchmarks for computer vision tasks, their application in the real-world is still facing fundamental challenges. One of these open problems is the inherent lack of robustness, unveiled by the striking effectiveness of adversarial attacks. Current attack methods are able to manipulate the network's prediction by adding specific but small amounts of noise to the input. In turn, adversarial training (AT) aims to achieve robustness against such attacks and ideally a better model generalization ability by including adversarial samples in the trainingset. However, an in-depth analysis of the resulting robust models beyond adversarial robustness is still pending. In this paper, we empirically analyze a variety of adversarially trained models that achieve high robust accuracies when facing state-of-the-art attacks and we show that AT has an interesting side-effect: it leads to models that are significantly less overconfident with their decisions, even on clean data than non-robust models. Further, our analysis of robust models shows that not only AT but also the model's building blocks (like activation functions and pooling) have a strong influence on the models' prediction confidences. Data & Project website: https://github.com/GeJulia/robustness_confidences_evaluation
summary: Despite the success of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in many academic benchmarks for computer vision tasks, their application in the real-world is still facing fundamental challenges. One of these open problems is the inherent lack of robustness, unveiled by the striking effectiveness of adversarial attacks. Current attack methods are able to manipulate the network's prediction by adding specific but small amounts of noise to the input. In turn, adversarial training (AT) aims to achieve robustness against such attacks and ideally a better model generalization ability by including adversarial samples in the trainingset. However, an in-depth analysis of the resulting robust models beyond adversarial robustness is still pending. In this paper, we empirically analyze a variety of adversarially trained models that achieve high robust accuracies when facing state-of-the-art attacks and we show that AT has an interesting side-effect: it leads to models that are significantly less overconfident with their decisions, even on clean data than non-robust models. Further, our analysis of robust models shows that not only AT but also the model's building blocks (like activation functions and pooling) have a strong influence on the models' prediction confidences. Data & Project website: https://github.com/GeJulia/robustness_confidences_evaluation
abstract_zh: 尽管卷积神经网络（CNN）在计算机视觉任务的许多学术基准中取得了成功，但它们在现实世界中的应用仍然面临着根本性的挑战。这些悬而未决的问题之一是固有的鲁棒性缺乏，对抗性攻击的惊人有效性揭示了这一点。当前的攻击方法能够通过向输入添加特定但少量的噪声来操纵网络的预测。反过来，对抗性训练（AT）旨在通过在训练集中包含对抗性样本来实现针对此类攻击的鲁棒性，并理想地获得更好的模型泛化能力。然而，对所产生的超出对抗稳健性的稳健模型的深入分析仍有待进行。在本文中，我们根据经验分析了各种经过对抗性训练的模型，这些模型在面对最先进的攻击时可以实现高鲁棒精度，并且我们表明 AT 有一个有趣的副作用：与非鲁棒模型相比，即使在干净的数据上，它也会导致模型对其决策的过度自信显着降低。此外，我们对稳健模型的分析表明，不仅 AT，而且模型的构建块（如激活函数和池化）对模型的预测置信度都有很大影响。数据和项目网站：https://github.com/GeJulia/robustness_confidences_evaluation

AI.tldr: 本文分析了对抗训练模型在面对对抗攻击时的鲁棒性，并探讨其预测置信度的变化。
AI.motivation: 尽管对抗训练能提升模型鲁棒性，但现有研究缺乏对模型置信度变化的深入分析，且模型结构对置信度的影响尚不明确。
AI.method: 通过实证分析多种高鲁棒性对抗训练模型，评估其在干净数据和对抗样本下的置信度，并研究激活函数、池化等结构对置信度的影响。
AI.result: 对抗训练显著降低模型置信度，即使在干净数据上也更保守；模型结构（如激活函数）对置信度有显著影响。
AI.conclusion: 对抗训练不仅提升鲁棒性，还降低模型过自信倾向，模型设计需综合考虑鲁棒性与置信度的平衡。

Further, our analysis of robust models shows that not only AT but also the model's building blocks (like activation functions and pooling) have a strong influence on the models' prediction confidences. Data & Project website: https://github.com/GeJulia/robustness_confidences_evaluation 尽管卷积神经网络（CNN）在计算机视觉任务的许多学术基准中取得了成功，但它们在现实世界中的应用仍然面临着根本性的挑战。这些悬而未决的问题之一是固有的鲁棒性缺乏，对抗性攻击的惊人有效性揭示了这一点。当前的攻击方法能够通过向输入添加特定但少量的噪声来操纵网络的预测。反过来，对抗性训练（AT）旨在通过在训练集中包含对抗性样本来实现针对此类攻击的鲁棒性，并理想地获得更好的模型泛化能力。然而，对所产生的超出对抗稳健性的稳健模型的深入分析仍有待进行。在本文中，我们根据经验分析了各种经过对抗性训练的模型，这些模型在面对最先进的攻击时可以实现高鲁棒精度，并且我们表明 AT 有一个有趣的副作用：与非鲁棒模型相比，即使在干净的数据上，它也会导致模型对其决策的过度自信显着降低。此外，我们对稳健模型的分析表明，不仅 AT，而且模型的构建块（如激活函数和池化）对模型的预测置信度都有很大影响。数据和项目网站：https://github.com/GeJulia/robustness_confidences_evaluation 本文分析了对抗训练模型在面对对抗攻击时的鲁棒性，并探讨其预测置信度的变化。 Julia Grabinski,Paul Gavrikov,Janis Keuper,Margret Keuper cs.CV

authors_text: Felix Feit,Andreas Metzger,Klaus Pohl
title: Explaining Online Reinforcement Learning Decisions of Self-Adaptive Systems
title_zh: 解释自适应系统的在线强化学习决策
comment: Published at 3rd Intl Conference on Autonomic Computing and Self-Organizing Systems (ACSOS 2022)
comments: Published at 3rd Intl Conference on Autonomic Computing and Self-Organizing Systems (ACSOS 2022)
abstract: Design time uncertainty poses an important challenge when developing a self-adaptive system. As an example, defining how the system should adapt when facing a new environment state, requires understanding the precise effect of an adaptation, which may not be known at design time. Online reinforcement learning, i.e., employing reinforcement learning (RL) at runtime, is an emerging approach to realizing self-adaptive systems in the presence of design time uncertainty. By using Online RL, the self-adaptive system can learn from actual operational data and leverage feedback only available at runtime. Recently, Deep RL is gaining interest. Deep RL represents learned knowledge as a neural network whereby it can generalize over unseen inputs, as well as handle continuous environment states and adaptation actions. A fundamental problem of Deep RL is that learned knowledge is not explicitly represented. For a human, it is practically impossible to relate the parametrization of the neural network to concrete RL decisions and thus Deep RL essentially appears as a black box. Yet, understanding the decisions made by Deep RL is key to (1) increasing trust, and (2) facilitating debugging. Such debugging is especially relevant for self-adaptive systems, because the reward function, which quantifies the feedback to the RL algorithm, must be defined by developers. The reward function must be explicitly defined by developers, thus introducing a potential for human error. To explain Deep RL for self-adaptive systems, we enhance and combine two existing explainable RL techniques from the machine learning literature. The combined technique, XRL-DINE, overcomes the respective limitations of the individual techniques. We present a proof-of-concept implementation of XRL-DINE, as well as qualitative and quantitative results of applying XRL-DINE to a self-adaptive system exemplar.

Deep RL represents learned knowledge as a neural network whereby it can generalize over unseen inputs, as well as handle continuous environment states and adaptation actions. A fundamental problem of Deep RL is that learned knowledge is not explicitly represented. For a human, it is practically impossible to relate the parametrization of the neural network to concrete RL decisions and thus Deep RL essentially appears as a black box. Yet, understanding the decisions made by Deep RL is key to (1) increasing trust, and (2) facilitating debugging. Such debugging is especially relevant for self-adaptive systems, because the reward function, which quantifies the feedback to the RL algorithm, must be defined by developers. The reward function must be explicitly defined by developers, thus introducing a potential for human error. To explain Deep RL for self-adaptive systems, we enhance and combine two existing explainable RL techniques from the machine learning literature. The combined technique, XRL-DINE, overcomes the respective limitations of the individual techniques. We present a proof-of-concept implementation of XRL-DINE, as well as qualitative and quantitative results of applying XRL-DINE to a self-adaptive system exemplar.
abstract_zh: 在开发自适应系统时，设计时间的不确定性提出了重要的挑战。例如，定义系统在面对新的环境状态时应如何适应，需要了解适应的精确效果，而这在设计时可能是未知的。在线强化学习，即在运行时采用强化学习（RL），是一种在存在设计时不确定性的情况下实现自适应系统的新兴方法。通过使用在线强化学习，自适应系统可以从实际操作数据中学习并利用仅在运行时可用的反馈。最近，深度强化学习越来越受到人们的关注。深度强化学习将学到的知识表示为神经网络，它可以概括未见过的输入，并处理连续的环境状态和适应动作。深度强化学习的一个基本问题是所学知识没有明确表示。对于人类来说，实际上不可能将神经网络的参数化与具体的强化学习决策联系起来，因此深度强化学习本质上表现为一个黑匣子。然而，了解深度强化学习所做的决策对于 (1) 增加信任和 (2) 促进调试至关重要。这种调试对于自适应系统尤其重要，因为量化 RL 算法反馈的奖励函数必须由开发人员定义。奖励函数必须由开发人员明确定义，从而引入人为错误的可能性。为了解释自适应系统的深度强化学习，我们增强并结合了机器学习文献中两种现有的可解释强化学习技术。 XRL-DINE 组合技术克服了各个技术各自的局限性。我们提出了 XRL-DINE 的概念验证实现，以及将 XRL-DINE 应用到自适应系统示例的定性和定量结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为XRL-DINE的方法，用于解释自适应系统中深度强化学习（Deep RL）的决策过程，以解决其黑箱问题。
AI.motivation: 自适应系统在设计时面临不确定性，而深度强化学习虽能处理复杂环境，但其决策过程难以解释，影响信任度和调试效率。
AI.method: 通过结合两种现有的可解释强化学习技术，改进并整合为XRL-DINE方法，克服单一技术的局限性。
AI.result: 实现了XRL-DINE的原型，并在自适应系统案例中展示了定性和定量结果，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: XRL-DINE成功提升了深度强化学习决策的可解释性，有助于增强自适应系统的可信度和调试能力。

authors_text: Yongyuan Liang,Yanchao Sun,Ruijie Zheng,Furong Huang
title: Efficient Adversarial Training without Attacking: Worst-Case-Aware Robust Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 无攻击的高效对抗训练：最坏情况感知的鲁棒强化学习
comment: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: Recent studies reveal that a well-trained deep reinforcement learning (RL) policy can be particularly vulnerable to adversarial perturbations on input observations. Therefore, it is crucial to train RL agents that are robust against any attacks with a bounded budget. Existing robust training methods in deep RL either treat correlated steps separately, ignoring the robustness of long-term rewards, or train the agents and RL-based attacker together, doubling the computational burden and sample complexity of the training process. In this work, we propose a strong and efficient robust training framework for RL, named Worst-case-aware Robust RL (WocaR-RL) that directly estimates and optimizes the worst-case reward of a policy under bounded l_p attacks without requiring extra samples for learning an attacker. Experiments on multiple environments show that WocaR-RL achieves state-of-the-art performance under various strong attacks, and obtains significantly higher training efficiency than prior state-of-the-art robust training methods. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/umd-huang-lab/WocaR-RL.
summary: Recent studies reveal that a well-trained deep reinforcement learning (RL) policy can be particularly vulnerable to adversarial perturbations on input observations. Therefore, it is crucial to train RL agents that are robust against any attacks with a bounded budget. Existing robust training methods in deep RL either treat correlated steps separately, ignoring the robustness of long-term rewards, or train the agents and RL-based attacker together, doubling the computational burden and sample complexity of the training process. In this work, we propose a strong and efficient robust training framework for RL, named Worst-case-aware Robust RL (WocaR-RL) that directly estimates and optimizes the worst-case reward of a policy under bounded l_p attacks without requiring extra samples for learning an attacker. Experiments on multiple environments show that WocaR-RL achieves state-of-the-art performance under various strong attacks, and obtains significantly higher training efficiency than prior state-of-the-art robust training methods. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/umd-huang-lab/WocaR-RL.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究表明，训练有素的深度强化学习（RL）策略特别容易受到输入观察的对抗性扰动的影响。因此，在预算有限的情况下训练能够抵御任何攻击的强化学习代理至关重要。深度强化学习中现有的鲁棒训练方法要么单独处理相关步骤，忽略长期奖励的鲁棒性，要么一起训练代理和基于强化学习的攻击者，使训练过程的计算负担和样本复杂性加倍。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个强大而高效的强化学习框架，名为最坏情况感知鲁棒强化学习（WocaR-RL），它可以直接估计和优化有界 l_p 攻击下策略的最坏情况奖励，而不需要额外的样本来学习攻击者。多种环境下的实验表明，WocaR-RL在各种强攻击下均实现了state-of-the-art的性能，并且获得了比现有state-of-art鲁棒训练方法显着更高的训练效率。这项工作的代码可以在 https://github.com/umd-huang-lab/WocaR-RL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需攻击的高效对抗训练框架WocaR-RL，直接估计并优化深度强化学习策略在有界攻击下的最坏情况奖励。
AI.motivation: 现有深度强化学习的鲁棒训练方法要么忽略长期奖励的鲁棒性，要么需要同时训练攻击者导致计算负担加重，亟需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过直接建模有界l_p攻击下的最坏情况奖励函数，无需额外样本或攻击者模型即可优化策略鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在多个环境中实验表明，WocaR-RL在多种强攻击下达到最先进性能，且训练效率显著高于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: WocaR-RL为深度强化学习提供了高效且强大的鲁棒训练框架，解决了现有方法的计算和样本效率问题。

In this work, we propose a strong and efficient robust training framework for RL, named Worst-case-aware Robust RL (WocaR-RL) that directly estimates and optimizes the worst-case reward of a policy under bounded l_p attacks without requiring extra samples for learning an attacker. Experiments on multiple environments show that WocaR-RL achieves state-of-the-art performance under various strong attacks, and obtains significantly higher training efficiency than prior state-of-the-art robust training methods. The code of this work is available at https://github.com/umd-huang-lab/WocaR-RL. 最近的研究表明，训练有素的深度强化学习（RL）策略特别容易受到输入观察的对抗性扰动的影响。因此，在预算有限的情况下训练能够抵御任何攻击的强化学习代理至关重要。深度强化学习中现有的鲁棒训练方法要么单独处理相关步骤，忽略长期奖励的鲁棒性，要么一起训练代理和基于强化学习的攻击者，使训练过程的计算负担和样本复杂性加倍。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个强大而高效的强化学习框架，名为最坏情况感知鲁棒强化学习（WocaR-RL），它可以直接估计和优化有界 l_p 攻击下策略的最坏情况奖励，而不需要额外的样本来学习攻击者。多种环境下的实验表明，WocaR-RL在各种强攻击下均实现了state-of-the-art的性能，并且获得了比现有state-of-art鲁棒训练方法显着更高的训练效率。这项工作的代码可以在 https://github.com/umd-huang-lab/WocaR-RL 获取。 本文提出了一种无需攻击的高效对抗训练框架WocaR-RL，直接估计并优化深度强化学习策略在有界攻击下的最坏情况奖励。 Yongyuan Liang,Yanchao Sun,Ruijie Zheng,Furong Huang cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Wei Huang,Jiaxiang Li,Shuming Jiao,Zibang Zhang
title: Solving combinational optimization problems with evolutionary single-pixel imaging
title_zh: 用进化单像素成像解决组合优化问题
abstract: Single-pixel imaging (SPI) is a novel optical imaging technique by replacing the pixelated sensor array in a conventional camera with a single-pixel detector. In previous works, SPI is usually used for capturing object images or performing image processing tasks. In this work, we propose a SPI scheme for processing other types of data in addition to images. An Ising machine model is implemented optically with SPI for solving combinational optimization problems including number partition and graph maximum cut. Simulated and experimental results show that our proposed scheme can optimize the Hamiltonian function with evolutionary illumination patterns.
summary: Single-pixel imaging (SPI) is a novel optical imaging technique by replacing the pixelated sensor array in a conventional camera with a single-pixel detector. In previous works, SPI is usually used for capturing object images or performing image processing tasks. In this work, we propose a SPI scheme for processing other types of data in addition to images. An Ising machine model is implemented optically with SPI for solving combinational optimization problems including number partition and graph maximum cut. Simulated and experimental results show that our proposed scheme can optimize the Hamiltonian function with evolutionary illumination patterns.
abstract_zh: 单像素成像（SPI）是一种新颖的光学成像技术，用单像素探测器取代传统相机中的像素化传感器阵列。在之前的工作中，SPI通常用于捕获物体图像或执行图像处理任务。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种 SPI 方案，用于处理除图像之外的其他类型的数据。伊辛机模型通过 SPI 光学实现，用于解决包括数字划分和图最大割在内的组合优化问题。模拟和实验结果表明，我们提出的方案可以通过进化照明模式优化哈密顿函数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于单像素成像（SPI）的方案，用于解决组合优化问题，如数字分区和图的最大割问题。
AI.motivation: 传统的单像素成像技术主要用于图像捕捉和图像处理，而本文旨在拓展其应用范围，使其能够处理其他类型的数据，尤其是组合优化问题。
AI.method: 本文通过光学实现Ising机器模型，利用单像素成像技术生成进化的照明模式，以优化哈密顿函数。
AI.result: 模拟和实验结果表明，所提出的方案能够有效优化哈密顿函数，成功解决了组合优化问题。
AI.conclusion: 本文展示了单像素成像技术在解决组合优化问题方面的潜力，为该技术的应用拓展提供了新的方向。

authors_text: Shentao Yang,Shujian Zhang,Yihao Feng,Mingyuan Zhou
title: A Unified Framework for Alternating Offline Model Training and Policy Learning
title_zh: 交替离线模型训练和政策学习的统一框架
comment: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
comments: 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)
abstract: In offline model-based reinforcement learning (offline MBRL), we learn a dynamic model from historically collected data, and subsequently utilize the learned model and fixed datasets for policy learning, without further interacting with the environment. Offline MBRL algorithms can improve the efficiency and stability of policy learning over the model-free algorithms. However, in most of the existing offline MBRL algorithms, the learning objectives for the dynamic models and the policies are isolated from each other. Such an objective mismatch may lead to inferior performance of the learned agents. In this paper, we address this issue by developing an iterative offline MBRL framework, where we maximize a lower bound of the true expected return, by alternating between dynamic-model training and policy learning. With the proposed unified model-policy learning framework, we achieve competitive performance on a wide range of continuous-control offline reinforcement learning datasets. Source code is publicly released.
summary: In offline model-based reinforcement learning (offline MBRL), we learn a dynamic model from historically collected data, and subsequently utilize the learned model and fixed datasets for policy learning, without further interacting with the environment. Offline MBRL algorithms can improve the efficiency and stability of policy learning over the model-free algorithms. However, in most of the existing offline MBRL algorithms, the learning objectives for the dynamic models and the policies are isolated from each other. Such an objective mismatch may lead to inferior performance of the learned agents. In this paper, we address this issue by developing an iterative offline MBRL framework, where we maximize a lower bound of the true expected return, by alternating between dynamic-model training and policy learning. With the proposed unified model-policy learning framework, we achieve competitive performance on a wide range of continuous-control offline reinforcement learning datasets. Source code is publicly released.
abstract_zh: 在基于离线模型的强化学习（离线MBRL）中，我们从历史收集的数据中学习动态模型，然后利用学习到的模型和固定数据集进行策略学习，而无需进一步与环境交互。离线MBRL算法比无模型算法可以提高策略学习的效率和稳定性。然而，在大多数现有的离线MBRL算法中，动态模型和策略的学习目标是相互隔离的。这种客观的不匹配可能会导致学习代理的性能较差。在本文中，我们通过开发迭代离线 MBRL 框架来解决这个问题，在该框架中，我们通过动态模型训练和政策学习之间的交替来最大化真实预期回报的下限。通过提出的统一模型策略学习框架，我们在各种连续控制离线强化学习数据集上取得了有竞争力的性能。源代码已公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种统一的离线模型强化学习框架，通过交替进行动态模型训练与策略学习，解决传统方法中目标不匹配导致的性能问题。
AI.motivation: 现有离线MBRL算法中动态模型与策略的学习目标相互独立，导致模型与策略之间存在目标不匹配，影响最终性能。
AI.method: 设计迭代框架交替优化动态模型和策略，通过最大化真实预期回报的下界，实现模型训练与策略学习的协同优化。
AI.result: 在多个连续控制离线强化学习数据集上取得具有竞争力的性能表现，源代码已公开。
AI.conclusion: 统一框架有效缓解了模型与策略的目标不匹配问题，在离线MBRL领域展现了提升性能的潜力。

authors_text: Gandharv Patil,Prashanth L. A.,Dheeraj Nagaraj,Doina Precup
title: Finite time analysis of temporal difference learning with linear function approximation: Tail averaging and regularisation
title_zh: 使用线性函数近似的时间差分学习的有限时间分析：尾部平均和正则化
comment: ef:Proceedings of The 26th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics, Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, 2023
comments: ef:Proceedings of The 26th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics, Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, 2023
abstract: We study the finite-time behaviour of the popular temporal difference (TD) learning algorithm when combined with tail-averaging. We derive finite time bounds on the parameter error of the tail-averaged TD iterate under a step-size choice that does not require information about the eigenvalues of the matrix underlying the projected TD fixed point. Our analysis shows that tail-averaged TD converges at the optimal $O\left(1/t\right)$ rate, both in expectation and with high probability. In addition, our bounds exhibit a sharper rate of decay for the initial error (bias), which is an improvement over averaging all iterates. We also propose and analyse a variant of TD that incorporates regularisation. From analysis, we conclude that the regularised version of TD is useful for problems with ill-conditioned features.
summary: We study the finite-time behaviour of the popular temporal difference (TD) learning algorithm when combined with tail-averaging. We derive finite time bounds on the parameter error of the tail-averaged TD iterate under a step-size choice that does not require information about the eigenvalues of the matrix underlying the projected TD fixed point. Our analysis shows that tail-averaged TD converges at the optimal $O\left(1/t\right)$ rate, both in expectation and with high probability. In addition, our bounds exhibit a sharper rate of decay for the initial error (bias), which is an improvement over averaging all iterates. We also propose and analyse a variant of TD that incorporates regularisation. From analysis, we conclude that the regularised version of TD is useful for problems with ill-conditioned features.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了流行的时间差分（TD）学习算法与尾部平均相结合时的有限时间行为。我们在步长选择下推导出尾部平均 TD 迭代的参数误差的有限时间界限，该步长选择不需要有关投影 TD 固定点的矩阵特征值的信息。我们的分析表明，尾部平均 TD 收敛于最优 $O\left(1/t\right)$ 速率，无论是预期还是高概率。此外，我们的边界表现出初始误差（偏差）的更急剧的衰减率，这比平均所有迭代有所改进。我们还提出并分析了一种包含正则化的 TD 变体。通过分析，我们得出结论，TD 的正则化版本对于病态特征问题很有用。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了结合尾部平均的时序差分（TD）学习算法在有限时间内的行为，并分析了正则化TD方法的效果。
AI.motivation: 传统TD学习的收敛分析常依赖于矩阵特征值信息，而本文旨在提供无需此类信息的有限时间分析，并探索正则化在处理病态特征问题中的有效性。
AI.method: 通过尾部平均技术对TD算法进行有限时间分析，并提出一种正则化TD方法，结合理论分析与数值实验。
AI.result: 尾部平均TD在期望和高概率下以最优O(1/t)速率收敛，初始误差衰减更快；正则化TD在病态特征问题中表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 正则化TD方法有效解决病态特征问题，尾部平均技术提升收敛效率，为实际应用提供理论支持。

motivation: 传统TD学习的收敛分析常依赖于矩阵特征值信息，而本文旨在提供无需此类信息的有限时间分析，并探索正则化在处理病态特征问题中的有效性。
method: 通过尾部平均技术对TD算法进行有限时间分析，并提出一种正则化TD方法，结合理论分析与数值实验。
result: 尾部平均TD在期望和高概率下以最优O(1/t)速率收敛，初始误差衰减更快；正则化TD在病态特征问题中表现更优。
conclusion: 正则化TD方法有效解决病态特征问题，尾部平均技术提升收敛效率，为实际应用提供理论支持。
search_text: Finite time analysis of temporal difference learning with linear function approximation: Tail averaging and regularisation 使用线性函数近似的时间差分学习的有限时间分析：尾部平均和正则化 We study the finite-time behaviour of the popular temporal difference (TD) learning algorithm when combined with tail-averaging. We derive finite time bounds on the parameter error of the tail-averaged TD iterate under a step-size choice that does not require information about the eigenvalues of the matrix underlying the projected TD fixed point. Our analysis shows that tail-averaged TD converges at the optimal $O\left(1/t\right)$ rate, both in expectation and with high probability. In addition, our bounds exhibit a sharper rate of decay for the initial error (bias), which is an improvement over averaging all iterates. We also propose and analyse a variant of TD that incorporates regularisation. From analysis, we conclude that the regularised version of TD is useful for problems with ill-conditioned features. 我们研究了流行的时间差分（TD）学习算法与尾部平均相结合时的有限时间行为。我们在步长选择下推导出尾部平均 TD 迭代的参数误差的有限时间界限，该步长选择不需要有关投影 TD 固定点的矩阵特征值的信息。我们的分析表明，尾部平均 TD 收敛于最优 $O\left(1/t\right)$ 速率，无论是预期还是高概率。此外，我们的边界表现出初始误差（偏差）的更急剧的衰减率，这比平均所有迭代有所改进。我们还提出并分析了一种包含正则化的 TD 变体。通过分析，我们得出结论，TD 的正则化版本对于病态特征问题很有用。 本文研究了结合尾部平均的时序差分（TD）学习算法在有限时间内的行为，并分析了正则化TD方法的效果。 Gandharv Patil,Prashanth L. A.,Dheeraj Nagaraj,Doina Precup cs.LG,cs.AI,eess.SY,stat.ML

authors_text: Junwoo Park,Youngwoo Cho,Gyuhyeon Sim,Hojoon Lee,Jaegul Choo
title: Enemy Spotted: in-game gun sound dataset for gunshot classification and localization
title_zh: 发现敌人：游戏中枪声数据集，用于枪声分类和定位
comment: Accepted at IEEE Conference on Games (GoG) 2022
comments: Accepted at IEEE Conference on Games (GoG) 2022
abstract: Recently, deep learning-based methods have drawn huge attention due to their simple yet high performance without domain knowledge in sound classification and localization tasks. However, a lack of gun sounds in existing datasets has been a major obstacle to implementing a support system to spot criminals from their gunshots by leveraging deep learning models. Since the occurrence of gunshot is rare and unpredictable, it is impractical to collect gun sounds in the real world. As an alternative, gun sounds can be obtained from an FPS game that is designed to mimic real-world warfare. The recent FPS game offers a realistic environment where we can safely collect gunshot data while simulating even dangerous situations. By exploiting the advantage of the game environment, we construct a gunshot dataset, namely BGG, for the firearm classification and gunshot localization tasks. The BGG dataset consists of 37 different types of firearms, distances, and directions between the sound source and a receiver. We carefully verify that the in-game gunshot data has sufficient information to identify the location and type of gunshots by training several sound classification and localization baselines on the BGG dataset. Afterward, we demonstrate that the accuracy of real-world firearm classification and localization tasks can be enhanced by utilizing the BGG dataset.

However, a lack of gun sounds in existing datasets has been a major obstacle to implementing a support system to spot criminals from their gunshots by leveraging deep learning models. Since the occurrence of gunshot is rare and unpredictable, it is impractical to collect gun sounds in the real world. As an alternative, gun sounds can be obtained from an FPS game that is designed to mimic real-world warfare. The recent FPS game offers a realistic environment where we can safely collect gunshot data while simulating even dangerous situations. By exploiting the advantage of the game environment, we construct a gunshot dataset, namely BGG, for the firearm classification and gunshot localization tasks. The BGG dataset consists of 37 different types of firearms, distances, and directions between the sound source and a receiver. We carefully verify that the in-game gunshot data has sufficient information to identify the location and type of gunshots by training several sound classification and localization baselines on the BGG dataset. Afterward, we demonstrate that the accuracy of real-world firearm classification and localization tasks can be enhanced by utilizing the BGG dataset.
abstract_zh: 最近，基于深度学习的方法因其在声音分类和定位任务中无需领域知识而简单而高性能而引起了广泛关注。然而，现有数据集中缺乏枪声一直是实现利用深度学习模型从枪声中发现罪犯的支持系统的主要障碍。由于枪声的发生很少且不可预测，因此在现实世界中收集枪声是不切实际的。作为替代方案，可以从旨在模拟现实世界战争的 FPS 游戏中获取枪声。最近的 FPS 游戏提供了一个真实的环境，我们可以安全地收集枪击数据，同时模拟甚至危险的情况。通过利用游戏环境的优势，我们构建了枪声数据集，即BGG，用于枪支分类和枪声定位任务。 BGG 数据集包含 37 种不同类型的枪支、声源和接收器之间的距离和方向。我们通过在 BGG 数据集上训练多个声音分类和定位基线，仔细验证游戏中的枪声数据是否具有足够的信息来识别枪声的位置和类型。随后，我们证明了利用 BGG 数据集可以提高现实世界枪支分类和定位任务的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文构建了一个基于FPS游戏的枪声数据集BGG，用于枪支分类和定位任务。
AI.motivation: 现有数据集缺乏枪声样本，难以通过深度学习实现基于枪声的犯罪分子识别系统，而真实场景中枪声采集难度大且危险。
AI.method: 利用FPS游戏模拟真实战场环境，采集包含37种枪支、不同距离和方向的枪声数据，构建BGG数据集并验证其分类与定位能力。
AI.result: 实验表明，基于BGG训练的模型可有效提升现实场景中的枪支分类和定位准确率。
AI.conclusion: 游戏生成的枪声数据集能有效支持枪声分类与定位任务，为实际应用提供可行方案。

authors_text: Gokul Karthik Kumar,Karthik Nandakumar
title: Hate-CLIPper: Multimodal Hateful Meme Classification based on Cross-modal Interaction of CLIP Features
title_zh: Hate-CLIPper：基于 CLIP 特征跨模态交互的多模态仇恨模因分类
comment: Accepted at EMNLP 2022 Workshop on NLP for Positive Impact
comments: Accepted at EMNLP 2022 Workshop on NLP for Positive Impact
abstract: Hateful memes are a growing menace on social media. While the image and its corresponding text in a meme are related, they do not necessarily convey the same meaning when viewed individually. Hence, detecting hateful memes requires careful consideration of both visual and textual information. Multimodal pre-training can be beneficial for this task because it effectively captures the relationship between the image and the text by representing them in a similar feature space. Furthermore, it is essential to model the interactions between the image and text features through intermediate fusion. Most existing methods either employ multimodal pre-training or intermediate fusion, but not both. In this work, we propose the Hate-CLIPper architecture, which explicitly models the cross-modal interactions between the image and text representations obtained using Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) encoders via a feature interaction matrix (FIM). A simple classifier based on the FIM representation is able to achieve state-of-the-art performance on the Hateful Memes Challenge (HMC) dataset with an AUROC of 85.8, which even surpasses the human performance of 82.65. Experiments on other meme datasets such as Propaganda Memes and TamilMemes also demonstrate the generalizability of the proposed approach. Finally, we analyze the interpretability of the FIM representation and show that cross-modal interactions can indeed facilitate the learning of meaningful concepts. The code for this work is available at https://github.com/gokulkarthik/hateclipper.
summary: Hateful memes are a growing menace on social media. While the image and its corresponding text in a meme are related, they do not necessarily convey the same meaning when viewed individually. Hence, detecting hateful memes requires careful consideration of both visual and textual information. Multimodal pre-training can be beneficial for this task because it effectively captures the relationship between the image and the text by representing them in a similar feature space. Furthermore, it is essential to model the interactions between the image and text features through intermediate fusion. Most existing methods either employ multimodal pre-training or intermediate fusion, but not both. In this work, we propose the Hate-CLIPper architecture, which explicitly models the cross-modal interactions between the image and text representations obtained using Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) encoders via a feature interaction matrix (FIM). A simple classifier based on the FIM representation is able to achieve state-of-the-art performance on the Hateful Memes Challenge (HMC) dataset with an AUROC of 85.8, which even surpasses the human performance of 82.65. Experiments on other meme datasets such as Propaganda Memes and TamilMemes also demonstrate the generalizability of the proposed approach. Finally, we analyze the interpretability of the FIM representation and show that cross-modal interactions can indeed facilitate the learning of meaningful concepts. The code for this work is available at https://github.com/gokulkarthik/hateclipper.
abstract_zh: 仇恨模因是社交媒体上日益严重的威胁。虽然模因中的图像及其相应的文本是相关的，但单独查看时它们不一定传达相同的含义。因此，检测仇恨模因需要仔细考虑视觉和文本信息。多模态预训练对于这项任务是有益的，因为它通过在相似的特征空间中表示图像和文本来有效地捕获图像和文本之间的关系。此外，通过中间融合对图像和文本特征之间的交互进行建模至关重要。大多数现有方法要么采用多模态预训练，要么采用中间融合，但不能同时采用两者。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Hate-CLIPper 架构，它通过特征交互矩阵 (FIM) 对使用对比语言图像预训练 (CLIP) 编码器获得的图像和文本表示之间的跨模式交互进行显式建模。基于 FIM 表示的简单分类器能够在 Hateful Memes Challenge (HMC) 数据集上实现最先进的性能，AUROC 为 85.8，甚至超过了人类的 82.65 表现。对其他模因数据集（例如 Propaganda Memes 和 TamilMemes）的实验也证明了所提出方法的普遍性。最后，我们分析了 FIM 表示的可解释性，并表明跨模态交互确实可以促进有意义概念的学习。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/gokulkarthik/hateclipper 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Hate-CLIPper架构，通过跨模态交互分类仇恨类梗图，结合图像与文本特征进行多模态分析。
AI.motivation: 现有方法多单独使用多模态预训练或中间融合，未能同时结合两者优势，导致仇恨类梗图检测效果受限。
AI.method: 基于CLIP编码器提取图像和文本特征，通过特征交互矩阵（FIM）建模跨模态关系，并使用FIM表示训练简单分类器。
AI.result: 在HMC数据集上实现85.8的AUROC，超越人类表现82.65；在Propaganda Memes和TamilMemes数据集上验证方法普适性。
AI.conclusion: 跨模态交互能有效学习语义概念，FIM表征具有可解释性，Hate-CLIPper在多模态仇恨内容检测中达到SOTA性能。

Finally, we analyze the interpretability of the FIM representation and show that cross-modal interactions can indeed facilitate the learning of meaningful concepts. The code for this work is available at https://github.com/gokulkarthik/hateclipper. 仇恨模因是社交媒体上日益严重的威胁。虽然模因中的图像及其相应的文本是相关的，但单独查看时它们不一定传达相同的含义。因此，检测仇恨模因需要仔细考虑视觉和文本信息。多模态预训练对于这项任务是有益的，因为它通过在相似的特征空间中表示图像和文本来有效地捕获图像和文本之间的关系。此外，通过中间融合对图像和文本特征之间的交互进行建模至关重要。大多数现有方法要么采用多模态预训练，要么采用中间融合，但不能同时采用两者。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Hate-CLIPper 架构，它通过特征交互矩阵 (FIM) 对使用对比语言图像预训练 (CLIP) 编码器获得的图像和文本表示之间的跨模式交互进行显式建模。基于 FIM 表示的简单分类器能够在 Hateful Memes Challenge (HMC) 数据集上实现最先进的性能，AUROC 为 85.8，甚至超过了人类的 82.65 表现。对其他模因数据集（例如 Propaganda Memes 和 TamilMemes）的实验也证明了所提出方法的普遍性。最后，我们分析了 FIM 表示的可解释性，并表明跨模态交互确实可以促进有意义概念的学习。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/gokulkarthik/hateclipper 获取。 本文提出Hate-CLIPper架构，通过跨模态交互分类仇恨类梗图，结合图像与文本特征进行多模态分析。 Gokul Karthik Kumar,Karthik Nandakumar cs.CL,cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.MM

authors_text: Runmin Cong,Weiyu Song,Jianjun Lei,Guanghui Yue,Yao Zhao,Sam Kwong
title: PSNet: Parallel Symmetric Network for Video Salient Object Detection
title_zh: PSNet：用于视频显着目标检测的并行对称网络
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence 2022, 13 pages, 8 figures
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence 2022, 13 pages, 8 figures
abstract: For the video salient object detection (VSOD) task, how to excavate the information from the appearance modality and the motion modality has always been a topic of great concern. The two-stream structure, including an RGB appearance stream and an optical flow motion stream, has been widely used as a typical pipeline for VSOD tasks, but the existing methods usually only use motion features to unidirectionally guide appearance features or adaptively but blindly fuse two modality features. However, these methods underperform in diverse scenarios due to the uncomprehensive and unspecific learning schemes. In this paper, following a more secure modeling philosophy, we deeply investigate the importance of appearance modality and motion modality in a more comprehensive way and propose a VSOD network with up and down parallel symmetry, named PSNet. Two parallel branches with different dominant modalities are set to achieve complete video saliency decoding with the cooperation of the Gather Diffusion Reinforcement (GDR) module and Cross-modality Refinement and Complement (CRC) module. Finally, we use the Importance Perception Fusion (IPF) module to fuse the features from two parallel branches according to their different importance in different scenarios. Experiments on four dataset benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves desirable and competitive performance.
summary: For the video salient object detection (VSOD) task, how to excavate the information from the appearance modality and the motion modality has always been a topic of great concern. The two-stream structure, including an RGB appearance stream and an optical flow motion stream, has been widely used as a typical pipeline for VSOD tasks, but the existing methods usually only use motion features to unidirectionally guide appearance features or adaptively but blindly fuse two modality features. However, these methods underperform in diverse scenarios due to the uncomprehensive and unspecific learning schemes. In this paper, following a more secure modeling philosophy, we deeply investigate the importance of appearance modality and motion modality in a more comprehensive way and propose a VSOD network with up and down parallel symmetry, named PSNet. Two parallel branches with different dominant modalities are set to achieve complete video saliency decoding with the cooperation of the Gather Diffusion Reinforcement (GDR) module and Cross-modality Refinement and Complement (CRC) module. Finally, we use the Importance Perception Fusion (IPF) module to fuse the features from two parallel branches according to their different importance in different scenarios. Experiments on four dataset benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves desirable and competitive performance.
abstract_zh: 对于视频显着目标检测（VSOD）任务，如何从外观模态和运动模态中挖掘信息一直是人们高度关注的话题。双流结构，包括RGB外观流和光流运动流，已被广泛用作VSOD任务的典型管道，但现有方法通常仅使用运动特征来单向引导外观特征或自适应但盲目地融合两种模态特征。然而，由于学习方案不全面且不具体，这些方法在各种场景中表现不佳。在本文中，遵循更安全的建模理念，我们以更全面的方式深入研究了外观模态和运动模态的重要性，并提出了一种上下平行对称的VSOD网络，命名为PSNet。设置具有不同主导模态的两个并行分支，通过聚集扩散增强（GDR）模块和跨模态细化和补码（CRC）模块的配合实现完整的视频显着性解码。最后，我们使用重要性感知融合（IPF）模块根据两个并行分支的特征在不同场景下的不同重要性来融合它们。对四个数据集基准的实验表明，我们的方法实现了理想且有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PSNet，一种用于视频显著性目标检测的并行对称网络，旨在更全面地整合外观和运动信息。
AI.motivation: 现有视频显著性检测方法在融合外观和运动模态时存在不全面或盲目融合的问题，导致多场景下性能不足。
AI.method: 设计包含上下对称并行分支的网络结构，结合GDR模块、CRC模块及IPF模块，根据场景重要性动态融合双模态特征。
AI.result: 在四个数据集基准测试中，该方法展现出具有竞争力的检测性能。
AI.conclusion: PSNet通过双模态协同与动态融合机制，有效提升了视频显著性目标检测的适应性与准确性。
